### yarralea — 2013-10-09T09:39:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-97327>

Hiya,

I have just discovered this forum today, and rather than poke around, stalk the threads, I figured I would come and just show myself.
I'm 39, female, from Perth Western Australia. Which has a great climate- if you like the beach. Cold nights, as the capital is surrounded by water or desert, but generally the weather is lovely. Being an Australian, I think of temperature in terms of celsius- I'm sure I'll need to get a grip on the temperature conversion eventually.

In terms of weight and health- I'm 39 and not suffering in any way, very lucky. My parents are both sick however, and as long as I can remember my mother has been heavy- she's probably about 120 kgs ( mmm pounds.... er, about 270) and my father is diabetic, with alzheimer's- probably from statins as he had a heart attack 40 years ago and has been on a host of meds. My older sister 44, is currently on hormone inhibitors post chemo and BC- she's arthritic and unhappy because of her treatment and current hormone state. So I see my genetic future, and want to change it. I had been doing a low carb diet since 2002 to control my weight- but it morphed into a low fat, super low calorie, high diet drink disaster. I was able to pull myself back, but would swing from out of control eating to ultra controlled olive counting. I seemed to have found some solace in paleo, being able to loosen up and have the odd bit of non ideal food and not spiral into a month long binge fest. I have been paleo for the good part of 8 months, dropping 20 kilos. I have a lot of butter in my diet, and for a period I was having some raw goats milk and raw cows milk. My weight is stable at 70 kilos now, but I would like to be a few kilos leaner. I don't exercise much, a few hours of roller derby each week and a few hours of softball 3 times a week.

I have been looking into CT, albeit a bit slowly. Here's how I've been going so far. I started by having some cold shower blasts and going for 20 minute dips in the ocean, current water temperature is 18 C. That has been easy. I have also started having cold baths. Cold water at home, in a standard tub with just a couple of kilos of ice added. I'm not wearing any compression garments, just au naturale. I'm not sure of the water temperature as I've not measured it. I have frozen 3 bags of water in ziplock bags as I refuse to buy ice for this. When I get more freezer space I will freeze more. I can stay in for about 30 minutes- depending upon what else is going on in the household. I usually do this at early night. My sleep has been the best thing since becoming more committed to turning off the iphone and teev. I know for sure that this stimulates me and keeps me from sleep. The part that I do find difficult is on roller derby / softball training nights when I finish sometimes at 9 pm, I have a hard time winding down and sleeping. I have started just leaving training earlier, choosing rest over exercise.

Eating..... er, I usually eat 5-6 egg yolks only in the morning, with some kimchi and at times an avocado. It's not high protein, its high fat, and I usually eat within half an hour of rising, and that usually keeps me going for most of the day, at work I'll have a can of sardines in oil and a few days a week I'll just snack on butter until dinner. Dinner is usually grassfed beef with vegetables- too often sweet potato roasted in beef tallow. We were having rice once a week, boiled in bone broth to save a bit of cash from the household budget, and the other people in the house are not as strict as I would like myself to be.

My daughter is where my story gets interesting. Diagnosed with global developmental delay at aged 3 we went from doctor to doctor looking for causes and solutions. Not one said try a gluten free, casein free diet. I was part of a low carber forum and someone suggested I take her off gluten. I knew instantly I was doing the right thing, because her withdrawal was horrendous. I joke now that I would have given her away to a bad home; she was about 4 at the time. I saw improvements, but they were not long lasting. As I type this she has just had a meltdown- she is now 7. She has been paleo since march, and we are seeing some improvements, eg the bed wetting instantly ceased, but still the odd epic meltdown when she does not get her own way. Its always at home- we have large power lines 500m from our house, I wonder if they are affecting her. She sleeps on a bunk bed, as her room is small to create space, I wonder if that affects her. We have wifi, I wonder if that affects her, and if she ever uses an iPhone/ iPad and at some level teev and computer, she is horrible. We are with a nutritional review service- treating her with amino acids, nutritional supplements and aloe vera juice to restore her gut, she has high levels of mercury and low levels of zinc. I am looking into doing some neuro feedback with her. I will struggle to convince the DH that the wifi needs to go and the other changes to improve our water and EMF cannot be afforded at this time due DH being out of work.

I have been trying to read as much of JKs articles, but I have garnered most of it from podcasts. I love podcasts, I can listen to them for hours and its my preferred way of getting information. I found links to JK on locarber.org and when I started podcasting I spotted him with Sean Croxton, Ben Greenfield and others- I certainly prefer listening over the blogs which exhaust me. I will continue to read them, I'm sure I will eventually cease to be overwhelmed by them. I do like the geek alert / non scientist sections and the way that is set out.

So I am hoping to get connected with people who understand where I am coming from- cartainly any other aussies, or hopefully another Perth person with whom to talk supplies with.

See you in the soup (primordial soup lol)

### yarralea — 2013-10-12T14:42:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-97608>

Well today was an awesome day for softball. I pitched in my debut game, and the team won, and I managed to K a few batters, and beat out a few ground balls. I was then asked to fill in for the seconds, which was fun, although I had to play outfield. Being a pitcher and a catcher means I'm accustomed to being always of the move- and I have to say I can't believe how tiring it is doing less. The day ended up cool and I was shivering at the end of it, as I had changed from the uniform and back into a dress, and then I drove home with the roof off the car and chilled a bit further.
I was having an interesting chat with one of the ladies from the 2s- her skin was all blotchy and I asked what caused it. We eventually chatted about her health. She was telling me that she has virtually no pituitary function, and takes a medication that replaces her pituitary secretions?? It got me wondering, given that hormones are a huge part of this program whether CT and an optimal lifestyle as prescribed by JK would serve her in restoring her pituitary. I recall that when she had a brain scan they assumed they would find a tumor, and then were surprised to not. If she doesn't take the meds she sleeps a lot (like a heap!) and can't manage her emotions (she said depression and anger towards her kids) If there is anyone out there who has a pituitary condition that they are treating please let me know, so I can say check out jackkruse.com it might change everything.

I bought two magnetic mattresses for my husband and my bed, as well as one for my daughter. I hope they arrive soon. In other news it looks as though my husband has a job offer on the table, so the bankrupcy plan of moving to the middle of nowhere is probably out. LOL!. I still like the idea of setting up a natural setting / alternative to lock up for young people...... and I know that eventually this is what I will have to do. I think I should just focus on my daughters health and development and save the world next.

### yarralea — 2013-10-16T16:01:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-97994>

Hiya- the wifi- which is the highest priority, is on the other side of the wall, in the hallway. EEK I know. We have just moved here and are still customising. And its really now starting to occur to me that this is a huge factor in her slowed progress- I can only really afford to do one thing. Switching the wifi to cabled is first and foremost.
As for the circuit breakers, I'm really not technically minded, so I guess that means we work out which ones can be turned off. I've turned a heap of switches (microwave- which I'm working on the husband to be rid of it- thinking of making it blow up and then be not replaced.) This will be easier when my husband is back at work, to lose the Mwave. It is currently used to heat up my daughters lunches. As a gluten free, grain free child who attends school, so many options are hot lunches- I'm hoping she will warm to the tuna in oil idea soon. My perfect world would include her devouring sardines lol.

Me- my FBB (fat based breakfast) is usually 6-8 egg yolks cooked in grass fed beef tallow, served with a big spoon of my homemade kimchi and a whole avocado chopped, maybe a slice of organic butter after, kind of as I am walking out the door. sometimes theres some organic bacon too. My nitrite free organic bacon is so good, I love my guy who makes it. He and his wife are the grandparent types that my daughtyer never has access to, as my parents live in Melbourne. Lunch- sometimes not- sometimes a small amount of macadamias, and I know this sounds bad, and it freaks out my co workers, but I'll have a slice of butter on a square of lindt 85%. They often see me eat butter. Often I'll also have a cup of plain bone broth, or a short black with a big spoon of CO. I love my king oscar sardines in olive oil, and occasionally I will make an ommelette at work, as I have a full kitchen at my disposal. Dinner is always grass fed beef with vegies- sometimes steak, sometimes slow cooker- as we have just purchase half a cow (Annabelle we have named her- because we all know that animals with names that have lived a good life are better for you) Usully I am into the sweet potato roasted and sprinkled with screaming seeds seasoning. If your an aussie- look up screaming seeds- so delish! But the penchant for sweet potatoe has to stop. Broccoli, cauli, asparagus are usually there and my husband and daughter eat potato- roasted in beef tallow. love my beef tallow! I also have some kimchi as well, I love it. I make about 20 jars a fortnight, giving at least half away or swapping them. Dinner is usally about 6, breakfast about 6am.

I'm big on brewing water kefir and kombucha. I brew about 12 litres a week- I do give some away, and I'm going to stop drinking as much as it does use suagr in the fermenting process and I'm concerned I'm getting some of those sweet carbs. Its a toss up- cos as a probiotic I'm a fan, and really I have 5 kilos to lose and no health issues. I eat kimchi everyday and now we are on lovely water that is not fluorodated I may need less. I should just sell it, heaps of my friends want it, because I do enjoy making it. If anyone drinks this on the program, or has an opinion, I would like to hear from them.

Grounding, I need to know more of this- if I'm walking around barefoot, and my house is ceramic tiles on a concrete slab- is that grounded?
I've been walking more barefoot of late. Husband has a pet hate of barefoot children, so I need to work on him. Where we live- Fremantle is very hippy / barefoot / organic/ lets be naturale/ commune baby! so he is getting more exposed to barefoot children and starting to warm to it. Slowly he likes his change. He is down to one slice of bread a day, and is given up the cheap crap food that is microwaveable- but that has been a slow progress. When he goes back to work no doubt his lunches will be pretty gross. I would love him to live longer and healthier with me, drink his coffee black with CO but he is 16 years older than me and set in his ways. That I have inspired him to change in any way at all is a miracle. He hasnt time to do all the research that I have, he has been looking for work and has ben committed to that for most of his time. Ick, when he goes back I will be doing more housework. Can't quite afford for a cleaning fairy- after him being out of work for 4 months we have some catch up to do- not to mention a few EMF priorities.

Magnetico- we will start with one of those el cheapo versions and after I have swapped my daughter bunk to a floor based futon, we will invest in a single magnetic. I want one for our bed too- but looking at 5k... I would probably have to tell my husband I won it lol!

Right, it is getting late, and although I love my f.lux (cheers JK) gotta dash. Couldnt get the f.lux on the iphone- it seems to require jailbreaking of the phone, and that sounds a bit illegal / scarey/ too much for me. I turn the brightness down as low as Iphone can go- but need to just forget I have an iphone. I actually left it at home today- kind of like a challenge. It was weird. ### yarralea — 2013-10-17T17:56:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-98160>

Kimchi- I just made 10 jars. I usually make it every four weeks. 2 purple cabbages, chopped medium small, soaked in salted water for 6+ hours. Then strain off the water. I then add 4-6 carrots, grated, a half cup to a cup of pureed ginger, the zest of 3 lemons, a few chopped chilies a whole bulb of garlic, crushed, a huge bunch of radishes, as much coriander as you can or like (I LOVE CORIANDER!) spring onions are also good. Then add another 1/4 cup salt- I just freepour so I guess its about this amount, and a dollop of honey to feed the ferment. Push hard into clean jars and tip out any rising liquid (salt draws it out) I leave on the sink with lids on, and each day I press down a bit more and tip out extra liquid. Then stick the jars in the cupboard for at least a week- although I have had jars for up to 8 weeks. Heaps of recipes online- you really dont need the starter yeasts etc IMO.

Kombucha- you need a scoby to start (Symbiotic Culture of Bacterial Yeast) which is this jelly gooplike disk that sits atop the kombucha brew. A two litre glass jar, make black tea (4 tea bags is what I use) and 1/2 cup of sugar. Make 1600ml, cool then add 200 mls of previous batch and the scoby. Cover with a cloth and secure with a band and keep in a place out of direct sunlight. 7-10 days later, strain and bottle. Keep the bottles in a cupboard for 3 days and it will carbonate. Then go again! Kombucha does not like metal objects and despises fluoridated water. I make at least 8 litres a week, but give a lot away. If you dont know anyone with a scoby you can grow your own my mixing the tea and sugar liquid with a commercial PLAIN kombucha and leaving in a dark place for a few weeks- it will form a scoby, the first batch will be a bit vinegary, but the results are good and I very much enjoy it.

Kimchi, every day, usually twice a day, kombucha or water kefir every day too. Now I'm not exposed to fluoridated water I would expect my gut bacteria to be ...world class! Lol.

Caroline, re my daughter food being microwaved, I know its not ideal, but my husband is doing it- hey tomorrows the last day! and the microwave is turned off when not in use. A few purchases on the weekend- a kettle and a cast iron pan. I've been avoiding buying one, but now DH has a job starting monday, I can relax the purse.

### yarralea — 2013-10-31T05:00:23+0200

### yarralea — 2013-11-12T14:11:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-100497>

My daughter and I are in Melbourne for a few days. A massive 20 degrees c difference in the temperature. I'm staying near the beach so tomorrow I'm hoping for a swim. Everyone thinks I'm crazy. Lol.

Neuroptimal is the name of the system. My daughter myself and husband have all had sessions : 4,3 and 1 session respectively. Michii has recently started to tie her shoe laces and demonstrated some cognitive improvements and I can't believe the increased clarity of my vision and thinking. Paul who suffers from head aches had a two week long head ache cease. So it made fiscal sense to buy a machine rather than the treatments. I will let you know how it works. Many neuroptimal clients are seeking relief from PTSD, add OCD etc. I have seen early improvements so we have committed to it. How it works is electrodes are applied to the head, along with an earthing point, the patient listens to music and the device reads the brain waves and slightly interferes with the music signal which lends the brain to create more alpha waves to make the correction or adjustment in response to the slight interruption. There are different stages of the 32 minute sessions. There are other neurofeedback systems as well however many require a practitioner determining what the brain should look like and pushing and pulling the brain with more aggressive neurofeedback. I have spoken in person with a few people that had positive experiences with neurofeedback, and the neuroptimal system and we have decided to fully explore this. We will continue to do the nutritional review and of course the diet will remain paleo. That of course is a brief description that is very basic. I'm on my phone at the moment, I will add some links when I'm not on an iPhone.

### yarralea — 2013-11-17T08:10:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-100880>

had another 50 minute ice bath last night. I have a cleaned out wheelie bin with a little stool inside for getting out with some level of grace. I listen to podcasts in it- it helps pass the time. I'm sure my neighbours would think me crazy.

What is strange is that a few kilos have crept on. I was 69kg, and now I am 74. I wonder if its a summer thing. The only thing I think has changed in my diet is the fat content has dropped a bit, and so have the calories, as I am not getting into the fruit. Market day is usually an icypole (cucumber lime and chilli- these girls have an icypole standand I like to support them in their venture). I am also going to slow my keffir and kombucha konsumption (lol) because maybe theres a bit too much sweet getting in, even though they are very fermented. I wonder if the el cheapo magnetic underlay is having an ill effect, and the week that went on the bed was week the scales started going the other way. I have also just had a week away, out of my own control zone of eating exactly how I like and need, although mostly good choices were made. I might have a month where absolutely no lindt 85% is taken- even though it has never slowed my progress before. I will tweak a few things and see if the scales can go back to the under 70 mark. I know its just a number, but as I wear dresses all the time, clothes are not a good measure tool. I guess I haven't been to roller derby for a while- since my husband has gone back to work it has not been practicable with my daughter- it also finishes so late I tend to not b e able to sleep for a while.

Back to CT. Might be a bit TMI but I had to get out half way to go to the bathroom- I wonder if CT has a habit of ...er... getting things in motion? Also I do recall Jack saying on a podcast that one tends to sleep better after a CT session- this is not the case with me, maybe I am missing a detail or two. I also recall him saying the details matter, so maybe I'm missing a detail or two. Does it matter if my head doesnt submerge, (mammalian dive reflect / vagus nerve) when I am CTing? I do this when I swim in the morning, but its kind of hard to get the face in when using the wheelie bin (aka the bogan jaccuzzi lol) method. Also returning to a normal temperature- that needs to be done in a certain way- I just get out and dress lightly. I usually go to bed with the hour and its just a sheet, maybe with my feet with an additional layer. Also does it effect menstrual cycle, or should one not CT whilst menstruating? If anyone is reading this, I would love to know if the book The Leptin Reset has a lot of detail on CT.

Are there some cheap ways to make blocks of ice that people are doing? Because while 15 kgs of ice is $15 here, its not something I want to continue to do twice a week. I have some freezer space, but really dont have any containers as we dont buy anything with a container. I think one litre milk carton that are square might be a good idea. Tessalating, standing without leakage then laying down when fully frozen in various stackable ways.

### yarralea — 2013-12-10T05:01:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-103025>

Okay- weight has crept up to 75, with the summer I believe. I'm trying to work out what sort of changes I need to make to my diet in order to alter for the season.
My daily food is such as: 5-7 egg yolks cooked in beef fat, sometimes with 3 rashers of bacon, kimchi and a whole avocado. Coffee with CO every other day and sometimes a tin of sardines and some organic butter. Lunch has consistently been spinach, avocado (whole) some chili mustard and coriander and some tuna in EVOO. Dinner is asparagas, brocoli, cauliflower, kimchi and GF beef cooked in beef fat. There's been a bit of dark chocolate here and there. There has been no dairy, no wheat or grains getting in.

Any tips of how people transistion to summer- I'm in OZ, so the weather is warm already. I've been doing a weekly ice bath and a weekly dip in the 18 degree ocean. I have also started a bit of neuroptimal neuro feedback.

### yarralea — 2013-12-11T06:24:05+0200

Well, I searched the net and I haven't actually found that much feedback from users but I read this and found it interesting, how does it compare with your experience so far? [http://totallyaddconnect.com/forums/topic/experience-with-neurofeedback-neuroptimal/](http://totallyaddconnect.com/forums/topic/experience-with-neurofeedback-neuroptimal/)

This is not a very scientific response, but I was asked whether I was taking medication before having some sessions- I think some meds that end in pam inhibit the brain's response to the program. I'm sure all users experience this treatment different. My experience with myself has been positive so far. My daughter initially showed a steep curve of improvement, followed by a decline in her compliance, which seems to have turned towards the positive- we will see. All I csan say, is I say enough promise in the technology after having two sessions, my daughter having three and my husband had a session and changed his mind from being very reluctant to drop $11k on a system, to saying yes. I speak to people in person from the NFB community in perth and feel positivity. I think its imperative for people to do their homework.

Having said that I think people who do not address other aspects of their lifestyle are not going to see dramatic improvements. The poster of the above blog did not give any info as to what else she was doing to alter her situation.

I have kept the carbs quite low- probably a few in the water keffir that I am making, and a mango here and there, but generally I have been eating well and feeling well.

The other is my page: Naturally Optimized Wellness. So business is commencing. ### yarralea — 2014-03-31T15:59:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-119048>

I missed out on the ice machine. But the good news is, there might be another one available.
One thing that has come with the slightly cooler weather- as in below 30 degrees centigrade.... is that tap water doesn't melt the ice so rapidly.

This is my ice bath recipe. If I am doing it wrong, someone give me some tips.
I start with my wheelie bin. (aussies will know- a total bogan spa) Its a recycling wheelie bin, cleaned out. And since we don't eat anything with a recyclable package, it does not get utilised for anything other than ice baths.
I run the hose for a minute to get the warmest water out, then half fill the wheelie bin. I have about 15 kgs of ice in bigger containers like ice cream buckets etc, and they go in, and they chill the water so the ice lasts a bit longer. when the two smallest blocks that are 500g each are melted, I get in. I usually wear socks, and in bathers. Q: Is the compression shirt advantageous? Because I wore it the other day and never have before..... was I supposed to start with that...oops.

Then I get 4 5kg bags of tube ice, so 20 kg and in that goes. Then the wheelie bin is almost at the brim and I'm in with my hands and fingers out. The water gets down to about 6 degrees. I'm in a comfortable squatting position, and I move my legs about to distribute water temperature- i notice behind my knees feels warm, so I move evry minute or so. The ice melts after about 30 minutes and I can do 60 minutes. I think I might do longer but my phone always goes flat. I listen to podcasts whilst CTing. It stops the boredom and is a great distraction. If there is anyone in perth, western australia who wants to talk crap with me in a wheelie bin, bring your vessel and we will CT (near fremantle)
The difficult part of CT for me is scheduling it. I don't like to do it before bed as I tend not to sleep well. Being a full time teacher and mother means days are out, and I might start to do mornings. I was also having sessions before softball games- about an hour before scheduled warm up. My team mates thought I would get injured, pull drivers etc, but my only injury was following a dodgy, ill-timed slide into third. I did notice that running felt faster and automatic- you know, when something happens in sport and there is no memory of it, not consciousness to it. Just a "wow, I got to first so fast" sort of thing....

Would adding epsom salts have any advantageous effect I wonder? Like boosting magnesium or keeping the water colder? I'm trying to think where I heard / read / found that idea.

Okay 9pm here. Lights out, screen off, have some sex.....

### yarralea — 2014-06-02T07:59:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-129328>

Okay I have just had a little bit of a spending spree. Earthing sheet for our bed- hopefully my daughters feet hit the end of it when she gets in.

I'm like the worlds UGLIEST sleeper. Twisted head, scrunched hands under forehead. Just plain ugly. It is causing me all manner of injuries, like numb arms upon waking, compacted chest muscles from laying one my side like a squished up mess. I'm hoping that I can work on that, not sure if any one has any comment about anything helpful. I was thinking of getting hypnotised to sleep on my back- I think I snore and am subconsciously self conscious about it. Its odd, I am quite comfortable on my back, but the instant I drop into sleep, I roll into UGLY.
Earthing mat for the computer, and a Vielight 330. I have tried the Vie light, I will search the threads to see what others have stated about this. I had a 25 minute session in my car, roof off 20 degrees C, I'm sure other drivers thought me a little cray cray.
In other news my daughter is changing schools, she will be going to an alternative setting, where shoes are optional and glass bottles encouraged. Yay.

One more question, I have this copper coily thingy, and it is used to change the structure of water- it was given to me, but I have NO idea whatsoever how or where to install it. So if anyone knows what the H I am talking about, and can give me a heads up, that would be great. The shower filter also arrived, much to do on this long weekend, which is almost over.

Also had an hour and a half in the ice bath yesterday, sitting in the glorious warm winter sun. The getting out of the bed in the morning is the hardest part. Must remember that the ice bath is easy, the hard part is getting out of bed.

### caroline — 2014-06-02T08:29:19+0300

### sooperb — 2014-06-02T11:28:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-129340>

We have our earthing sheet more or less across the middle of the bed, so your daughter could easily touch down on part of it. You've really got into the swing lol, well done. I still chuckle at the idea of using a recycling bin, how on earth do you get in and out? Come to that how can you squat for up to an hour without your legs complaining?

I thought you were going to say your copper coil was for something different entirely lol. Maybe you just put it in the water and nature does the rest?

### yarralea — 2014-06-10T03:32:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-130313>

Its like a wall squat- as the back and the knees are pretty much against the sides of the wheelie bin. So it can be easily done. I have a small step ladder, and getting in and out is easily done- out is a bit harder, it requires lifting oneself up by straightening the arms on the edge and flipping out the legs. Im relatively fit so I can manage it.

### yarralea — 2014-06-16T12:03:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-131006>

Managed a 25 minute ice bath this morning. Getting in was fine, staying in was fine- apart from the fact I had to chip out to work. It was the leaving the bed that was tough. I have to admit I was concerned that going from a nice warm bed to a chilly bin (NZeders will get that) might be a bit tough- but it was just the leaving of the bed that was the tough part. I had two cats curled up with me. It appears they love the earthing sheet too. It was from earthingoz.com.au and I also picked up the desk mat and the Vielight- which I love love love. Great for my sinuses which don't bother me, but I have noticed they are clearer. I was wondering, does anyone use it more than once per day? Any comments on frequency?.. if you can comment on your use if you have stopped by, that would be OSSOMM!

how are you doing????

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2014-06-18T00:04:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-131186>

H

> [yarralea said:](/goto/post?id=97327)
>
> Hiya,
>
> I have just discovered this forum today, and rather than poke around, stalk the threads, I figured I would come and just show myself.
> I'm 39, female, from Perth Western Australia. Which has a great climate- if you like the beach. Cold nights, as the capital is surrounded by water or desert, but generally the weather is lovely. Being an Australian, I think of temperature in terms of celsius- I'm sure I'll need to get a grip on the temperature conversion eventually.
>
> In terms of weight and health- I'm 39 and not suffering in any way, very lucky. My parents are both sick however, and as long as I can remember my mother has been heavy- she's probably about 120 kgs ( mmm pounds.... er, about 270) and my father is diabetic, with alzheimer's- probably from statins as he had a heart attack 40 years ago and has been on a host of meds. My older sister 44, is currently on hormone inhibitors post chemo and BC- she's arthritic and unhappy because of her treatment and current hormone state. So I see my genetic future, and want to change it. I had been doing a low carb diet since 2002 to control my weight- but it morphed into a low fat, super low calorie, high diet drink disaster. I was able to pull myself back, but would swing from out of control eating to ultra controlled olive counting. I seemed to have found some solace in paleo, being able to loosen up and have the odd bit of non ideal food and not spiral into a month long binge fest. I have been paleo for the good part of 8 months, dropping 20 kilos. I have a lot of butter in my diet, and for a period I was having some raw goats milk and raw cows milk. My weight is stable at 70 kilos now, but I would like to be a few kilos leaner. I don't exercise much, a few hours of roller derby each week and a few hours of softball 3 times a week.
>
> I have been looking into CT, albeit a bit slowly. Here's how I've been going so far. I started by having some cold shower blasts and going for 20 minute dips in the ocean, current water temperature is 18 C. That has been easy. I have also started having cold baths. Cold water at home, in a standard tub with just a couple of kilos of ice added. I'm not wearing any compression garments, just au naturale. I'm not sure of the water temperature as I've not measured it. I have frozen 3 bags of water in ziplock bags as I refuse to buy ice for this. When I get more freezer space I will freeze more. I can stay in for about 30 minutes- depending upon what else is going on in the household. I usually do this at early night. My sleep has been the best thing since becoming more committed to turning off the iphone and teev. I know for sure that this stimulates me and keeps me from sleep. The part that I do find difficult is on roller derby / softball training nights when I finish sometimes at 9 pm, I have a hard time winding down and sleeping. I have started just leaving training earlier, choosing rest over exercise.
>
> Eating..... er, I usually eat 5-6 egg yolks only in the morning, with some kimchi and at times an avocado. It's not high protein, its high fat, and I usually eat within half an hour of rising, and that usually keeps me going for most of the day, at work I'll have a can of sardines in oil and a few days a week I'll just snack on butter until dinner. Dinner is usually grassfed beef with vegetables- too often sweet potato roasted in beef tallow. We were having rice once a week, boiled in bone broth to save a bit of cash from the household budget, and the other people in the house are not as strict as I would like myself to be.
>
> My daughter is where my story gets interesting. Diagnosed with global developmental delay at aged 3 we went from doctor to doctor looking for causes and solutions. Not one said try a gluten free, casein free diet. I was part of a low carber forum and someone suggested I take her off gluten. I knew instantly I was doing the right thing, because her withdrawal was horrendous. I joke now that I would have given her away to a bad home; she was about 4 at the time. I saw improvements, but they were not long lasting. As I type this she has just had a meltdown- she is now 7. She has been paleo since march, and we are seeing some improvements, eg the bed wetting instantly ceased, but still the odd epic meltdown when she does not get her own way. Its always at home- we have large power lines 500m from our house, I wonder if they are affecting her. She sleeps on a bunk bed, as her room is small to create space, I wonder if that affects her. We have wifi, I wonder if that affects her, and if she ever uses an iPhone/ iPad and at some level teev and computer, she is horrible. We are with a nutritional review service- treating her with amino acids, nutritional supplements and aloe vera juice to restore her gut, she has high levels of mercury and low levels of zinc. I am looking into doing some neuro feedback with her. I will struggle to convince the DH that the wifi needs to go and the other changes to improve our water and EMF cannot be afforded at this time due DH being out of work.
>
> I have been trying to read as much of JKs articles, but I have garnered most of it from podcasts. I love podcasts, I can listen to them for hours and its my preferred way of getting information. I found links to JK on locarber.org and when I started podcasting I spotted him with Sean Croxton, Ben Greenfield and others- I certainly prefer listening over the blogs which exhaust me. I will continue to read them, I'm sure I will eventually cease to be overwhelmed by them. I do like the geek alert / non scientist sections and the way that is set out.
>
> So I am hoping to get connected with people who understand where I am coming from- cartainly any other aussies, or hopefully another Perth person with whom to talk supplies with.
>
> See you in the soup (primordial soup lol)

eyy

### yarralea — 2014-08-14T09:56:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-137810>

MRI done-serious tear to meniscus. I had an ACL reco 8 years ago, and I'm here to tell you that the meniscus being damaged beats the pants off the ACL in terms of pain. I was nearly ill this morning hobbling on crutches from bedroom to couch, but a BPC fixed that, good and proper. I have also been having BPCacao- MCT, CO GF butter and raw cacao.... I wonder if being ketogenic in these days before and after surgery will assist in some way- I'll possibly lose some muscle mass from being inactive- I'm playing banagrams by myself (freeform scrabble) mmm some of these words need explanation..... agequit (rage quit is what others do when they play me, and a colleague told me I was too old to play agile sports) Chair- well the injury started by falling off a chair at work. Orthop.... well I blame the pain meds!
BUT WINNING! harassed my way into seeing surgeon on tuesday and surgery set for Thursday. The first appointment was going to be in a months time- even as a private patient, I mean C'mon!

### yarralea — 2014-08-19T02:23:08+0300

Ah well due to my suprisingly low number of sick days I have been going into work- yesterday and today, and poss tomorrow- saving sick leave for post op.

I have also written the good start of my business plan. Drug, alcohol mental health rehab for young people in the care of DCP- so I think this knee injury- which is worse than previously diagnosed, as I have also done the ACL and there is also come patella issue, this knee issue is the universe giving me some time off to plan and research what I need to run it.
Jack will have to visit. I am exploring grants to assist in buying the property- 100-200 kms north or south of Perth Western australia, and within 50 kms of the coast for seafood access. Any latitude recommendations?

### caroline — 2014-08-19T02:25:31+0300

### yarralea — 2014-08-21T07:56:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-138427>

I'm so pumped! I love fresh enthusiasm, and my new idea- which is actually 5 years in the making, is now taking shape.

I'm an art teacher and I work with students that get kicked out of every other school. They are ADHD, often on the spectrum, lots of anxiety, generally traumatized. I'm a counsellor / social worker with a paintbrush. Anyways, I've been doing neurofeedback with my daughter and husband and the positive danger (LOL) of using NFB is that one changes how they see things, they change the way they process and make decisions. So my frustration that I can only do so much during the time that the students turn up, and it all gets undone when they go home smoke cones and live in the shit of which they can't escape- so they never process their trauma and they stay on the path of low self care and poor outcomes.
So my idea is a drug rehab / life reset where participants have a 100 day, possibly longer program of living without screens, wifi and generally without electricity, artificial light. Earthing, Organic food, seafood and permaculture, teamed with education about simple lifestyle choices and possibly neurofeedback will be a part of it. Participants are staggered in, and build the community, expanding the capacity to 8 students. DCP will most likely fund each participant as they generally have a difficulty housing this sort of client, and they generally pay NGOs top dollar to work with these kids.

so all excited

Basically I want an income, I see a need, and this way I get to have an optimized space. 5 acres enough?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-138428>

### yarralea — 2014-08-23T04:42:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-138648>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=138483)
>
> How are you doing today?

I'm doing well. Apart from a torn knee meniscus and a torn ACL- yes, I don't think I've updated here, but the MRI showed that the ACL was also torn. Clearly, I do not do things by halves! The operation that was supposed to be thursday is now wednesday. I'm back at work, and I have to say, walking- okay hobbling / limping around. Surgeon says, get on it, get it moving he wants me to be able to straighten it and bend it to 120 degrees before he goes in. The plan is to reconstruct the ACL and hope the meniscus isn't too messed up and repair that as well. I'll be working right up to wednesday morning. Then after that I'll be on my magic carpet clinging tightly to my morphine drip.

Oh and my surgeon is just so gorgeous I blushed in his office. Probably about 45, salt and pepper hair, green eyes, tan, tall with seriously no metabolic issues apparent to my eyes. Oh and married with 3 children to a teeny asian lady. That's okay, I'm sure she could disappear quickly.... oh wait, my husband, never mind. He's so healthy he's probably on this forum. Actually, not a chance. When I told him I got into an ice bath post injury he looked kind of stunned. Me, I was devastated that I failed to impress him with easy concept of submerging a whole body into a bath with ice for fun. Maybe we're not soul mates after all. hahahahahha

I'm going to watch my softball team play today. I watched them train for ten minutes the other night, it nearly killed me. But a game, well that is going to be hideous. I'm a controlling force on the diamond, loud, instructive, demanding and directing. I could probably do all those things from the sidelines, but it would not be very effective. It's going to suck suck suck.

### yarralea — 2014-08-24T07:38:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-138748>

Imagine being able to build a sleep out, but free standing. I'm starting to look into mudbrick constructions, and ascertain how I would build some dwellings- with being grounded the main aim. These pods (sleep outs) will be small- 7 foot high, rustic in appearance. Walls to be brick, or concrete that is rendered inside and out. Used coffee grounds to add colour to any wall or timber.... I like the idea of slate floors- laid upon concrete, or a polished concrete floor- or recycled hardwood overlays. These are purely for sleeping, so I need to explore ways to insulate and air out as the location (australia / perth) I'm thinking is freezing at night and can be hot during the day- but I guess that has a few merits too. Natural timber furniture, desk and drawers...The roof would be slanted to catch water and connected to tanks. No other plumbing or electricity.
Self composting toilets on the property that are low squatting in design- each sleep pod has a rope to find the loo at night.
Bees are kept on the property to enhance the garden and provide a source of beeswax for candles- large glass jars need to be collected from now on I'm glad I like olives.
Rotating chook pen- a few chooks per person, the pen moves around the property creating vegetable patches that assist in the sustenance of the people.

Pool- small fibreglass with some iron rods providing the water to be earthed. MMM how to keep it clean? Magnesium and pink salt and keeping it dark? My fish fond uses a UV bacteria filter- how do people here keep their water clean in tubs? I use a wheelie bin for CT, so my water goes on the garden, and replaced every time. Maybe I use the wheelie bin system and the water is recycled to the garden? I miss my wheelie bin ice baths, so can't get in or out of a wheelie bin with this crap knee. Tomorrow morning I'll use the indoor bath- its been 12 days since my last CT- I had one the night of the injury.
Sorry about the dribble! I think I need to read JK's diary and how he has set up his place. Best go reread a few more blog posts. The pain in my head will probably make my knee hurt less! I'm about to take my daughter hand her dyspraxic friend to the pools- good opportunity to re read the epi-paleo reset a bit more.

### caroline — 2014-08-24T07:47:16+0300

Anyway ..... she has an autistic son. She and her husband's family are from around this area .... but they live in England. Her DH is a QC and George Clooney's fiance works under him.

Their firm does a lot of pro bono work ..... who knows where their interests lie......

### yarralea — 2014-08-27T15:17:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-139035>

Well shit just got real. My surgery was supposed to be today, I am devastated to say the least.

My surgery was scheduled for 2 in the afternoon- I was finally, after nil by mouth since 7am- so thirsty, seeing the anesthetist who was asking all the questions and yep, we were looking already to go. In comes the dishy surgeon, who asks me how everything is, and I offer that mobility has improved to where he wanted it, and my gait was changed from a walking with a quite a bent knee- inner front ball planting gait to still bent knee but much better and a flat foot plant (heel and ball at the same time). I also offered up that my calf was pretty sore, so I was not sure whether anything had changed inside the knee. So he gives the back of the knee at the calf a squeeze and I yelp. He is unsure, but suspects a blood clot. Surgery won't be today, and I get wheeled off for an ultrasound which reveals a small clot. So I have 10 injections to give myself of a blood thinner CLEXANE- I've had one, plus a blood test. Return for an ultrasound in 7-10 days to see if it has dissipated. I am happy nothing horrific went wrong in surgery- but am majorly effed off as surgery today was set to mean I was able to fly to see my bestie on the 19th September in Radelaide, and I have ten days from the 29th Sept to go home to Melbs to see my pancreatic cancer / alzheimer's / diabetic dad who is not long for this world- although it seems a handy way to improve your cancer markers is to forget you have cancer- he has past 1 year since diagnosis. His demented state is pretty awful. I am fairly motivated to stay low carb after his stream of diagnoses. So flights up in the air (lol) and surgery EFF knows when. sorry, I'm wanting to swear.

What scares the shit out of me is how / why did I get a blood clot? Physically active, - even in the last week I wasn't on my butt, eating beautifully and having at least weekly ice baths (haven't had one since night of injury- due to not being able to get into my wheelie bin ice bath) Why did I royally murder my knee when I'm doing more for my health right than I have ever before- hello have given up mobile phone after sunset and given up really using it at work. Seriously, I've been eating low carb for 15 years ish, and paleo for the last two. WHY NOW?

Are you eating plenty of seafood? Or just paleo..... Seafood is helping not getting blood clots...

Might be you still have a lot of EMF in your environment... at work.. etc might be you need to do even more changes? That might be what the clot tries so desperately to tell you....

### yarralea — 2014-08-29T01:57:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-139192>

Yes I still work in an environment with wifi- but I was reflecting the other day I work in a school with 25 kids, usually with 5 in my room- much better than most classrooms where 25 kids have 25 mobile phones. Which is why I am wanting out and to start my project, for preventing ill health for me and my family.
In terms of seafood I ate a dozen oysters the morning the clot developed on the monday afternoon- I eat seafood a few times a week, prawns and fish and squid are the usual choices. I have a bulletproof coffee each morning, then a bullet proof cocoa sometimes later in the morning- so 20 g gfbutter, 30g coconut oil and 20 mls of MCT oil- I sometimes have the cocoa instead of the coffee if I'm wanting to avoid the caffeine effect- so there is plenty of fat going in 100 or 200g of fat in hot drinks and 4 egg yolks a day most days.

I am going to do a bulletproof fast for the next 48 hours- just coffee and fat, to see if I can get my body to consume these annoying clot cells- I have regularly done this process over the last year, and love it. Let's see if it loves me.

Like I want to have an ice bath, but am freaking out that I might end up with a clot floating into my lung causing me to die.

### yarralea — 2014-08-30T03:21:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-139307>

I found it on the PC- but I prefer to listen without watching- but this podcast is tricky to find via the podcast app. I found another one, with a guy called Evan- and I listened to that last night. My husband got up at 1 am, tired of my trying to lie full length on our half sheet earthing sheet. It was myself and the two cats (which he despises) and he went to the couch. I found I was less restless after he left- he closed the bedroon door, and the glow of the T-Hub that is in the kitchen was blocked. I despise that thing. I am not appreciating everything wifi at the moment- (I avoid the word hate) I have just discovered that our wifi can be turned off nearly all the time anda cable is capable if using the PC- since I despise the ipads (we have 2, generally not in use) the T-Hub which is like a telephone, digital radio, tablet, effing glorified clock- I want to throw it, the phones are connected the TV T box is connected. I want it all gone. GONE! So in my disgust of everything I have bought a recovery bag- kind of earthing sheet sleeping bag for me to take when I travel, post op when I'm laid up and it will be primarily for my daughter to sleep in- she tends to crawl into ours- and DH tends to lose out to 2 girls and 2 girl cats. Should probably earth the couch.... I also bought an earthing mat for me to stand on when I'm at work. It will mean becoming more mindful when I'm teaching, and making the students come to me.

30 hours in to a fast- just sent my DH to get grass fed organic butter- he's back. COFFEE TIME!

Okay, I'm about to do my first CT since I hopped into the bath in the hour post injury. I figure I am over reacting that I might die with this small clot. I wonder shall I keep my ever so sexy compression stockings on? I think so. Compression shirt might be the go as well. Face first fo shizzle too.

But if I do die, it has been nice here. Cheers Caroline for being a presence in my journey. You're such a leg end (lol) I sometimes read in awe at the understanding of JK's writings from you all- whereas me, I'm sure I understand about a tenth of what is written. (\*people go yeah, a tenth, thats a pretty solid start lol)
I'm moving kind of well considering the damage I have done I just picked up 2 chopped up marrow bones, bone broth resumes tonight- I am wondering whether, since I do have a few fibres left on my ACL if I can regrow those and maybe do laser therapy on the meniscus. But then I get scared of the bucket handle flipping on the meniscus and that being slightly not as bad as a clot floating into a lung.

### caroline — 2014-09-04T10:40:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-139891>

Hi Yarrra .... hows it going???

thinking of you....

### yarralea — 2014-09-05T02:29:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-139951>

breakfasts seem to be getting a seafood twist. I found these awesome marinated Sardines, then I found the sardines unmarinated so I can reuse the marinade and get twice the sardines. So that is todays breakfast- and I have been adding a snack of sardines during the day- dinner is the same, my amazing DH cooks meat and veg every night.

And I have resumed medication, I'm still not walking normally enough and I am noticing the muscle tone in my left calf to be getting a lot softer- my calves have always been awesome, so one is noticeably NOT as defined.

I'm doing well, apart from the fact my surgery is now 14 th October and that means the 9-12 months from sport hasn't even started yet. Even the little things like play tennis at recess and lunch with the students is not doable- although I almost tried yesterday. So Bocce it will have to be
The medication is Clexane 80mls x twice a day- the thing I do not like about it, it is so dehydrating. I'm a girl who always gets 2, often times 3 litres a day, and to wake up dehydrated I find just awful. Being dehydrated even a little is damaging so I'm really hating on this medication, but seeing I have these flights I am going to take it up until my return flight home next sunday has passed, then take a week off- then resume for the next flight to Melbourne. And drink even more water.

Anyone know the movie Juno?
"I drank my weight in Sunny D and I gotta go pronto!"

I wonder how foot reflexology would be? massage? it would get everything moving better?

maybe you should become a rock climber - like Jeremy?

### yarralea — 2014-09-10T09:19:00+0300

I'm drinking so so much water. I had a feldenkreis biofeedback session which has made things move a lot better, Im flying to Radelaide tomorrow so I have been shooting up the clexane to ensure the clot- which has dissipated, does not return bigger and bolder and rougher and tougher.

### caroline — 2014-09-10T10:35:51+0300

### yarralea — 2014-09-15T03:49:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-141020>

Back from my weekend jaunt to Radelaide. That's Adelaide to non South Australians.... Great news my dishy surgeon called me on SATURDAY NIGHT, as he is going on holidays (yes, with his wife and children) But the good news is he asked me about the medication and said it sounded too much, and to get the ultrasound, demonstrate the clot has dissipated and just to keep an eye on it.
Talk about hydrated- on the day of the flight, and during the flight I consumed 4 litres of RO then 3.5 litres of mineral water during the flight. On the saturday I bought 8 litres of pellegrino and they were gone by the flight time on sunday afternoon and I consumed 3 litres on the plane.

Gpoing to the GP today, so hopefully I don't end up on yet another medication. Hopefully less

### yarralea — 2014-10-21T10:23:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-144561>

I couldn't access the forum for a while- it seems now I can. I recall a hack email of sorts....

Anywhooo, back from Melbs where I ate oysters three times a week and stayed with family. On the plus side no one was murdered, staying with family I find difficult- basically because I'm weird (like most of us)

Well I have had my ACL hamstring graft recon, and the mesicus was mushed and Dr Dream coat decided to trim 30% of it. A week later I'm still swollen and sore.
Ive been getting out and sunbathing for an hour or so each day and I have earthing mats for my elevated feet. I have had a post op xray yesterday, and the surgeon hasn't called in alarm yet, so I can only see how movement develops. I can't get the wound wet yet, but am keen to be able to resume ice baths soon.

### yarralea — 2014-11-04T13:48:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-146092>

Clot... gone! Knee operation... done. This was a trim of meniscus by 30% and the ACL was reconstructed via a hamstring graft. The hamstring I feel has some scar tissue- I felt a hideous tweak the other day wjich made me anxious- but it is the scar tissue.. allegedly. So off crutches and walking almost normally. I can't immerse the wound yet- but I am HANGING to get some CT happening. I've been oystering a few times a week- I just bought 2 dozen for breakfast tomorrow.

### yarralea — 2014-11-15T11:16:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-147439>

Well! I have a dishy physio too. I figure if I have to pay to go see these guys I may as well have something nice to look at. I see them both on the same day in a few weeks. That might be too much in the one day, but I will just have to handle it.

Recovery is going quite well really. I can straighten my leg almost fully, but not in the natural gait of walking. I can get beyond a 90 degree bend. I did a bit of wall squat the other day, and will be back walking to work soon, likely monday.

I went to the local pools today- the outdoor pool is really cold- not 12 degrees like my chilly bin, but I swear it is about 20 degrees. Celsius people.... hoping I can start getting down to the beach in the mornings.

### yarralea — 2015-05-12T14:14:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-163946>

Okay. I have just had 6 months away from the forum. I am now back in the wheelie bin ice bath. I have jumped in pretty much every morning for a week.

With my knee being out of order and I'm also going to blame being away for a month at Christmas, my weight had gone up to 85kg and I know people say exercise is not the way to lose weight, but I'm pretty sure being leg up and inactive after being a softball rollerderby girl is pretty damn chunkifying. I don't look a huge amount different, and since it is now cooler weather and the daily ice baths have been evoked, a few kgs have dropped off and I'm feeling confident that winter will be a good thing for shaping up.

I always knew CT was good for sports performance- i WAS USING it an hour before matches and was hitting that high performance zone in every game- you know, when you run so fast and all of a sudden you are standing on third- I describe it like letting a dog off a leash, well since the start of the year I have been back into the gym- just at school, a good hour sesh a week with the students plus a 3 minute brench press workout here, 100 kettle bell swings there. Well the week before I started back in the bath I did our fitness coach's leg press challenge- basically how many leg presses you can do in 3 minmutes, 40 kgs. First week, (3 weeks ago) I did 98 and crawled from it. Next week, 129- after an ice bath that morning. Today, after a week of daily CT I did 138 reps in 3 minutes. (yeah I crawled off that one...)

Now aussie folk, and those 55 degrees fahrenheit equates to about 13 degrees Celsius right? What temp are people dipping into and for how long? I can generally do an hour at about 12 degrees- I usually run short of time and life gets me out of the tub rather than the need to warm up / get out. I don't usually warm up tooquick, just get dressed lightly and walk to work with people thinking I'm crazy in a dress no jacket. It's just about 1.5 to get to work, but as soon as I'm there I have crazy energy levels. Pretty poor fine motor for the first half an hour after CT- some morning make up has been quite hilarious.

My drug rehab farm concept is some backers- just needs monies now. I've started to look at land in the middle of BFN (bum f\*\*k nowhere) within half an hour from the ocean. Looking around Geraldton, north of, about Horrocks / Northhampton way. Have started to wean the husband and child off the teev. Actually the child has been a right obsessed little cow re teev, and that has motivated the husband to consider giving up watching the nightly horrors and tat that is news. If anyone wants to buy a time share at the rehab, let me know....

Anyhoo- I'm back and when I'm engaged in a forum I do much better.

### yarralea — 2015-06-14T14:22:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-168068>

Daily ice bath continues- I think I've been in everyday (usually in the morning) for the last month. I have had a couple of days where I have done two in a day. What its is doing to my lifting is incredible- almost doubling on some weights / body parts.

MMM how cold is too cold? The weather here means the water drops over night and I have outsourced the setting of my ice baths to my DH- he gets up at 4, dumps in the ice in and my 6 am the water is 12 degrees, although we have had a few days where it has been 6 degreees (C) and most often starts off about 9 C and then I raise the temp with my body to about 11 or 12 in the next hour. So is 9 degrees okay? I do believe that CT has addictive qualities- I love the start of my day and like today, couldn't afford the time this morning, I simply had to get my hour of chill in before the day was out.

I set my own ice bath this afternoon, and the temp was 2 C- I actually added some warm water to that. I would think that just too harsh for my feet. I wear 3 pairs of socks when in the ice bath, and are in up to my neck for 50 minutes to an hour or whatever time life affords.

### yarralea — 2015-06-14T14:23:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-168069>

In other news I have not lost any scale weight, I guess I've built some muscle and shape has changed a bit.....

### yarralea — 2015-06-20T06:23:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-169004>

My husband is the best! He bought me wetsuit booties for my feet, which keeps them from getting super cold. I have had to jump out of the ice bath before due to my feet being too cold- but not any longer.

In other news ice baths are, I am sure, ADDICTIVE. Now part of my daily routine I am loving them. My dear husband sets the ice bath each morning, with the temp being usually abound 9 C- but occasionally he has made it a bit chilly, at 6 degrees C. Today I got in at 10 degrees and it just didn't feel as cold, I guess when the feet aren't frozen that makes a difference.
I would love to know what other peoples CT recipes are, what times, what temps, what set ups, what routines people incorporate...

### caroline — 2015-06-20T12:39:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-169022>

A lot of folks say the colder the better....... Hey!!!

### Lahelada — 2015-06-20T18:27:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-169049>

My answer would be whatever temperature is comfortable enough for you to stay immersed for 20 minutes but not comfy if you get my meaning.

### nicld — 2015-06-21T00:03:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-169092>

I love my booties. I am getting better in the summer about going without but when i want to do long cold session, it is a must. My CT most of the time, is inside with an overhead heater going to get some fornier effect. In the summer, I love getting a 2 hour soak in, then going in the sun for some nude rewarming.

Winter time when I can make all the ice I want and add snow, I get it as cold as I can for about 20-30 minutes. Eventually it seems one goes numb to the cold and all is great.

### fitness@home — 2015-06-22T19:37:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-169266>

> [yarralea said:](/goto/post?id=169004)
>
> I would love to know what other peoples CT recipes are, what times, what temps, what set ups, what routines people incorporate...

My husband is a keeper too. He built a CT tank for me and we keep it at a steady 55F (which by online calcs = 12.7 C), so not as cold as what you are using. I use it after work for 30-45 minutes and on the weekends for longer durations.

On weekends I float in our pool outside for an hour in sunshine and then come inside and use the tank.

### yarralea — 2015-06-24T16:42:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-169471>

> [fitness@home said:](/goto/post?id=169266)
>
> My husband is a keeper too. He built a CT tank for me and we keep it at a steady 55F (which by online calcs = 12.7 C), so not as cold as what you are using. I use it after work for 30-45 minutes and on the weekends for longer durations.
>
> On weekends I float in our pool outside for an hour in sunshine and then come inside and use the tank.

Built a tank? Do you have plans, or pictures of your man's amazing construction in your journal?

Today the temp was about 4 degrees in the water and 6 degrees outside according to my thermometer. I could have stayed in for an hour, but got in 15 minutes late- so had only 45 minutes. The water was about 7 degrees when I got out, there's probably 120 litres in the wheelie bin ice bath (classy, I know) so I'm probably never at risk of getting out due to cold, rather stupid life tasks such as attending work get in the way of extended CT. The air temp makes my hands quite cold- they are never in the water. And funnily, the hour after I get out and dress and go to work, my feet are the coldest. I love my wetsuit booties. Love them like I love looking at my knee surgeon...... I miss him. I guess I will go back at see him for my 9 month check up in a few months.

### caroline — 2015-06-25T03:57:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-169510>

How are you coming along with your project Yarra?

### yarralea — 2015-06-25T10:47:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-169560>

As in the rehab? My husband is making it his major project now- so things are starting to move forward. Business name, looking at locations, forming a board- I wonder if JK would sit on the board? I have my board members hand picked and we are writing a constitution. As many Quilt principles that are feasible will be adhered to. Bedrooms will not be wired, but will be earthed. Screen free, except for administration and I will probably have to let them access school work for a brief period during the day, RO water, CT a possibility / option- sucking down oysters and going fishing where possible. But lots of vegetables, lots of yoga / stretching, meditation and likely some neurofeedback options too. Art therapy
We are at the stage where we are heading to a retreat this weekend as a family and will be getting what is in my head, onto a mind map so the DH is more attune. He only has about 600 podcasts to listen to as well, but I tell you, the neurofeedback has really shaped how we are approaching this. He is more open to new ideas, and even was discussing the benefits of having no heating- and this on days where you open your mouth and frosty air steams out!

### caroline — 2015-06-25T11:37:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-169561>

This all sounds wonderful Yarra ...you need to come to our GOHWell summit in Melbourne. Please google Prof Michael Crawford.. Have you joined our website?

### yarralea — 2015-07-17T04:26:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-172161>

Just out of the chilly bin and I can b arely type. My DH says I wonder if ity is better to have a beanie on during, or perhaps post CT. I've never used a beanie (woolly head hat thingy) during CT, so wonder if it beneficial or not really.

What about post CT? I just shiver more when out than in- I wear my ugg boots but dress normally, which is lightly. It is best to warm up naturally and slowly right?

I just came home from Melbs- saw my dad- his dementia is worse. He still knows us and still have a glimmer of his sense of humour. I really hope he doesn't hang on much longer. He has pancreatic cancer, which is pretty deadly - and thereforebetter than languishing with dementia- but he was diagnosed nearly 2 years ago and the cancer is not rapidly progressing. He now has some spots on his lungs, but not a huge concern- his breathing is not laboured. He is tired most of the time, some days better than others. He had a good day when we held his 80th birthday party, but was wiped out for a few days after wards. When he feels well he hates being in the care home and says it's a waste of a bed, a waste of time and he should be doing things. However his mental function is pretty low, low executive functioning. He isn't really my dad any more.

But the good news is I survived living with my sister for 7 days. No murders were committed, although I had to seriously bite myu tongue.

### caroline — 2015-07-17T06:39:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-172164>

Hi Yarra!
it is better to wear nothing for awhile after C/T ....just kind of jiggle around! clean the house????

A beanie is a great idea ..... you lose a lot of heat thru your head.

It is freaking cold here

the worst thing is the very high winds...

so sorry about your Dad ........xoxo

So Ms Caroline, losing heat through your head- advantageous or not? In my mind not, but I'm not as up to speed with the how and why of the JK way as you are. I have been meaning to go back to the blogs again, but so so much going on at the moment!

### caroline — 2015-07-18T10:40:01+0300

### yarralea — 2016-02-05T05:57:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-188294>

it has been too long.

Since last post so much has happened. Firstly my dad died just after his 80th birthday. Such a relief. At the end of the year I quit my cushy awesome job teaching art in a school jammed full of struggling wifi and bought an old stone cottage in Burra South australia. Aka gateway to the desert. The house is just stunning and the reset community an achievable goal. it gets cold here at night and in summer the temp tange can be 8c at night to 40 plus day. Winter is a frigid affair.

Currently i am sunbathing nude and about to have a dip in my pool which without ice sits at about 19 degrees Celsius. Sadly it is indoor but has a big doorway to look out to light.

It is a fibreglass pool set in a slate surround- i wonder the best way to earth the water? i also still have my wheelie bin ice bath but am enjoying the pool so have been neglecting it.

### caroline — 2016-02-05T09:16:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-188311>

Bout time girl! Tell us more!

How do you feel about visitors???

So sorry about your Dad xo

### yarralea — 2016-02-09T02:11:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-188581>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=188311)
>
> Bout time girl! Tell us more!
>
>
> How do you feel about visitors???
>
> So sorry about your Dad xo

Cheers re the condolences. His life and demise was a poster of what NOT to do. At least his dementia didn't get as bad as some poor folks suffer before the cancer- well the morphine ramp up accelerated death- did.
I feel awesome about visitors!!!! Anyone who dares to challenge themselves to the wheelie bin chilly bin can certainly do so. The crew on this forum are probably well up for that challenge. The water is directly from my rainwater tank, and I usually drop a little magnesium in there.
Yesterday my Infrared sauna arrived. Now situated in the corner next to the plunge pool, it is just awesome. I had a few FIR sauna sessions at a friends home and just loved the feeling. Not so sure on the colour therapy uses and misuses, so I've not delved into that- I guess whatever matches the time of day, right? DH likes a sauna after getting in from his morning exercise (I know I know....) but I think I prefer them at night. Cold in the morning, heat at night?

I would love to know if anyone has any strong opinions on plunging from a 54 c sauna into a 18 degree pool. The pool is 19 degrees but after a cool spell, it has been at 18 C. Winters here are something I have been "warned" about by the locals, so when its a top of 9 and -2 C overnight I wonder how the pool temp will respond.

In regards to the NOW reset therapeutic community- I have started a website- it's not quite live- I need a few more technical tips to get it functioning just right. I'm using squarespace, and the live support is not the time I am up an on the computer...
The property needs a lawn development for earthing, and I need to change every globe in the property from brilliant white to low and warm- I personally use a lot of candles but not quite ready to let others be in charge of fire in this beautiful house.

Hey Caroline, if you want to swing by my facebook page to see lots of pics let me know! It is the most stunning cottage- 20 foot high timber ceilings, antique wall friezes and old skool light switches. AND the property behind this one just came on the market..... build capacity, share a bore for gardens..... rubs hands..... empire building evil laugh.....

But here's a picture of my chilly bin for today. Trying to get a 4 degree bath happening. Using every kilo of ice available.

Hey Yarra! ......you are an amazing work in progress! You are an inspiration!

We are looking at getting a 4 wheel Dive and some kind of a caravan ..... any ideas?

You never know who may show up on your doorstep !

Have you hung up the roller blades???

### yarralea — 2016-02-09T02:38:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-188584>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=188583)
>
> Hey Yarra! ......you are an amazing work in progress! You are an inspiration!
>
> We are looking at getting a 4 wheel Dive and some kind of a caravan ..... any ideas?
>
> You never know who may show up on your doorstep !
>
> Have you hung up the roller blades???

Roller SKATES love, and no. There is a nice outdoor smooth as tennis court, and I can skate my little heart out- all alone on that. There's an oval with an incline to sprint up, and plenty of biking around. It's only just of a km to my daughters school so a quick up and back is done twice a day.

You're in Sydney right? Well Burra is a handy 13 hours from Syds and 11 hours from Melbs. We recently drove a car we bought back from Queensland. Perth is a 3 day trip and with kids.... well, there might not be any survivors for our friends from over there. We are 150 km north of Radelaide, and I welcome any Radelaide peeps to make contact and come and visit. Burra is on the map for grey nomads caravanning around our beautiful brown (donut hole scorched) land. Burra has an ageing population, and homes here are about as affordable as anywhere. I am LOVING the freedom of NOT owing a million dollars in mortgages. The stress that one lives with when in that situation is highlighted when it has been removed. The biggest stress I have is the fact that the kitchen sink takes 2 hours to drain. So water recycling to the garden it is!

I should also regale you that I was up at 3 effing 30 this morning. Woken in a nano second to the sound of the cat vomming all over my kingsize bed. World's most hideous way to wake up- cat is still alive for those who might wonder.

### caroline — 2016-02-09T04:54:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-188593>

Yikes .....

We are an hour south of Sydney.

### yarralea — 2016-02-15T03:52:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-189060>

I have noticed a huge boost in my sleep since adding a lot more sunbaking and sauna with cold plunges. I have never slept 9 hours two nights in a row, but that totally happened last night.

I'm kind of in love with my sauna. So it's a Far IR and I have it at around 58 degrees Celsius. Cool plunge in the end. I did a plunge mid sauna and it just didn't seem right.

I am still seeking an idea of how to earth my pool water. Both for my plunge pool (mid summer 19 degrees, and my chilly bin)

### caroline — 2016-02-15T23:05:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-189125>

Great Yarra .....tell us more

Where did you get it? Expensive?

### yarralea — 2016-03-04T01:01:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-190153>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=189125)
>
> Great Yarra .....tell us more
>
> Where did you get it? Expensive?

The FIR sauna, it came from the glorious gumtree. It's an iHealth 2 person- Far. My friends had exactly the same sort- they had researched quite a lot and when I had a session in theirs I felt quite comfortable. We got it for $2600 (aud) My DH likes an AM sauna, after his morning walk- and when I say morning, it's practically the middle of the night, like 430am! He likes it when he returns, so his body is already warmed up. He also likes to have the temp up very high (62 degrees C). I have managed to change this creature of habit a fair bit- but have to acknowledge my limits. His post dinner snack of chocolate has been cut back, so I'm just going to acknowledge the wins! I like mine either before yoga at around 530pm, or around 8 pm at night. I find I sleep so much better, longer. Not so sure I should be having one every night, but I want to, but have nights off- kind of having time coffee.
In other news I have started a new job. I found someone on facebook who runs a regenerative farm that grows ethical, pastured etc chickens. lambs, pigs and soon to be beef, maybe DUCKS. YUMMMMMMM! I am getting to know the farming sustainability, but really I am the delivery driver- 3 days a week. One day we pack for orders so I am learning a lot about the cuts of animals, best ways in which to treat them. They are Joe Salatin fans (Joe has spent time at their farm) and they are hard working people. Given the fact that the farm is nowhere near the coast, it is not an oyster farm. Just imagine living at an oyster bank...... They have an organic, informed about soil microbiome and the impact of chemicals- so we get on well. I get paid actual cash, but there is a major meat pay off that makes me happy. I have access to as many chicken feet as I could ever use.

Life is good.

### yarralea — 2016-03-04T01:05:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yarraleas-journal-with-dd7-in-mind.8385/post-190154>

In other news, I have started a website for the NOW reset community. The aim is to have a live in environment where people can come to digitally detox, deal with trauma and learn to live a life that is more in line with our natural biology. Essentially, I am seeking people with addiction problems.
on facebook, the page is naturally optimized wellness
[https://www.facebook.com/naturallyoptimizedwellness/](https://www.facebook.com/naturallyoptimizedwellness/)
and the website is
naturallyoptimizedwellness.com although I think I need something shorter.....
