Who was it who noticed their heart beating faster at an intersection with power lines? And now I can't stay on the computer for 30 minutes without my eyes complaining. I think some of these sensitivities were there before, but the symptoms were lost in our huge collection of other symptoms. But other symptoms are new, yes, like your mega anxiety. Your experience, that you rationalized as concern over the blogs, has actually helped me see that my sister is anxious. Yesterday it had been raining for a couple days, and during my visit she was complaining about the usual stuff more than normal. Now I realize that the lack of sun was decreasing her power even more, with the resultant increase in anxiety.

Yes, I remember that copper and zinc need to be balanced in the body, not too much, not too little. But external copper, I'll have to think about that.

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-17T18:00:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-revelation.8464/post-98161>

This is such an amazing thread. I made a realization on my RC this morning. that when we're in the moment, we can intuitively identify things that may occur and impact us, either negatively or positively.

taking off your earrings, and rings really resonates w/me Audrey. As I don't wear my rings at home, and up until I' started working a desk job - I NEVER work rings or watches - b/c when your work in a warehouse or flight line environment - they can easily catch and you can loose a finger or a hand. It bugs DH that I don't wear my rings all the time. I will when we go out in public for events, but running around town etc - I just don't.

I've got diamond stud earrings that I saved my own money to buy and I wear them daily. The reality is I should take them out and let the holes close up. but what to do with that... I now look at all my friends who've pierced their infant daughters ears and wonder WFT are we doing to our children? Chaos does not have pierced ears, and we made a rule that if she wants them she has to be at least 13, but before that we'll talk about how the metal in the ears could be a very bad thing, and why.

The piercings also have me wondering about nose piercings and how that may just be a directional sign for emf to shoot up the nasal cavity to the vortex of the brain.. uggh No wonder some of my friends have such off kilter thoughts - they're directing the EMF straight to their brain and overloading the system...

### Rick — 2013-10-17T04:42:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opioid-peptides-in-gliadin-and-casein.8476/post-98073>

So I'm wondering if anyone could shed some light on these exorphins that allegedly create the mild euphoria especially when eating wheat and dairy together, like classic comfort foods such as mac & cheese, pizza, fettucine al fredo etc...

Is it necessary to have EMF involved for these peptides to break the intestinal brush border and the BBB to hit our opioid receptors? How could we test this now since presumably all modern humans have been chronically exposed to high freq RF signals so we all have membranes that are somewhat permeable at least. Impossible to design an experiment that separates our diet from our environment now?

Can we blame these foods alone for their addictive nature, that is to say, prior to 1895? Or do we know empirically that these foods were addictive long before Marconi due to the permanent transition from HGs to agriculturists? Aside from the social pressures to develop stable communities, specialization of work, commerce and the arts, was there also chemical persuasion to give up the hunter gatherer life for settling down to cultivate grains, beans and potatoes?

Just curious if any moderators or admins would care to weigh-in on this topic...

RH

### QuantumRadical — 2013-10-19T21:55:24+0300

AC (Audrey/Caroline), I hide the geek in me pretty easily. I don't find this easy or hard, just absolutely fascinating--breaks the dogma we all believed in for so long. If you get really interested in something, you just find the time to figure it out. I wish I was working in a connected field, but right now I am not. At 60 society limits what is available to you--so I am trying to figure it out--would love to go get a PhD in BioPhysics and have put some thought into how to do that at age 60. I would also love to do applied R&D in this whole area too, but government agencies tend to put up barriers no matter how much they say they are looking for new ideas and innovation. And without a PhD or MD you automatically lack credibility. I always looked for enthusiasm and attitude first--degrees, not so much--when I hired people. But, .... Bureaucracy is what it is. I have always been pretty good at taking very complex topics or issues and distilling them down to the "actionable" essence. Jack is a master at this as well. His protocols are very straightforward but the implications are profound if you follow them- or not. I ramble.

I may get some time to listen to the webinar soon so I can see what Jack said about me and my bro'!

# Thread: Lava
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lava.8485/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 36
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lava — 2013-10-17T21:34:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lava.8485/post-98173>

I begin my journal admitting to myself that it has taken me almost 2 years to write this stuff down...My journey begins as the youngest child in a military family. The odd "redheaded" child..As a child I had several kidney infections and was hospitalized at the age of 5 (same hospital Jackie Kennedy was at) with an unknown virus. I was given mega doses of gamma globulin. Several years past and all I can recall is skin rashes, warts, those kinds of things..Had an early pregnancy. tubal ligation at the age of 20. No regrets. He is now 38 years old with an arthritic back and neuropathy. Anyway, back to me.. I ended up with large fibroids in my uterus and breasts. I had my uterus removed..Kept the ovaries. A few years later I am diagnosed with Hep C after many years of complaining of fatigue...I cleared the virus after 4 months on interferon..I am still clear. That was 12 years ago..I still carry around a huge ball and chain of fatigue. I have been from doctor to doctor...One (woman doctor) told me I was just getting old...WOW..
Finally I see an anti-aging doctor who took the time to measure my thyroid and adrenals and iron...she plasters me with HC and synthetic thyroid meds which of course I had a bad reaction to. (I guess I forgot to mention I am major chemical sensitive) Also she said I had recurring EBV. My glands were swollen, vertigo, the whole works. I could not get out of bed...My body started breaking out in basal cell carcinomas and warts..I am covered now. gross....tmi tmi....AND THEN I stumble across this webpage..It's been almost 2 years..The minute I stopped being a vegetarian things started to open up for me....I started with the Leptin rx and leaky gut..Wow, who knew!!! I then started on the adrenal rx..About this time I also began learning how screwed up my circadian rhythm was. So I did all of that. Now I sleep. There are no artificial lights at our house now. Also FYI, the Big Island doesn't have blue lights because of the telescopes on Mauna Kea. We live in the country so no wi-fi from other sources and we unplug the wi-fi at night. The magnetico sleep pad is unbelievable...I am just now starting to drop the weight I gained from the HC.. My thyroid is getting optimal. Still low t4..adrenals are dicey, still up and down. iron is back up to optimal, shape of my red blood cells still iffy, kidney functions still hovering low, hormone panel still sucks. My Ana is still high and now the pattern is speckled along with the homogen? (can't remember the word) No diagnosis on anything. All other labs are optimal.. I no longer see doctors unless I am bleeding to death.
One thing I forgot to add to this journal is the fact that when we bought this house 7 years ago there was an cell phone antenna for the truckers. I wondered about it and my intuition told me to get rid of it..but I didn't. Then my little dog died of a brain tumor and all bets were off. The tower was out of here...It's gone now. But I think we still suffer the damage it caused. it was maybe 100 yards from our home....I recently found out one of my cats has a tumor. We lost 3 cats during the tower days.
I also learned last night that years of working nights is still affecting me...very interesting...Some days are great..some days fatigue hits me like a brick wall......

I am really glad you are here and getting your life back .....baby step by baby step......

The Q&A on Monday will be huge for us.... see you there......

### Lava — 2013-10-18T22:49:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lava.8485/post-98293>

I have indeed been gob smacked......all of this is really coming together for me..I love it!!!

Had a long talk with my compounding pharmacist last week..I told her I only wanted progesterone cream. She argued with me for a half an hour about how I needed E3....so now I have a lovely rx for E3 PG and Test. I'm not going to use it...What a waste. It's hard to get through the dogma of others, even as a paying customer.."she" knew what was right and I was an idiot. LOL

Caroline ~ I only do the large hoop earrings if it's a special occasion.....I am a farmer so pretty much without jewels most of the time..One great thing about Big Island living is you can wear your PJ's to town and no one would blink an eye..

Still working on my seafood consumption. Leary of the local fish due to Japan leak...Still try and get down for CT. There is a small sea/saline pond the locals call "ice pond." and yes, it is ccccooooooolllld.

Staring my permaculture garden this weekend..digging in the dirt, barefoot, under the sun...

### Lava — 2013-10-31T19:48:41+0200

Today I have to laugh at myself but also pat myself on the back. I think I have finally gotten through to my DH..I am in the "I want a perm mode." When I get this way terrible things happen: I end up with a toxic perm. In my google search on perms I find a new perm called a "digital perm"....who knew, right? I am telling my DH about the "new perm" technology and he said, "Are you kidding me? Your going to plug your hair in? What about all the EMF's involved? Don't do it!"......blew my mind and I thought wow, how could I not have thought of that!!! So no digital perm for me, no perm period. But what a great way to find out that all of this is sinking in with DH!!! Victory!!!

It's Christmas....Merry Christmas and Happy Holidays to everyone...Thank you for all of the info here and the support....

### Curves — 2013-12-21T19:23:37+0200

Lava, thank you so much for starting this thread. I wanted to add my warm thoughts and deep appreciation to Jack, especially today on the Winter Solstice. Since initiating Jack's protocols a year and a half ago, I've gone from depressed and finding it hard to get out of bed in the morning to vivacious and joyful. Although I've been away from the forum for a while, it is not for lack of love for Jack and the community here. Since I last posted, my boyfriend and I have moved from our two small apartments in San Francisco to one nice big home near Portland, Oregon. Although I'm over an hour from the beach now, I can easily run out in the yard and stick my feet directly in the cold damp earth. The cold air and earth are intoxicating -- after a few moments barefoot outside I can feel my energy shift and my body relax. I've also just completed a 3 month public speaking course so I can better publicly articulate my experience (more on that another time). I'm pretty damn excited for all the beautiful things that will reveal themselves in 2014. Love to you all and a big bear hug to you Jack. Muah!

Thank you Caroline and nonchalant!

I love being here in Oregon and I love the house we are living in, but it's a temporary location. This city is waaaay too highly electrified. There are high tension electric towers weaving in and out of this area feeding the Intel fabs nearby. The scariest thing is that they actually call the land under the towers "parks!" Seriously, this is land where people walk and hike and kids play -- it makes me crazy each time I see it. Plus, many of the cell phone towers are physically located in a high school.

Inside our home though we are working to minimize exposure to unnatural EMFs and artificial light. Although I'm never 100% comfortable given the local electrification I do feel it's a big step in the right direction.

So good to be in all your company again

# Thread: Merrily Measuring My Magnetism
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/merrily-measuring-my-magnetism.8486/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 42
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martin — 2013-10-18T00:32:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/merrily-measuring-my-magnetism.8486/post-98184>

I got my meter setup and started measuring in my house. At my studio workbench, there are lots of tools and some electrical devices, so I've been concerned about what my exposure has been.

Using the Swedish National Energy Department's advisory warning of schoolchildren not being exposed to ELF mag fields over **3.0 mG** for long periods of time every day, I set about duplicating a typical exposure when I work here.

My MacBook is showing **.5 mG** (milligauss) at a distance of about an inch off the surface of the keyboard's trackpad. It varies as I move it across the keyboard due to the inner workings of the electronics inside. If I hold the measuring probe down on the keyboard, it goes as high as **15.8 mG** in places, and as little as **1.6 mG** in others. But these measurements fall quickly away as soon as I move the probe away from my trusty laptop. (note to self: look for external keyboard for a laptop?)

12 inches (30 cm) away from the computer, at my torso, it reads **0.00 mG.**

Sweet.

I have external computer speakers with a power supply (heavy thingy that plugs into the home's wiring) that reads **10 mG** at 12 inches. I don't want to spend much time with my head near that thing, so I ran an extension cord in order to place it further from my precious body and mind as not to degrade it too much so I can be ready when a certain Aussie comes to town to check out the compound for the future commune.

Next was a soldering gun that I use to repair and build projects. It's a classic Craftsman model from the '70's that I bought at a garage sale for $3. It's in perfect shape and barely used, so I'm quite proud of it. These tools have been used for generations all over the world to fix and make things and I was floored when it overloaded my meter's setting and I had to go to the next highest range in order to measure it - **180 mG!!!** This was taken at 12 inches from my head, like someone working closely on a project.

Ugh.

So, for generations we've been dehydrating our ass into the state we find ourselves in. Who knew?!

Becker. And now we do.

More later from the Big Head

We will need another success update from you when you have time .... you know I hang on every single word....

Of course it's you, my dear. This IS serious Men's business!

I'm on a mission alright. I've strayed from my course from time to time, but I've become resolute. While I've made progress, there's still a long way to go.

Summer was decent, albeit rainy as hell, and I have a wonderful tan to show for it. Many a day, I'd strip down to me skivvies (hold on tight, girl!) and work in the garden. The photons were lovely!

GQ has not returned my numerous calls and emails.

Soon, an update. But I gotta have something to brag on besides my fat ass head.

Big Fat Head

OMG, I remember reading that men working in an office surrounded by electrical equipment were developing breast cancer and the reading was only 92 mG!

[http://sammilham.com/Male breast cancer in office workers.pdf](http://sammilham.com/Male%20breast%20cancer%20in%20office%20workers.pdf)

### Martin — 2013-10-18T18:38:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/merrily-measuring-my-magnetism.8486/post-98278>

Another tip: I have a decent Klein multimeter (a regular device for measuring all kinds of electrical currents) and found that it was not sensitive enough to be used in conjunction with the MagCheck 95e probe (which can be bought separately - I did!) and ended up going back to Magnetic Sciences and getting the matched multimeter in the kit linked above. I ran side-by-side tests and confirmed that the METEX meter they provide is showing me what I was missing before.

Know better, do better.

I'm off again, with probe in hand, to measure my little corner of the world!

Biggie

### Lava — 2013-10-18T23:10:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/merrily-measuring-my-magnetism.8486/post-98294>

[http://www.magneticsciences.com/MSI95Gaussmeter.html](http://www.magneticsciences.com/MSI95Gaussmeter.html) Just to be sure, is this the one you decided on?

### Martin — 2013-10-18T23:34:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/merrily-measuring-my-magnetism.8486/post-98296>

Hi Lava, yes, that's the one. It has a separate probe (blue boxy thingy in the photo of the case) that you plug into the meter and then hold the probe near an object to test it's field. You rotate the probe slowly to find the highest reading, and you can use it to zero in on a source versus a meter with a three-axis configuration (three probes built into the meter itself that read in three different directions at once, averaging the total score).

There are bennies to a single-axis like this one, and different advantages to the three-axis meters. I like fine tuning my measurements and exploring so I chose this one. It seems like the best for the money and has a great accuracy history.

Good hunting!

BGH

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-10-19T18:54:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/merrily-measuring-my-magnetism.8486/post-98342>

Using a gauss meter, I found that buried power lines are detected. The highest reading was on top of the green transformer box. The reading dropped considerably 30 feet away. The reading continued to drop 50 yards away. Once again, distance is key ... the more distance between you and a source, the less EMF impact.

As to comparing to the impact of residential overhead powerlines to buried power lines, I don't know. But I recall my recent experience of my heart racing while stopped at a traffic under large number of high voltage power lines. So I would infer, the greater amount of voltage (whether overhead or buried), the more EMF we experience.

Great question!

This was a topic brought up by DW at breakfast this morning. She'd been watching a documentary on earthing and brought up our decision to take the earthing sheets off our bed last year due to our concern that we were picking up currents from the overhead and buried power lines near where we ran our ground wire out to the earth, not to mention the fields we were picking up while in bed with our body acting like an "antenna". The EMF webinar was invaluable in cutting thru the bs and speculation.

Now that I have my gaussmeter, I'm going to go out and see what I can see around this yard thing. I figure I need much more sensitive, sophisticated measuring equipment to really get at the facts, but this may show me something.

Having said all that, I take it for granted that when I'm down in the garden or in the field, I'm doing the best I can. If I lived in a neighborhood with lots of houses and power lines running everywhere, I'd find a place where there is no Body Voltage readings. It seems to be the one value where I can really pick up on easily with the equipment I have.

This is all opinion from the...

Big Headed Guy.

I've been using the body voltage meter to reduce the emfs. I was surprised at how high our hot tub in the master bath was eventhoughnwe didn't use it. Turned it off at the circuit. Also, my daughters room is above the garage and was able to bring it down by eliminating the fluorescent lighting in the garage and turning circuit off at night. Still working on this. I am able to work a lot at home although I am an IT software developer. I have found the lowest readings are from the basement or on nice days outside on a covered concrete porch area. I've eliminated all blue light by sitting close to a window.....except for my computer. ### nicld — 2013-10-20T23:25:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/merrily-measuring-my-magnetism.8486/post-98438>

Ok, I have been checking the stuff around the house today and it appears that we are not too bad.

When I stand in the middle of our DD's room I get a reading of 0 mG.
Where I sit at home 0.3 mG
My side of the bed 0.1 mG.
the sound machine that I use to drown out the husband's nightly noises 0.6 mG.
Laptop 0.9 mG.
I could barely get a reading off of the TV's.
My alarm clock that does sit above my head on a wood board did read 3.5 mG, just a little high.
The kindle was 0.2 mG
The LED lights in the shelf above the bed was 16.5 mG when on and 13 when off. Think these might have to stay off and unplugged.

Now the bad bad ones.
The space heater off 44 mG on 55 mg. Ughhhh, no more using this to heat up
Our outlet by the phone highest reading was 57 mG.
Power cords behind the DD's chair highest I got was 141 mG EEEEEKKKK. No wonder why the kid seems screwed up. Since her bedroom reading is 0 mG I will be keeping a magnet by her chair and will be trying to up it.
Microwave was 100 mG, no surprise, been keeping that unplugged.
Stove and fridge was not too bad 4 and 3.7 mG.

I still have a couple more things to check but I think this was a pretty good start. Oh and I have rented the meter that Martin recommended.

So what I need to do yet is see about EMF reducing or eliminating power cords. Or maybe I can build something to shield the power bars. Find a different lamp for the bedroom and unplug the LED lights.

The DD gets funding from the county and I think I might look into them buying her a magnetico pad. But I have to really figure out how to teach the hubby about this and get his buy in so we can ask the county for the pad. Right now she is on generic Ritalin to keep her from hurting herself but if I can make her field better and get her off of the drugs that would be a win.

Question - how far away should EMF emitting devises be or is it a accumulation of all that are in the house. I have not found anything on this so I might do an Ask Jack question on this.

### caroline — 2013-10-18T08:11:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bg-5-and-bg-11.8489/post-98233>

Sammy - I have posted elsewhere about frozen sushi grade tuna I found in a fish market. It is vac packed in about 4oz pieces and it is beautiful. Each piece is less than $5.00 ...$22.00 Kilo

I just let it thaw slightly and then slice thinly. You can just add lime juice and ginger or whatever you fancy.

I usually buy big cans of salmon from Aldi and also am going to get some of the packs of frozen fish there and make the seafood stew in the recipe section.

Have you tried a MHS? mackerel head smoothie? I buy some small frozen fish from an asian store. I thaw slightly and chop a bit and throw in a blender with corriander and lemon juice and water and blitz the crap out of it!!!

fish stores have really cheap fish heads ... the other day I saw salmon heads for $3.50 K. I would just simmer gently. There is plenty of meat on them. I bet if you got to know a fishmonger - he would give you some for free.
When people have fish filleted - the fishmonger often just throws the bones etc away .... you could make stock.

Hemming just spoke about this somewhere the last day or so......

### Inger — 2013-10-18T10:04:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bg-5-and-bg-11.8489/post-98245>

Sammy... I would not think too complicated.. just eat wild foods in season, lots of raw fish and wild meats or grassfed. If you have had too much of O6 in your diet until now, just avoid them for a while. Avocado and olive oil are pretty fine anyways, just take care to find very high quality EVOO (freshness is the clue). Or, if you are thinking you have too much O6 in your body, just avoid them all together and eat only fatty fish and coconut oil and fatty meat. Jack has said, if you eat very much fatty fish you can use coconut or other saturated fats with it without a issue, because you get more than enough anyways. That is how I do. I just eat so much fatty fish that I do not need to worry about absorption issues. Then once in a while you can make you a salmon or mackerel sashimi or fish head smoothie and no other fats added, so they go straight to your brain

> [Awainer1 said:](/goto/post?id=98259)
>
> Sammy I use bacon fat or rendered pork lard when I cook seafood. It has the least amount of saturates fat among oils you can cook with. Butter and coconut oil have more. You can render your own pork lard in a crockpot its easy.

Tallow is only slightly higher in saturated fat but has a better O6:O3 ratio. I'm not saying any of them is universally better, just adding another option

# Thread: How do I get to ketosis?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-get-to-ketosis.8492/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Julie5 — 2013-10-18T07:10:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-get-to-ketosis.8492/post-98231>

It seems to me that on the leptin /rx I am supposed to eat just seafood/meat and fat. Is that right? I tested for ketosis yesterday, and I'm not very far along that route, but I've been eating some veggies (asparagus, mushrooms, carrots, beets, spinach, celery, onions). I have very high HDL, and according to JK it's OK to eat the nightshades with a high HDL. i only eat small portion of one veggie at dinner only. Should I cut out ALL veggies and stick with just fat/protein?

### Inger — 2013-10-18T09:27:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-i-get-to-ketosis.8492/post-98239>

Julie I think if you get under 20-30 grams of CH it is fine as long as you eat veggies in season? You need to count the carbs in the beginning, cooked carrots easily have quite a lot of them etc. Maybe try leafy greens? I myself eat almost zero veggies, I chew on raw dirty carrots from the garden once in a while, to get bacterias in for my gut. And I eat wild raw dried mushrooms, very seldom I fry them gently too in butter. I eat raw onions almost every day, and I like organic cucumber for hydration. Actually my only real carb source is wild raw berries but the berries that grow here has very little carbs in them. I think Jack has said he covers his veggies in fat  so always eat enough fat! I eat so much fat if the calorie theory was right I should be 300 pounds by now..lol But I am just fine

# Thread: Psychic abilities.....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychic-abilities.8501/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 53
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2013-10-19T03:45:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychic-abilities.8501/post-98307>

Has anyone here ever been to a Psychic encounter?

I went last night to a group thing........This completely knocked my sox off...I have never, ever experienced anything like it.

I used the restroom before it started ... This was at a wellness centre. They have blackboards on the walls and people can leave inspirational quotes etc. One of the quotes ....."Quantum Physics - the science of spirituality"

I though to myself - Wow ...I am in the right place!

I was completely mesmerised by this woman......her energy was unbelievable.

There is a bit of a backstory here - sorry in advance....

I met a young woman at the beach about six months ago and we have become friends.
The first time we met accidently - we wound up talking for about 5 hours.

We taked about so many things. She had lost her Dad a few years ago and we talked about my DH.
She asked me if I had ever seen a physic and if I was open to it. I said of course I would love to do that - I am always open to anything.

She told me her sister was a physic and she would email me her phone number .... she did and I went into a complete melt down the next day walking on the beach. For some reason - just the thought of this woman really gave me some clarity on some things - but i was terrified.... It is like she has a direct line to my inner being.

Yesterday morning my friend called and told me her sister was doing a group thing in town and would I like to go? I said I would love to. By evening I was a wreck - my energy all depleted etc.

I can't explain any of this - except right now I am thinking that she is taking all my negative energy and she will replace it with positive energy ....crazy I know....

I think I am headed into something amazing..........

Any one had any experience with a medium?

### nicld — 2013-10-19T04:11:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychic-abilities.8501/post-98311>

Nope but I find the whole idea of them fascinating. We have a show on here in the states about a medium and what she can pick up on is unreal. Whether she can truly channel loved ones or not if it gives someone peace within their soul, how can it be bad. I do think that there are people who are more sensitive to energy.

Maybe you can ask about that little boy that visited you on the beach.

Keep us posted on how it is going. I am very intrigued.

### caroline — 2013-10-19T04:18:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychic-abilities.8501/post-98312>

I saw with my own eyes what she can do ........she had people in tears ...a box of tissues kept making the rounds. One man - she had to take him into another room because he was quite overcome - nothing bad - just extremely emotional.

I didn't realize that there is a 'Medium" code of ethics - she will never tell you anything bad.

She asked one woman if her and husband were trying to conceive - and she said no - absolutely not. The medium then said she should be keeping her legs crossed at all times!

### fitness@home — 2013-10-19T05:32:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychic-abilities.8501/post-98314>

I have seen a "reader" about three times in the the past 15 years. Each one picked up things about me or my children that were quite accurate. The first one was excellent.

I recorded each session and then listened & transcribed them. Need to go back and them review again .

Each of these women had other careers. The readings were how they used their gift. It was always random when I saw them. The thought would cross my mind and I'd find someone local.

Interesting to say the least...

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-19T07:48:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychic-abilities.8501/post-98316>

The answer is in EE5 and EE6........if your brain is a quantum computer......then it means they can also be used as an antenna too.......and they can pick up and sense these electromagnetic fields.......of people. Energy is never created or destroyed.......it can only be transformed. Think about that for a while..................it is far more profound than you can ever imagine.

### Inger — 2013-10-19T08:41:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychic-abilities.8501/post-98319>

Very interesting. I have always been careful about mediums, never went to one. My gut told me to be careful. IDK... maybe there are not only good energies involved. I do not know for sure. Interesting to hear about your experiences! My one sister went to one long ago if I remember correctly.. I do not think it did any good but I have to ask her again about it

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-19T08:46:30+0300

### Josh — 2013-10-19T09:26:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychic-abilities.8501/post-98322>

There are "sciences" that have worked this type of phenomena out in detail. Both Yoga and Taoist systems have extensive technologies for managing this type of phenomena. In general this type of phenomena is not used casually and out of the context of training for transformation and or healing work. Its manifestation in the physical universe can certainly be partially explained and probably even measured at the quantam mechanical level. There are no market models to support the rigorous development of technology to measure this type of phenomena so that more people would consider it "real". I was quite skeptical until my life took a turn in my mid twenties and I left medical school to study yoga and meditation in India where I have spent 7 of the last 20 years or so. I have experienced so much phenomena that I now realize the real challenge once we become tuned to it is to manage the information and to remain responsive in the physical universe where our body actually is in a given moment. Most teachers, caution students to not "indulge" in these sorts of experiences and explorations. I have been taught that they may occasionally offer some perspective or perhaps "clearing" on some level. As Buddha was reported to have said, regardless of what happens "CHeriveti, Cheriveti"-keep going...

### Josh — 2013-10-19T09:36:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychic-abilities.8501/post-98323>

A good simple resource is "The Magus of Strovolus" by Kyriacos Markiades. The context is "Western" and within the context of christianity. The examples and explanation of phenomena are touching and straightforward. Markiades was a skeptical Professor of Sociology at the University of Maine and the "Magus" was a friend of his family in Cyprus.

### caroline — 2013-10-19T09:59:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychic-abilities.8501/post-98324>

This is by far and away the most profound thing I have ever encountered. I guess there was about thirty people there last night .... she knew no one except, obviously, her sister who I was with. She hugged and greeted everyone who came but the people she interacted with was not premeditated in any way ....... It was all so intense but so lovely and heartwarming.
This was, in no way unkind or thoughtless .....and it was very hard work as she had to sort out the spirit coming to her and crowding her mind and then figuring out what the message was exactly with the people involved. I suppose before - I would have thought that the person was giving her cues ...and that does happen in some ways - just to sort thru what the messages are .... it was a lot of hard work for both.......but mostly because people were blown away and became very emotional.

I am sure I would have been slightly skeptical before .....but not now. I could not believe the intense energy coming from this woman.

I was wrung out emotionally when it was over. I want to meet with her but I am scared to go that deep emotionally....but I have to........ have know that since the first time I heard her name 6 month ago. Some how - I have had a deep emotional connection with her since then.

Quantum magic and Quantum entanglement once again......

### Gracelind — 2013-10-19T16:07:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychic-abilities.8501/post-98328>

About 10 years ago my primary care doc told me of her experience at a conference with Caroline Myss, who was then doing intuitive healing. Doc said all the others were crowding around Caroline, but she stayed back, observing. After all the flurry, Caroline came up to Doc, seeking her out of the crowd, and said, "You have an issue with your ovary, but it will turn out just fine." Doc was in the midst of exams that soon determined she did have a large ovarian cyst--that was benign. She hadn't told anyone about it, waiting for the results, first.

OTOH, I pass a small building along the highway to the mountains that has a purple neon sign that says, "Psychic." I wonder if she sits inside and knows that I'm not going to stop ... She is not compelling. But I do have several friends who have had experiences that impressed and touched them deeply. Some psychics do seem to have special gifts.

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-19T16:24:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychic-abilities.8501/post-98329>

Audrey

yes I've experienced this first hand. There's a great lady up north of DC - who does great readings. I've been to her several times. The last time I went to her - she asked me about some of my experiences which I've never told anyone about - freaked me out. but she assured me that those experiences were normal for those who read energy. growing up I had lots of dejavous - but it wasn't if you know what I mean, she told me I was seeing into my past lives, as well as future ones.

I can remember getting ready to leave for the christmas holidays from school, and I picked up my airplane ticket - and I had a bad feeling about my flight - couldn't tell you why but I felt like something was going to happen on that flight - enough so that I actually attempted to change my ticket - either way - I got on the flight and nothing happened. but on the flight back from christmas training, the military aircraft we were flying in had an inflight emergency. I was sleeping, and suddenly woke up, everyone else was either sleeping or studying, I realized the crew were working hard to solve the problem. We had already descended and were on final, when the pilot, aborted the landing, we went back up to altitude and circled for an hour and 1/2. I knew at that moment this is what my mind perceived when I picked up that ticket. Every one else was having a great time thought it was cool, One of my seat mates, looked at me and asked if I was sick - I told her no - but we've got a really BIG problem. She scoffed at me. then the captain of the Aircraft came on the communications system - and told us that we were in the process of dumping fuel (we were in a tanker aircraft) and we would be making an emergency landing b/c the landing gear wouldn't come down, even with the manual over ride. At that point everyone began to freak out. at that point, the boom operator, and the flight tech came down the isle, they both had this grim look on their face and they began to crawl down into hatches to actually see if they could activate the last manual override for the landing gear. it eventually worked - but it was one of those things, that when I get a feeling about something - I don't ignore it, EVER.

I really think all of this goes back to how you pick your friends etc. those people who reflect the same focus you do, are attracted to you. those that are not are repelled. I now no longer worry about friendships, and how they come and go. its part of how things work. you're called to people who reflect your thoughts on life, irregardless of their political or religious views. you sense they're in the right state.

I would know you Audrey, or Inger, or Cristi, or anyone else here on the forum w/o knowing what you look like b/c we have the same vibration.... KWIM? its the vibration that's Jack's talking about...

### veelansu — 2013-10-20T01:36:23+0300

True psychics take donations as they are only in it to share their gift. I'm always leary of those that charge exorbitant fees no matter how good they are. ### Hope — 2013-10-20T21:33:34+0300

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=98378)

Audrey,

wanted to just say...acceptance is one of the hardest things we will ever do. Real surrender, to stop fighting.....very hard. I am in love with Chinese Medicine, some of the things you learn there are wow. One major thing is this, acceptance...in fact, my teacher says,

All disease comes from resisting what is.

Of course 5K years ago there was no EMF etc but this is still a big piece, bc when we resist, nothing works well...starts with the thoughts and trickles down to the body since our body and mind are one and the same. a lot of healing can come from going inside, deep inside, and accepting. Of course, having support in any way - this class or whatever - is always great. But you don't NEED it... This is one major thing I am just learning, or starting to, as well. You have a great store of wisdom and healing right inside you.

you said right here, "all of you are X....and I am Y...." - right there is an opportunity to accept..yourself, where you are right now, with what life has given you. You are amazing. I encourage you to accept and celebrate all you are....I know I do. But even that doesn't matter. You are on your own path....you know it, deep down, if you are willing to look. Even in the deepest pain - we all have some - there can be some greater space and openness if you open to it. I am certain you will get there, in your own time and way.

Your heart always knows.....think less....feel more. Dr K always talks about intuition too...this is the same...follow your heart

Sending you much love ~ a fellow traveler and seeker.

### caroline — 2013-10-21T03:10:23+0300

### Hope — 2013-10-22T02:53:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychic-abilities.8501/post-98534>

Dear Audrey and Inger,

you are too sweet to ask how I am. I have been a bit absent from the forum, bc I have been trying to spend as much of my 'free' time outdoors, with a dear friend's dog and also decluttering my home....more than ever before. I can't tell you how much I have given away to create space and have less stuff in our town home....it became this huge burden - or I should say - the extra stuff in my house and the lack of beauty and vacant-cy was something I realized led to a lot of negativity in my brain...subconsciously, I would tell myself how much I hated this place. Not just because of the location (DC Metro, yuck) but the insides....and though I'm far from done, I feel so much better now about this place and otherwise too.

I am making good progress I think, love the magnetico and I think it plus the KI is really helping me kick more Hg than I ever have in the 4 years that I have been hoping to dump more metals. My adrenals are in the dumps but I am happy that my night and afternoon cortisol is now normal....all the things I'm doing are making a difference, I truly believe that and I am amazed at my desire to do what I shared with Audrey too...to go inside, to listen to my heart...it has so much to say when I give it the space and subtract distractions like things, people and thoughts that are not helpful in my journey....and I am learning how easy it is for me to want to flee into those things, or have until now. I now look forward to winter even more, more darkness, more time to rest..I've become so in love with early sleep and it is amazing the difference that it makes.

I still have not conceived a baby and though my hormones, I feel, are better (and also per my cycle, my temperatures, some of my labs), I still have a ways to go to dump more metals - and with it as Jack explained my sensitivity to nonnative EMF - but my big realization is that DH's swimmers are also not great and sadly he is not into sleeping early but I give him supplements and lots of fish which thankfully he loves.

I also just turned 40 and am working on the accepting of whatever 'fate' may be with me and having a family....and I am much more open to adoption. When I realize how deeply I love a dog that is not even mine (though of course only animals can ever love us so purely and unconditionally), and how much joy he brings me, deep in my heart, it is not so so hard to be OK with not having my own baby. I want to be even more open and OK with accepting whatever happens - even no children at all.....my greatest desire is to become as healthy as possible, to have the energy I need for all the things I want to do.....although I would love, still, for life to come through me, I don't know if it will. But I know my being a-live each day is a complete miracle and I am working on no longer comparing myself with other people who are skinnier, more successful, or whatever-er than me.

I think most of all, dumping the toxicities gives me a desire to look inside, to get to know myself in a way I never have as the last decade - when most people build great careers and families - has been mostly about survival, making it through each day...not thriving. And that is my great desire. To stop believing (as I am now) that my life must be hard because my life has been hard in the past and I must do whatever just to survive and to start (as I am too) to really believe that I do have a say in how I live, the kind of life I lead.....not a slave to the rat race. Keeping up with the Joneses'. But in a way that's right for me, or whatever I can get my DH to compromise on (working on moving out of this area still). I don't need a big freaking mansion with lots of stuff. I want freedom to eat great food, be and meet with like-minded people, and spend time in nature, and live there. I need very little STUFF and told my husband, don't give me any more presents....give me an experience, give me time, give me a trip out to nature. But not stuff to put on a shelf. I'm done with it.

So I too am way behind other people, when you compare to what people in 'the western world' do....but I'm OK with that now. I know deep in my heart that all my work will pay off and if I can delay or avoid getting cancer I am happy - as I am convinced with my family history and issues that I had to uncover myself over the years, if I did not, I am sure I would have gotten much sicker much younger.....so I am grateful and will no longer care when 'whoever' thinks I'm crazy.....for what I do and believe in. To each his own.

Of course I know nothing is guaranteed...I can drop dead tomorrow. I was sad when I heard of Candace Pert passing a couple weeks ago - she was only 67. But, she was amazing, her whole being and mind...and her heart just stopped. maybe bc she lived in MD, this EMF bath...I don't know. But she also died happy, of that I am certain...in her sleep. What a way to go.

Anyway I am rambling and must stop. I do need to update my journal. I just really want to keep working on this house...and finishing paperwork too....but I do think of you two so often when I'm in nature...imagining you're there with me, standing in a creek....or Emma saying hi. I was so happy, Inger, to see a picture of her and know for now she is still alive. I am sure she will die with great love for you in her heart.

Sending lots of love to you both from afar ~

### Jackie Nelson — 2019-07-02T08:36:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychic-abilities.8501/post-273604>

I've never been to a psychic but lately I've been having some absolutely profound experiences with manifesting . I have a science degree
and worked as a Clinical Nurse Specialist in the oh so WRONG conventional medical model. But my heart has always been drawn to art and if my mom
had not been so fearful (she didn't want me to be a starving artist), I would have followed that path. What is it about art that is related to the psychic experiences ... too hard for me to explain the details here. but it's profound. My entire world has changed in the past month and it's all because of art and belief and "mind stuff" ... we humans are truly mysterious and powerful. Tiny example ... may not seem like much, but I could give a dozen more: I painted a VERY bright, large and sparkling fridge magnet that said "Born to Win" .... and I love just repeating that phrase ... is there electrical energy emitted in saying this ? I think so. In any case, I've never won anything in my life, but a week after that magnet was put up on my fridge, I won something big. The rational / scientific person would say it was just coincidence, but I know it wasn't. I manifested it. And anyone whose beliefs are strong enough can do the same. This is the simplistic version of what's been happening to me. The rest is convoluted and impossible to explain. Life is AWESOME and oh so mysterious.

### caroline — 2019-07-03T06:31:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychic-abilities.8501/post-273633>

When my DH was in the hospital dying ....a young friend of his who was 9 yo. was also in the hospital for an infection.

She painted a beautiful picture for me of endless rainbows at the time he actually died. She had no knowledge until later that day.

I have had it framed and it is my most prized possession. I look at it every morning when I get up.

It is so powerful it takes my breathe away every single time I look at it ....and it has been 7 years.

Sometimes I tend to put the things that are very special to me away in a box. I don’t like a lot of clutter. I think to myself... I will file the memory in my brain.

But nothing is better then letting the color of the rainbow hit your eye and stimulate that memory.

I think it’s time to buy a frame and bring on the clutter... thanks for the nudge!

On a side note... when I was sick and didn’t realize it. I had a little boy in my classroom draw me a rainbow everyday for the whole school year. It was actually his signature. Definitely colored my world!

### HoneyChild — 2013-10-20T15:00:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-labs-please-help.8509/post-98403>

I'm by no means an expert on interpreting labs however here are a few of the things that jump out at me...

Bun Creatine ration 16 - anything over 15 is considered very bad, showing you have dehydration probably from EMFs
HDL 55 R >39 - in the optimal reset HDL is advised to be more than 60, might be another sign of dehydration
Triglycerides 68 R 0-149 - looks good, we want them to be less than 75
Glucose 85 R 65-99 - looks not bad, advised to be below 88

Also looks like you have thyroid problems and T4 and T3 are on the low side.

You can get more help by looking here: [http://www.jackkruse.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/Optimizing-Labs.pdf](http://www.jackkruse.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/Optimizing-Labs.pdf)

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-23T01:20:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-october-sleep-webinar-q-a.8524/post-98632>

[http://www.chemicalelements.com/groups/transition.html](http://www.chemicalelements.com/groups/transition.html)

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-23T01:21:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-october-sleep-webinar-q-a.8524/post-98634>

The interesting thing about transition metals is that their valence electrons, or the electrons they use to combine with other elements, are present in more than one shell. This is the reason why they often exhibit several common oxidation states. There are three noteworthy elements in the transition metals family. **These elements are iron, cobalt, and nickel, and they are the only elements known to produce a magnetic field.**

Now go back and listen to the September webinar.............

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-23T01:24:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-october-sleep-webinar-q-a.8524/post-98636>

And the valence electron thing..........that is big deal for how EMF interacts with these metals. This is what makes them special..........

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-23T01:27:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-october-sleep-webinar-q-a.8524/post-98637>

The transition metals are more electronegative than the main group metals, for example, and are therefore more likely to form covalent compounds.

When I teach you about the quantum cell.......like I mentioned last night during the Webinar Q & A .........this is going to be big.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-23T01:29:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-october-sleep-webinar-q-a.8524/post-98639>

Another difference between the main group metals and transition metals can be seen in the formulas of the compounds they form. The main group metals tend to form salts (such as NaCl, Mg3N2, and CaS) in which there are just enough negative ions to balance the charge on the positive ions. The transition metals form similar compounds \[such as FeCl3, HgI2, or Cd(OH)2\], but they are more likely than main group metals to form complexes, such as the FeCl4-, HgI42-, and Cd(OH)42- ions, that have an excess number of negative ions.

The negative ions thing: Huge for making a liquid crystalline semiconductor with water, a magnetic dipole.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-23T01:34:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-october-sleep-webinar-q-a.8524/post-98640>

A third difference between main group and transition-metal ions is the ease with which they **form stable compounds with neutral molecules, such as water** or ammonia. Salts of main group metal ions dissolve in water to form aqueous solutions.

H2O
NaCl(s) Na+(aq) + Cl-(aq)

When we let the water evaporate, we get back the original starting material, NaCl(s). **Salts of the transition-metal ions can display a very different behavior. _That behavior is why they are used with the proteins coded for by DNA to do some magical things............_**

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-23T01:38:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-october-sleep-webinar-q-a.8524/post-98641>

The relationship between the electron configurations of transition-metal elements and their ions is complex!!!!!!
​

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-23T01:40:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-october-sleep-webinar-q-a.8524/post-98642>

Remember all the things I told you about how energy is fractally organized in life from lowest to highest and it parallels atomic structure? And how it ties to coma?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-23T01:42:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-october-sleep-webinar-q-a.8524/post-98644>

****

Let's consider the chemistry of cobalt which forms complexes that contain either Co\[SUP\]2+\[/SUP\] or Co\[SUP\]3+\[/SUP\] ions.
The electron configuration of a neutral cobalt atom is written as follows.

Co: \[Ar\] 4_s_\[SUP\]2\[/SUP\] 3_d_\[SUP\]7\[/SUP\]​

The discussion of the relative energies of the atomic orbitals suggests that the 4_s_ orbital has a lower energy than the 3_d_orbitals. Thus, we might expect cobalt to lose electrons from the higher energy 3_d_ orbitals, but this is not what is observed. The Co\[SUP\]2+\[/SUP\] and Co\[SUP\]3+\[/SUP\] ions have the following electron configurations.

Co\[SUP\]2+\[/SUP\]: \[Ar\] 3_d_\[SUP\]7\[/SUP\]​

********

In general, electrons are removed from the valence-shell _s_ orbitals before they are removed from valence _d_ orbitals when transition metals are ionized.
​

********

Co\[SUP\]3+\[/SUP\]: \[Ar\] 3_d_\[SUP\]6\[/SUP\]​

\[SUP\]
EMF ionizes these metals!!!!!! Guess what that implies?

\[/SUP\]
​

****

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-october-sleep-webinar-q-a.8524/post-98645>

****

Most transition metals form more than one oxidation state.
​

****

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-23T01:47:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-october-sleep-webinar-q-a.8524/post-98647>

****

_Common Oxidation States of the First Series of Transition Metals_

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-23T01:49:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-october-sleep-webinar-q-a.8524/post-98649>

Consider the following reaction in which manganese is oxidized from the +2 to the +7 oxidation state.

Mn2+(aq) + 4 H2O(l) MnO4-(aq) + 8 H+(aq) + 5 e-
When the manganese atom is oxidized, it becomes more electronegative. In the +7 oxidation state, this atom is electronegative enough to react with water to form a covalent oxide, MnO4-.

It is useful to have a way of distinguishing between the charge on a transition-metal ion and the oxidation state of the transition metal. By convention, symbols such as Mn2+ refer to ions that carry a +2 charge. Symbols such as Mn(VII) are used to describe compounds in which manganese is in the +7 oxidation state.

Mn(VII) is not the only example of an oxidation state powerful enough to decompose water. As soon as Mn2+ is oxidized to Mn(IV), it reacts with water to form MnO2. A similar phenomenon can be seen in the chemistry of both vanadium and chromium. Vanadium exists in aqueous solutions as the V2+ ion. But once it is oxidized to the +4 or +5 oxidation state, it reacts with water to form the VO2+ or VO2+ ion. The Cr3+ ion can be found in aqueous solution. But once this ion is oxidized to Cr(VI), it reacts with water to form the CrO42- and Cr2O72- ions.

Treatment of cells with βOHB increased histone acetylation at the Foxo3a and Mt2 promoters, and both genes were activated by selective depletion of HDAC1 and HDAC2. Consistent with increased FOXO3A and MT2 activity, treatment of mice with βOHB conferred substantial protection against oxidative stress. ...Mitochondrial superoxide dismutase (Mn-SOD) and catalase are two other well-defined FOXO3A targets that contribute to its protective activity against oxidative stress. Protein immunoblotting of kidney tissue isolated from PBS- or βOHB-treated mice showed increased expression of FOXO3A, Mn-SOD, and catalase............Mitochondrial SOD all uses transition metals...........I spoke about them in the EE series already........EE4 I believe.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-24T02:46:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-october-sleep-webinar-q-a.8524/post-98797>

The simplest method to measure the electrical resistance of a sample of some material is to place it in an electrical circuit in series with a current source I and measure the resulting voltage V across the sample. The resistance of the sample is given by Ohm's law as R = V / I. If the voltage is zero, this means that the resistance is zero.
**Superconductors are also able to maintain a current with no applied voltage whatsoever, a property exploited in superconducting electromagnets such as those found in MRI machines.**

So superconductors can eliminate the electric field of an EMF wave and magnify the magnetic effect......to make a magnetic current that has no resistance and no voltage..........this is a way to power a zero entropy system. (Future issue for EMF mitigation)

All superconductors form quantum vortices too folks.........see how this links to sleep now?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-24T17:55:24+0300

Ketogenesis inhibits the mTORC1 pathway. Turns out that FOXO3A induces the mTORC1 inhibitor TSC1. FOXO3A regulates glycolysis via transcriptional control of tumour suppressor TSC1. The pathway goes beta-hydroxybutyrate --> FOXO3A --> TSC1 --> mTORC1 inhibition. This means TSC1 would increase autophagy. This happens best in sleep when someone is eating a high fat moderate protein diet. Since mTORC1 blocks ketogenesis by inhibiting PPAR alpha, this means that FOXO3A induces PPAR alpha and ketogenesis. So the complete pathway goes like this.
Beta-hydroxybutyrate --> FOXO3A --> TSC1 --> mTORC1 inhibition --> PPAR alpha --> ketogenic genes.
All life transfers energy using chemical redox reactions. Transition metals are used in all biologic proteins that perform redox reactions because of their electron shells. This makes them all special. This means they involve the transfer of electrons from one substance as a donor to another which is an electron acceptor. They do this based upon the math called the reductive potential. (We spoke about the main reducing element in biochemistry in EMF 4: NADPH made from the Pentose Phosphate Pathway (PPP). The reduction potential is based upon the affinity for electrons compared to the affinity of hydrogen for electrons as a baseline. It begs the question then, where do the electrons come from to power these reactions? The short answer is, the sun.

The sun splits water from its molecular chemical composition of H2O into 2 Hydrogen + 2 electrons + oxygen. These electrons are transferred to plants and food stuffs via photosynthesis on the back of the water molecule. This is why plants, trees, and bacteria need water to grow. Animals have a more complex way of doing it, because as more complex life forms they require more energy to power their newer evolutionary designed systems. Animals transfer the electrons from food to their mitochondria using water in a cell as the transfer mechanism. In this process they use proteins **loaded with iron** to do it using quantum tunneling.

When EMF is excessive, increases of ROS are leached from the mitochondrial membrane, the gut, gut bacteria and lung barriers, the blood brain barrier all become leaky to inflammatory products. Normal physiologic ROS leak in mitochondrial are vital in optimizing ATP synthesis by stimulating mitochondrial biogenesis. When you leak more you become energy inefficient in processing electrons across your inner mitochondrial membrane via quantum tunneling. In Nick Lane’s book, Power, Sex, Suicide, this view point is supported by the fact that antioxidants lower not only ROS leak but also mtDNA copy number and ATP synthesis levels. Superoxide (O2Â·−) reacts avidly with vascular NOÂ· to form peroxynitrite (ONOO−). The cofactor tetrahydro-L-biopterin (BH4) is highly sensitive to oxidation by ONOO−. Tetrahydrobiopterin is a naturally occurring essential cofactor of the three aromatic amino acid hydroxylase enzymes, used in the degradation of amino acid phenylalanine and in the biosynthesis of the neurotransmitters serotonin (5-hydroxytryptamine, 5-HT), melatonin, dopamine, norepinephrine (noradrenaline), epinephrine (adrenaline), and is a cofactor for the production of nitric oxide (NO) by the nitric oxide synthetase.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-24T18:17:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-october-sleep-webinar-q-a.8524/post-98871>

Nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide phosphate (NADPH) oxidases represent major sources of this reactive oxygen species. NADPH oxidase generates superoxide by transferring electrons from NADPH inside the cell across the membrane and coupling these to molecular oxygen to produce superoxide anion, a reactive free-radical. Superoxide can be produced in phagosomes, which contain ingested bacteria and fungi, or it can be produced outside of the cell. In a phagosome, superoxide can spontaneously form hydrogen peroxide that will undergo further reactions to generate reactive oxygen species (ROS). Superoxide kills bacteria and fungi by mechanisms that are not yet fully understood, (but we know it kills them) but may inactivate critical metabolic enzymes, initiate lipid peroxidation, and **liberates redox-active iron,** which allows the generation of indiscriminate oxidants such as the **hydroxyl radical.**

KEY GOLD MEMBER MOMENT: WHAT DID I TELL YOU IN THE SEPTEMBER WEBINAR ABOUT THE HYDROXYL FREE RADICAL? WHAT DOES IT SPECIFICALLY DO IN THE EPIGENETIC CASCADE? IT SILENCES THE SILENCER..........That means it DIRECTLY CHANGES HOW DNA is expressed..................HOLY MOLY.

Superoxide anion is particularly important as the product of the one-electron reduction of dioxygen O2, which occurs widely in nature. Both dioxygen and superoxide ion are free radicals that exhibit paramagnetism.

Key Point: The generation of superoxide requires NADPH and thus ties the Pentose Phosphate Pathway (PPP) to this reducing co-factor to mitigate the risks of EMF. This means that a high fat diet is the preferred diet to mitigate an excessive man made EMF risk to biology. This means that what we have believed to constitute a healthy diet can no longer be true in an altered field.

The First Law is usually written as the equation

ΔU = Q + W

The more I read about all these different coenzyme forms of niacin the more confused I seem to get. I just want to make absolutely sure...

NADPH can be made if you're using complex I in electron transport chain but in doing so makes superoxide (v. bad)
NADPH can be made via PPP but doesn't make superoxide (v. good)
If you make lots of NADPH because of complex I then you shut down the PPP because you're sending a message to say the PPP isn't needed (negative feedback loop).

"The reactants necessary for this enzyme mechanism \[isocitrate dehydrogenase\] to work are isocitrate, NAD+/NADP+, and Mn2+ or Mg2+. The products of the reaction are alpha-ketoglutarate, carbon dioxide, and NADH + H+/NADPH + H+."
[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Isocitrate\_dehydrogenase](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Isocitrate_dehydrogenase)

It looks like pyruvate, malate, a-ketoglutarate, isocitrate and 3-hydroxybutyrate feed complex I whereas succinate feeds complex II. (Biochemistry, Part 1 By Debajyoti Das)

Superconductors are also able to maintain a current with no applied voltage whatsoever, a property exploited in superconducting electromagnets such as those found in MRI machines. Experiments have demonstrated that currents in superconducting coils can persist for years without any measurable degradation. Experimental evidence points to a current lifetime of at least 100,000 years. Theoretical estimates for the lifetime of a persistent current can exceed the estimated lifetime of the universe, depending on the wire geometry and the temperature.

In a normal conductor, an electric current may be visualized as a fluid of electrons moving across a heavy ionic lattice. The electrons are constantly colliding with the ions in the lattice, and during each collision some of the energy carried by the current is absorbed by the lattice and converted into heat, which is essentially the vibrational kinetic energy of the lattice ions. As a result, the energy carried by the current is constantly being dissipated. This is the phenomenon of electrical resistance in an electric wire.
The situation is different in a conventional superconductor or a non conventional quasi liquid crystal semiconductor in a living cell. In a conventional superconductor, the electronic fluid cannot be resolved into individual electrons. Instead, it consists of bound pairs of electrons known as Cooper pairs. This pairing is caused by an attractive force between electrons from the exchange of phonons. Why? electrons are fermions and photons are bosons so they attract one another instead of invoking the quantum exclusion principle. Due to the laws of quantum mechanics, the energy spectrum of the "Cooper pair fluid" possesses an energy gap, meaning there is a minimum amount of energy ΔE that must be supplied in order to excite the fluid. This energy can come from food electrons or from the power of the sun light that is splitting electrons from water in every cell's cytoarchitecture to liberate electrons to be added to the semiconducting liquid crystals of water. This energy can be banked and used later when it is need to overcome entropy in the cell. There are no QED or physics laws on energy storage Randy. Therefore, if ΔE is larger than the thermal energy of the lattice, (in this case the electric wire as an example) given by kT, where k is Boltzmann's constant and T is the temperature, the fluid will not be scattered by the lattice. The Cooper pair fluid is thus acts as a superfluid filled with energy, meaning it can flow without energy dissipation. This is precisely how a Bose Einstein condensation occurs.........now go re read what I wrote in EE6 and you will see how I came up with my theory on how the brain really works..........It is a metastable QED machine that uses the two main maxwell demons to get around the second law........photons coherence and BEC condensation of the liquid crystalline lattice employing a superconducting fluid of electrons in water.......suspended in this water is the collagen cytoarchitecture that is in contact with every dimension of the living matrix. This is how coherence in biology happens........according to me.

In 69 a book came out called Energy Flow in Biology by biophysicist Harold Morowitz. I read it in residency when I was learning about how energy flows are tied to natural cycle formation. This is self assembling. This is how the thermohaline currents work, this is how the carbon cycle works, the nitrogen cycle, and interestingly it is how circadian biology works too. Water density in CSF is linked to convection and conductive currents in the brain and it these energy flow dictate how biochemistry is carried out. These so-called Barnard Rayleigh convection cells arise as the low density, lighter water at the bottom of the ocean or flask repeatedly rises to the top while the denser water at the top sinks to the bottom, and so on in a cyclic manner when energy is added in any fashion.

I got this insight from Morowitz. Morowitz’s idea was that energy flow leads to space-time structures, in particular, to cycles.

Before phase transition, the individual water molecules move around randomly and aimlessly without any order or pattern. At phase transition, however, they begin to move collectively until all at once coherently as the CSF would in our subarachnoid space. Then I had an interesting thought.......

What happens when biochemical reactions are involved in this cycling process? Does biochemical cycling also take place as the result of energy flow?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-27T00:45:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-october-sleep-webinar-q-a.8524/post-99108>

Here I found the theorem of biochemical cycling.......If energy flows into any system so that a steady state is maintained with a net flow of material around the system from A to B to C, the principle of microscopic reversibility will no longer hold: there will not be a detailed balance in every step. A cycle is created. This is how biochemistry actually happens.............

Now consider EMF in the same idea pattern:

If the equilibrium system is now irradiated by a constant flux of electromagnetic radiation such that there is net absorption of photons by the system, a steady state will be reached at which there is a flow of heat out into the reservoir (energy sink) equal to the flux of electromagnetic energy into the system. At this point, there will be a different set of occupation numbers and transition probabilities, fi′ and t’ij for there are now both radiation induced transitions as well as the random thermally induced transitions characteristic of the previous equilibrium state.

This means that for some pairs of states i and j, For, if the equality holds in all pairs of states, it must imply that for every transition involving the absorption of photons, a reverse transition will take place involving the radiation of the photon such that there is **no net absorption of electromagnetic radiation** by the system. This contradicts our original assumption that there is absorption of radiant energy (see previous paragraph), so now we must conclude that the equality of forward and reverse transitions does not hold for some pairs of states. This is why life use's EMF to animate itself. It is capable of creating a metastable protoplasm where straight biochemistry can not. Remember electromagnetic forces are among the strongest in nature and they are present in enormous volumes from stars.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-27T00:54:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-october-sleep-webinar-q-a.8524/post-99110>

In steady state systems, the flow of energy through the system from a source to a sink will lead to at least one cycle in the system. The formation of steady state cycles has the important thermo-dynamic consequence that, despite large fluxes of materials and chemical transformations in the system, the net change in entropy of the system is zero, because entropy is a state function.

A return to the same state will always entail no net entropy change. Hence we can see where a zero entropy system can be crafted in a metastable state. Can we not? Of course, the compensatory change in entropy of the surroundings could be greater than zero, but entropy does not accumulate in the system, provided that the cycles are perfect in the sense that exactly the same state is reproduced, which is definitely not the case in real life. Or is it?

This is why mammals evolved heat loss........they are returning entropy to the system in order to create Schrodinger's idea of a zero entropy state. .......I think this is precisely what we see with respect to circadian biology in us. It is hugely important. Light and dark are invariant and tied to time because they are constructed of an EMF.

We should also expect this sort of a system to find all the processes are thermodynamically linked, or coupled together in a symmetrical way. This is precisely how metabolism is organized in a cell.

No?

Guess what else I found out in 2000? The Onsager reciprocity relation. This relation implies that equilibrium is never maintained in a cyclic process at the molecular level. This implies that quantum probabilities are infinite in a "metastable" state. Are you beginning to see how I created EE 6 now?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-27T01:05:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-october-sleep-webinar-q-a.8524/post-99114>

Sounds radical until you consider how the second law was formulated.

Thermodynamic equilibrium may nevertheless have within it balanced flows of energy and material, as long as the flows are linearly proportional to the forces.

This should not be surprising in retrospect, as the laws of thermodynamics were themselves a generalization of macroscopic phenomena, and do not depend on the knowledge of what is happening in detail at the molecular level. The molecular level was not even discovered when the law was formed Randy!!!!

What this means is that many locally non-equilibrium situations involving cyclic flow processes, like circadian biology and biochemistry, can be described as near approximations to the equilibrium situation, more specifically, as fluctuations occurring within a larger, encompassing equilibrium system, so long as the flows and forces which tend to restore the equilibrium are linearly proportional to one another. In this way, the second law is never violated by life.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-oil-sends-anxiety-through-the-roof.8528/post-98576>

Hi, glad to discover this forum which appears to be full of extremely advanced thinking on these topics. Anyway, I'm following the leaky guy protocol, as a result of extreme anxiety which arose after a car accident, resulting in gall bladder removal. I wanted to include fish oils and chose a very high level EPA brand, but it sent my anxiety through the roof. I had to come off it immediately. Can anyone shed light on the mechanisms by which EPA could trigger such extreme uneasy after consumption?

### japhyryder — 2013-10-22T19:47:03+0300

Over these last 15 years I've experimented relentlessly to find what has changed in my brain and it is something major. Depression, OCD, chronic fatigue, anxiety etc have all manifested since that fateful day. This forum genuinely feels home to some of the most erudite minds I've encountered on the subject of diet and mental health, so I'm hoping someone can elaborate. I can't tolerate kelp or taurine either incidentally, but fish oil virtually made me psychotic.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-oil-sends-anxiety-through-the-roof.8528/post-98725>

I have salicylate sensitivity issues, and I can't take any capsules (cellulose, gelatins), I always open them up . Also fish oils are high in _amines_ I believe, and cause anxiety and depression in some people. I know first hand, with things like tomato sauce, and things that are close to salicylates chemically, like any benzo type things, give me anxiety quick. I also have much worse issues than anxiety if I have salicylates to include very bad breathing issues at sleep onset if I have them. I avoid them like the plague, except for Coconut oil, I'm able to tolerate that , I started in small doses, and now am eating it all day, but I can't really add in too many other salicylates yet. But I'm exceptionally better than I was 2 years ago with all of this. Avoidance and some brain retraining, along with staying paleoish, doing CT, avoiding emfs, tweaking my circadian cycle, and doing everything I can to lower inflammation has made it a lot better. I haven't had anxiety in 3 years now, I have however felt it coming on , on a very great occasion, but I know now that it is definitely something I encountered, or ate.

### JanSz — 2013-10-23T23:37:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-oil-sends-anxiety-through-the-roof.8528/post-98769>

> [japhyryder said:](/goto/post?id=98576)
>
> Hi, glad to discover this forum which appears to be full of extremely advanced thinking on these topics. Anyway, I'm following the leaky guy protocol, as a result of extreme anxiety which arose after a car accident, resulting in gall bladder removal. I wanted to include fish oils and chose a **very high level EPA brand**, but it sent my anxiety through the roof. I had to come off it immediately. Can anyone shed light on the mechanisms by which EPA could trigger such extreme uneasy after consumption?

Fish and krill oils should be avoided for number of reasons.

EPA is a major reason why they should be avoided.

One of the main reasons why dr Kruse recommends seafood is their DHA content.

The DHA content is important but even more important is that when in seafood it comes with halo.

I can't explain the halo except that I think about it as energy, electrons.
Similar situation as with water in Australia and New Zealand, lacking electrons due to ozone hole over them.
Then dr Kruse recommends for Aussies to use imported spring/mineral/RO water or RO water made from local sea water.

One of the way to judge ones situation in regard to Fatty Acids is to do at Mayo Clinic or LabCorp blood analysis.
Ask doc for script:.

Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

[http://www.mayomedicallaboratories.com/test-catalog/Clinical+and+Interpretive/82042](http://www.mayomedicallaboratories.com/test-catalog/Clinical+and+Interpretive/82042)

There are on the market DHA supplements. Remember no halo, less energy.

They are either DHA only (vegetable source) or DHA with minimal amount of EPA.

One is DHA from Nutricology

To large EPA content suppresses AA (omega6), valuable fat.

Other than excess of EPA some people have low AA because they have a low GLA

omega6
LA--->GLA---->DGLA-----AA

Supplemental GLA comes from three sources
rape seed
Evening Primrose
Black Currant

Evening Primrose is popular and good supplement
rape seed should be avoided

I am currently doing experiment.
Originally I had lowish GLA & DGLA
With supplementation I got GLA in right place
but DGLA is still low.
I have seen article that suggested Black Currant, will see if that will work.

/////////////

# Thread: Gauging interest for a weekend with Jack
