### tellmisty — 2013-10-22T23:14:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gauging-interest-for-a-weekend-with-jack.8532/post-98607>

Hi y'all,

I just had a convo with Jack about hosting a weekend event for our community called "Get Optimal Weekend" (or GO weekend). If we did it, it would be a Friday registration, Friday night talk with Jack and a cocktail party. Saturday would be a massive lab hacking day ... we'll have slots for a few of you to sign up to send your labs and health history ahead of time, and publicly for the entire group as a kind of "demonstration," Jack would spend the day biohacking labs, offer suggestions on things you can try with your doctor, protocols of his that you might try, you can ask him questions and we can all learn together. There would be a LOT of Q&A on Saturday (questions welcome from anyone, not just those having their labs hacked).

And on Sunday, we'll have an opening session together to close the event, and there will be a few slots available for private consults with Jack (for an extra fee).

Based on all the feedback I've gotten from you guys over the last 18 months, it seems that doing an event like this is something you guys want. Jack isn't sure if there is enough interest for us to put in the work.

SO. We need to hear from you. Please chime in if you would be interested in coming out to an event like this ... if we do it, it would probably be in February 2014, maybe around Mardi Gras time in New Orleans. Cost would probably be $499 for the weekend for $199 for Platinum Klub members, Gold Klub members, $699 for Silver Klub, $999 for Bronze Klub. We want to keep the event small and intimate for the first time, so we'll probably limit to 25-40 people.

GO weekend will live or die by the responses to this thread. ;-) (So nudge your friends to chime in if this is something you want!)

### tellmisty — 2013-10-23T01:59:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gauging-interest-for-a-weekend-with-jack.8532/post-98653>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=98615)
>
> This sounds like such a great opportunity ....I would love it and would work hard to make it happen.
>
> Misty - I am not clear on the pricing?...

Pricing was clarified in a later post.  Basically, Gold Klub and Platinum Klub members get the best price (as per always).

> [ATL\_Paleo said:](/goto/post?id=98621)
>
> Trying to find motel rooms in New Orleans around Mardi Gras could be a great challenge .... are they available ??? and at what cost ???
>
> I have a tentative interest ... but not during Mardi Gras.

We are looking at the weekend BEFORE Mardi Gras. We chose NOLA instead of a more remote location because we wanted it to be easy for people to fly in (without being too expensive), we are looking at venues that are close to Farmer's Markets and Whole Foods (so the food part will be convenient), and yes, we will negotiate rates on rooms wherever we choose. We are getting pricing together now on venues and rooms.

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=98643)
>
> ^THIS^ I could probably swing the whole family down if things worked out.. maybe even a drive in the car... and maybe even talk my parents to come down at the same time...

This idea is good in theory, but choosing a place that is far from the airport adds to people's cost if they are flying in. And choosing smaller airports make flights more expensive. So trying to put this together in a way that will be affordable for everyone ... off-season flights (cheaper), off-event weekend (cheaper), close to airport (cuts out driving cost and potentially car rental costs).

Open to all ideas and thoughts at this point ... so keep sharing them!!!

A girl on our team is an event planner, and she's already been pricing out venues. We're trying to negotiate double rooms in the $100-120 range now; fingers crossed on that! We'll need 20 people minimum to be able to pull the event off. 40-50 is the sweet spot for our first one ...

PS - Flights were quite "average" for this weekend when we checked. Not too expensive! As with all things travel, the sooner you book, the better rates you get.

### veelansu — 2013-10-24T20:43:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gauging-interest-for-a-weekend-with-jack.8532/post-98898>

The three airports in the area are Pensacola, FL (PNS), Gulfport/Biloxi, MS (GPT) and Mobile, AL (MOB). Mobile is central to either Mississippi or Florida but it is the more pricey one when it comes to tickets. Gulfport tends to have pricing about $100 less than Mobile depending on the date (US Flights). Pensacola is sometimes more and sometimes significantly less than either. If we decide to do a place other than NOLA, these are the three that one would look at to travel into. NOLA is cheaper than all three. Sometimes when traveling to Mob, I just fly into NOLA and drive 1 1/2 hours because the cost is that much better. Something to think about.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-10-24T21:09:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gauging-interest-for-a-weekend-with-jack.8532/post-98899>

One possibility in NOLA. [http://www.starwoodhotels.com/fourpoints/property/area/map.html?propertyID=1357](http://www.starwoodhotels.com/fourpoints/property/area/map.html?propertyID=1357)

Rooms from $ 89 for the weekend of Feb 21 to Feb 23, 2014 .... conference rooms available .... free hotel shuttle from MSY airport which is 3 miles away .... just off I-10 so the Mardi Gras crowds should be minimal for the parades scheduled that weekend.

If the weekend is in NOLA, the part I like the best is what seems to be a nice park across the street ..... not the ocean, but grounding, etc. is possible.

### RGP — 2013-10-25T14:48:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gauging-interest-for-a-weekend-with-jack.8532/post-99002>

We don't have the disposable income for the other levels of membership. I for one could make this happen if the $800 I am saving for attendance is used for travel expenses.

What is the true intent of this get together: to meet and greet and help as many people as possible, or is this another revenue stream? The attendance will be greater if it is available to the masses. For those of you who are old enough, remember when the VW bug hit the American market.

### cantweight — 2013-10-25T15:43:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gauging-interest-for-a-weekend-with-jack.8532/post-99007>

### tellmisty — 2013-10-25T15:54:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gauging-interest-for-a-weekend-with-jack.8532/post-99009>

> [RGP said:](/goto/post?id=99002)
>
> Why don't you gauge the interest if all members were charged $199? There is a reason many or most of the members are bronze club members. We don't have the disposable income for the other levels of membership. I for one could make this happen if the $800 I am saving for attendance is used for travel expenses.
>
> What is the true intent of this get together: to meet and greet and help as many people as possible, or is this another revenue stream? The attendance will be greater if it is available to the masses. For those of you who are old enough, remember when the VW bug hit the American market.
>
> Just a thought.

Putting an event like this on takes money, plain and simple. There is a lot of time that goes into planning, and there are a lot of expenses for the event in general. No one works for free ... it all has to be budgeted. And Jack's time is very valuable, too ... if he's blocking off his schedule to do an event, I have to make sure that there's compensation for him to make it worthwhile. I can't make the math work at $199, plain and simple. As the event grows and we get sponsors for it, maybe that price point will be more feasible.

The event fee covers your pass into the event. Food, hotel and travel are up to each guest (that's just frankly too much for us to plan for each person).

I understand that not everyone can do it, but for those who've been clamoring for it, I'm trying to deliver! Jack gives away TONS to members of this community who never spend a dime, for which we can all be grateful.

### Hope — 2013-10-25T16:47:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gauging-interest-for-a-weekend-with-jack.8532/post-99012>

Yes, this stuff is very expensive and SO much work to put together....ugh. I admire you for being willing to take it on Misty!

My big question would be if there is really time to do everyone's HH and give input on every single person that wants it...if there are 25 people and we have all day on Saturday = 8 hours, that gives 19.2 minutes a person and the time would have to be very strict. I think managing expectations is key too so that people aren't disappointed. It was very hard when I got all my work done for a consult and then....boom.

And I think having people get their own hotels or lodging is perfect too - there are always discounts to be had with enough notice, and there is airbnb and Craigslist. People could get together and rent a large home with several bedrooms and kitchens and not have to pay more than $50 a night maybe.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-10-25T18:01:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gauging-interest-for-a-weekend-with-jack.8532/post-99019>

To date, the only benefits that have been posted are the chance to meet Jack (very nice), the opportunity to meet other Kruisers in person, and the opportunity to have a Consult w/ Jack (for an so far undisclosed additional fee).

After reading your post and thinking, I wonder aloud how Misty and Jack will be able to provide value to match the cost of $ 499 to $ 899 plus travel and lodging. I would love to meet Jack, but I wonder if that is worth about $ 1,000 to me.

Your comment that really resonated w/ me was TIME. I agree that having 25 or more people for just a weekend will probably not be enough time to accomplish things that most people would want to do.

I guess the next step is for Misty and team to post an agenda ...... so we can evaulate if our personal goals are likely to be achieve at a GO weekend.

# Thread: Will this really work for me?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-this-really-work-for-me.8536/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### GoldFishie — 2013-10-23T02:18:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-this-really-work-for-me.8536/post-98663>

Hello all. My name is Jan (age 61) and I'm from Atlanta. I have many years of a crapped out PVN and have exhausted the best alternative help that I can access. So here I land and am considering 'taking the plunge' but since my diet is so extremely limited, I'm wondering if it will work for me.

I have started to assiduously follow no lights/electronics after sundown, grounding for at least an hour a day, all the EMF stuff - had SmartMeter removed, etc., sleep in completely dark BR, drink 2 gallons RO water/day, eat within 30 minutes of waking up and then every 2.5-3 hours throughout the day. Hammer DHA/fish oils, curcumin, etc. From all appearances AI although never positive on TPO. Stage 3 kidney disease. Was on steroids from age 7-17. Tried to take Tirosint and for about 3 weeks life was wonderful. Then bam I just got plain pissy and could not tolerate it. Can't handle even an entsy tiny amount of DHEA cream. I pretty much go around in first gear or flat on my back. Up twice during the night to urinate, have to drink during the night, sleep on average 7 hours. Genesis 1:2 bowels (without form and void).

5'10" 145#

As to diet ~ I read what Dr. Kruse recommends. I have never had an oyster in my life, I am allergic to shellfish, have never been an organ meat eater, fish makes me hyper. Allergic to eggs, dairy & coconut. I hammer olive oil. So there goes all of the primo foods he suggests. He says no fowl and that is the staple of my diet, plus grass feed beef. Beef tallow nauseates me. I can handle duck fat. I do eat a small amount of either sweet potato, winter squash or apple with a meal. Tons of vegetables. Obviously cannot do BAB. Have mastered kraut but now cannot handle any salt. So there it sits . . . If I eat any salt my arms go numb at night. Can't handle water keifer or kombucha.

I have a mesolimbic windup but am safe to be on the playground as my frontal lobe is intact.

You know, I'd like to be special in other ways in life!

I have really resisted going this route especially with the recommended CT because I'm frickin freezing all of the time. Face I would be willing to start with.

I have access to any and all labs no problem. It's just the diet I'm concerned about.

Well, the sun is setting so I must disappear. Looking forward to some encouragement from ya'll in the a.m.!

~jan

### caroline — 2013-10-23T02:40:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-this-really-work-for-me.8536/post-98666>

Welcome Gold! Sounds like you have made a pretty good start.

Why are you eating so often? A lot of folks here are very limited with their diet and they manage.

Have you read Jack's book? Listened to his podcasts? The webinars and follow up Q&A are pure gold. When you read a blog make very sure that you read all the comments too. Dr. K. Answers all questions at the end of a blog.

Have you read the easy start guides on the home page?

Please start a journal in the journal section so Dr. Kruse can get some idea about you - he needs context.

This is doable ....however ... It will take time. Yoy didn't get broken overnite and this isn't a linear process ....but if you are committed and 100% all in - 6 months from now and a year from now you won't recognize yourself...

Tell us a little bit more about your life, do you have children? If so - how were the pregnancies?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-23T04:41:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/will-this-really-work-for-me.8536/post-98694>

1.My name is Jan (age 61) and I'm from Atlanta. I have many years of a crapped out PVN (Can you say EMF anyone?)
2.As to diet ~ I read what Dr. Kruse recommends. I have never had an oyster in my life, I am allergic to shellfish, have never been an organ meat eater, fish makes me hyper. Allergic to eggs, dairy & coconut. I hammer olive oil. So there goes all of the primo foods he suggests. He says no fowl and that is the staple of my diet, plus grass feed beef. Beef tallow nauseates me. I can handle duck fat. I do eat a small amount of either sweet potato, winter squash or apple with a meal. Tons of vegetables. Obviously cannot do BAB. Have mastered kraut but now cannot handle any salt. So there it sits . . . If I eat any salt my arms go numb at night. Can't handle water keifer or kombucha. (No wonder you are struggling.......no DC current)

Key take home: RO water, ketogenic diet, Read the quantum electron blog, hormone 101, 102, EE7. The biggie from EE 7:Why do humans need iodine at its core compared to its nearest ancestor the chimps?

It turns our sufficient dietary iodine is crucial for proper ketogenesis in our liver and in our brain for full immune and neural development. Ketogenesis is needed to make a hominid brain at a foundational level as you learned in Energy and Epigenetics 1 blog. Iodine however, does a lot more to humans than most realize.

IODINE, ESTROGEN, and KETOSIS: THE WAKE UP CALL FOR MENOPAUSAL WOMEN

Iodine is the link back to autoimmunity and the neurogenesis connection of the MHC 1 gene. Iodine absorption falls in the human gut when estrogen levels rise from any cause. This helps explain why women have much higher rates of autoimmune diseases like Multiple Sclerosis and hypothyroidism than men. It also explains why women have less myelin formation than men in adulthood. Remember from Energy and Epigenetics 1 we must be ketotic to make myelin in humans. Women have less iodine absorption by design. Women have higher estrogen levels to bear children. Lower levels of myelination allow women to be “more sensitive” to environmental triggers to pass that information to their offspring’s DNA. Myelination, also happens to be a proxy for mammalian regeneration. This was proven by Robert O. Becker. This now explains why women also make T2 thyroid hormone from their ovaries and breasts. This helps them offset their decrease ability to absorb iodine from their guts.

BIOLOGY GEEKS: Until recent years, T2, because of its very low affinity for thyroid hormone receptors (THR), was considered an inactive metabolite of thyroid hormones. However, several recent studies indicate that T2 is more important than originally thought. In fact, T2 is necessary for production of the deiodinase enzyme that converts the less active T4 into the potent T3 in the body. Early studies on diiodothyronine revealed its ability to stimulate cellular/mitochondrial respiration during the activation of the Pentose phosphate fat burning pathway by a receptor-independent pathway. Mitochondrial and energy-releasing mechanisms seem to be major targets of T2, although outside the mitochondria T2 also has effects on carriers, ion-exchangers, and enzymes.

Significant increases in the liver actions of glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase and malic enzyme were found in studies cited below. These enzymes are
necessary for fat metabolism and liberation of energy in the form of beta oxidation. T2 exhibits significant increases in Growth Hormone release from the pituitary have been found in studies. Both T2 and T3 increased Growth Hormone release by 5-fold. This makes sense when you consider women need growth hormone to stimulate their breast and ovarian tissue for fertility and reproduction.

NON GEEKS: In women, iodine is also critical in making breast milk, tears, and saliva. The higher your estrogen level, or the lower your SHBG level, the more likely your eyes, mouth, skin, and vagina will be dry. You also won’t make a lot of breast milk to feed a child. Your ability to sweat will also be altered.

I personally believe this is why women go through menopause now. It is because of their evolutionary design. No one seems to have a clue why menopause exists. I think I do. Women need to lower their estrogen levels as they age, to reclaim their total body iodine levels, to help them have a longer lifespan as they age by increasing their ability to myelinate to increase their regenerative DC current by increasing their iodine absorption. This also helps explain why diabetic women have a higher incidence of peripheral neuropathy than men do. They have less myelin, so any further decline in iodine assimilation impairs ketogenesis to regenerate myelin and diminishes their ability to heal and regenerate. This is where the decreased immunity seen in diabetes rears its ugly head for those with metabolic syndrome.

This also helps explain why women in menopause get hot flashes and night sweats. Iodine stimulates uncoupling proteins and it stimulates the sweat glands. When they had their menstrual period, they did not have the stimulatory effect of iodine, but now without their cycle they do, rather suddenly. It is not from a lack of estrogen, as most physicians believe, it is from more iodine in their bodies. Sweating is a new evolutionary design in hominids. Primates do not have the sweat gland we do. It is a change to the mammalian body plan unique to humans. This was done to be able to cool our bodies down to save energy, because they work better cooled down because it increases semiconducting currents. Primates use vitamin C as an endogenous antioxidant but humans evolved to use iodine as their peripheral antioxidant. We have large brains so we have a lot more semiconductors to cool everywhere on our body. Iodine also helps lower the oxidation of DHA in synapses in humans. This is why the brain has its own thyroid hormone control system because we have way more semiconduction circuits in our brain. When we lose our iodine function in the brain we lose the ability to offset some of the inflammatory cytokines in the brain circuits. In the frontal lobes this causes ADHD or depression and in the leptin receptors it causes an inability to sense energy balance and leads to obesity. When humans become energy inefficient they usually gain weight, as I laid out in EMF 2 and recently in Energy and Epigenetics 4, using Kleiber’s law.

Sweating is another buffer that we use to become more energy efficient, before we need to expand out fat mass to have the same effect. This is why women gain fat mass in menopause too. It happens because they are less energy efficient because of their loss of progesterone and prolactin. This reflex sweating, seen in humans, is done to cool down women’s new found rediscovery of efficient semiconduction, as their estrogen levels fall in menopause. Once they acclimate to their new increased iodine absorption, their symptoms resolve because they adapt by increasing their myelination and their DC current improves. Many menopausal women, get placed upon estrogen and sometimes their symptoms of hot flashes goes away, but so does their ability to reclaim iodine to myelinate. This implies they can alter their immune balance as they age. Iodine happens to increase neurogenesis in humans, so when iodine is low cognitive haze is also a result. The brain has its own thyroid hormone system to control neurogenesis even if the body stores are low. This is an example of how the brain controls energy partitioning for itself over vegetative systems in the thyroid gland. This patterns what we see in atoms too.

So if you get placed upon estrogen, or happen to be estrogen dominant for any reason at all, male or female, you may get cognitive haze. Iodine increases our ability to become ketotic to myelinate and regenerate our immune system and our brain because of the MHC 1 evolutionary connection. If you do not eat a ketotic diet when these changes happen these benefits will be hidden from you. For most of the blogosphere they remain a mystery.

# Thread: British cardiologist is getting the message, are you?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/british-cardiologist-is-getting-the-message-are-you.8551/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-24T17:31:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/british-cardiologist-is-getting-the-message-are-you.8551/post-98857>

[http://www.latimes.com/science/scie...ed-fat-20131022,0,2193813.story#axzz2iUotmaMg](http://www.latimes.com/science/sciencenow/la-sci-saturated-fat-20131022,0,2193813.story#axzz2iUotmaMg)

The British Medical Journal has issued a clarion call to all who want to ward off heart disease: Forget the statins and bring back the bacon (or at least the full-fat yogurt). Saturated fat is not the widow-maker it's been made out to be, writes British cardiologist Aseem Malhotra in a stinging "Observations" column in the BMJ: The more likely culprits are empty carbs and added sugar.
Virtually all the truths about preventing heart attacks that physicians and patients have held dear for more than a generation are wrong and need to be abandoned, Malhotra writes. He musters a passel of recent research that suggests that the "obsession" with lowering a patients' total cholesterol with statins, and a public health message that has made all sources of saturated fat verboten to the health-conscious, have failed to reduce heart disease.

Indeed, he writes, they have set off market forces that have put people at greater risk. After the Framingham Heart Study showed a correlation between total cholesterol and risk for coronary artery disease in the early 1970s, patients at risk for heart disease were urged to swear off red meat, school lunchrooms shifted to fat-free and low-fat milk, and a food industry eager to please consumers cutting their fat intake rushed to boost the flavor of their new fat-free offerings with added sugar (and, of course, with trans-fats).

The result is a rate of obesity that has "rocketed" upward, writes Malhotra. And, despite a generation of patients taking statins (and enduring their common side effects), the trends in cardiovascular disease have not demonstrably budged.

Malhotra cites a 2009 UCLA study showing that three-quarters of patients admitted to the hospital with acute myocardial infarction do not have high total cholesterol; what they do have, at a rate of 66%, is metabolic syndrome -- a cluster of worrying signs including hypertension, high fasting blood sugar, abdominal obesity, high triglycerides and low HDL ("good" cholesterol).

Meanwhile, research has shown that when people with high LDL cholesterol (the "bad" kind) purge their diet of saturated fats, they lower one kind of LDL (the large, buoyant particles called "Type A" LDL), but not the small, dense particles ("Type B" LDL) that are linked to high carbohydrate intake and are implicated in heart disease.

Recent research has also shown that Mediterranean diets -- admittedly skimpy on red meat but hardly light on saturated fats -- have outpaced both statins and low-fat diets as a means of preventing repeat heart attacks. Other research suggests that the saturated fat in dairy foods may protect against hypertension, inflammation and a host of other dysfunctions increasingly linked to heart attacks.

"It is time to bust the myth of the role of saturated fat in heart disease and wind back the harms of dietary advice that has contributed to obesity," writes Malhotra.

Whether physicians at the front lines of health have gotten the message, a change in thinking is evident, at least, among some of medicine's leaders. But it's not easy to tune out years of what Malhotra calls "the mantra that saturated fat must be removed to reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease."

"When saturated fat got mixed up with the high sugar added to processed food in the second half of the 20th century, it got a bad name," noted UC San Francisco pediatric endocrinologist Robert Lustig. On the question of which is worse -- saturated fat or added sugar, Lustig added, "The American Heart Assn. has weighed in -- the sugar many times over."

Real food includes saturated fat, Lustig said, and real food lives up to the principle that food should confer wellness, not illness. "Instead of lowering serum cholesterol with statins, which is dubious at best, how about serving up some real food?"

\[For the record, 5:20 p.m. Oct. 22: An earlier version of this story misspelled the name of cardiologist Aseem Malhotra.\]

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-22T02:31:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/british-cardiologist-is-getting-the-message-are-you.8551/post-106559>

Life is neither static nor unchanging. Change is the only "real evidence" we have in life for life. Evolution is more about adaptivity than adaptability. Those who cannot change their minds cannot change anything. The measure of intelligence is the ability to change. Never fear change. Fear stasis.

### Lahelada — 2014-01-22T02:38:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/british-cardiologist-is-getting-the-message-are-you.8551/post-106563>

Give her time. This is scary times for her. She believes that whitecoat will make it all better and to suggest that she might try something else is like taking out a card at the bottom of a house of cards as far as hope is concerned. At this moment she is a rabbit caught in the headlights.Let her relax and she might come round . I understand your sadness though.

### caroline — 2014-01-22T02:51:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/british-cardiologist-is-getting-the-message-are-you.8551/post-106568>

I have thought long and hard about this .... when my DH was so ill......would I have taken someone else's advice? no matter how well meaning and how heart felt? I think probably not. At the time you are so scared and so worried and so traumatised and there is so many very, very caring Docs and nurses and support staff holding you together and giving their all.

As Lah said - it feels like a house of cards..... and, in our case, we had been supported by all these wonderful and caring professionals for so long ... both me and my DH. of course - now I can ask much better questions and know so much more.

We sometimes forget that is what they were taught - that is what they know .... and that is where Dr. K. was too - only 8 or so years ago.

But - I think magnesium is a given isn't it? I think Dr. K. has told us that some hospitals administer it after surgery?

### Hope — 2014-01-22T03:32:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/british-cardiologist-is-getting-the-message-are-you.8551/post-106576>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=106568)
>
> I have thought long and hard about this .... when my DH was so ill......would I have taken someone else's advice? no matter how well meaning and how heart felt? I think probably not. At the time you are so scared and so worried and so traumatised and there is so many very, very caring Docs and nurses and support staff holding you together and giving their all.
>
> As Lah said - it feels like a house of cards..... and, in our case, we had been supported by all these wonderful and caring professionals for so long ... both me and my DH. of course - now I can ask much better questions and know so much more.
>
> We sometimes forget that is what they were taught - that is what they know .... and that is where Dr. K. was too - only 8 or so years ago.
>
> But - I think magnesium is a given isn't it? I think Dr. K. has told us that some hospitals administer it after surgery?

Thank you guys, and Dr K. Love all you wrote. I know you are right. I guess I have become so passionate.....and so deeply believe this, and see so much suffering, it's just so hard to see...the ongoing FB posts on their anxiety and not knowing......and how the drugs he will get and another surgery to help with the issues.....and here is something they could try. But no openness. I know I have to accept it. I see the same resistance in my DH too. His father just passed after a stroke 3 years ago. 23andme says DH has higher risk for AFib, which led to his dad's stroke. He thinks I am still mostly crazy too, even about blue light, bc it's inconvenient. It too breaks my heart. But at least with DH I have some ability or opportunity for influence. At least with the food, bc he will eat anything I make. he may still get a stroke or whatever....

But yes the magnesium is a given. But.....she just says they checked electrolytes. But I am sure not the right way....ugh. Just so hard, to see the suffering, when there could be another way, maybe. With all we learn here, ugh. Just so sad. It really is about medical deities. Maybe it would be for me too, still, if those MDs just didn't have answers, back in 1998, or couldn't help me, or wanted to silly things like take my gallbladder out or irradiate my thyroid. I knew it wasn't right. I knew nothing about diet and medicine or anything alternative AT ALL in those weeks....but it wasn't right for me. Of course I didn't have all my arteries blocked around my heart, as my friend's husband did. Who knows what I would do if my husband did now.

I guess in the end, it's hard to imagine people walking off a cliff, when there are other paths downhill.....to the valley of health and safety.

Anyway....thanks you guys for chiming in. I AM so thankful for this 'place'. To know I'm not the only one so into all we learn here. It was and is so great too to have events like last night, to hear Dr K and Misty, but even other folks asking questions I too wonder about....

### caroline — 2014-01-22T03:51:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/british-cardiologist-is-getting-the-message-are-you.8551/post-106578>

Hope - your friend just needs your love and support .... it is ingrained in us that our Doctors are trying their best and I really think they are. I have the loveliest Doc now - he truly cares about me and is very interested in all this but when push comes to shove - I need to rely on him and trust his judgement and hopefully I would be alert enough to offer imput.

When it is an acute situation - you just don't have time to research and get other opinions. As you said - you can somewhat influence your DH to some extent ...but in the end the decisions are his to make for himself.....

### Hope — 2014-01-22T16:46:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/british-cardiologist-is-getting-the-message-are-you.8551/post-106625>

I know Audrey....I know. Thanks for the reminder.....

### Julie5 — 2013-10-27T04:04:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heart-disease.8573/post-99136>

So I spent yesterday and today in the hospital. I had angina for several days, then went to my primary care doc. He told me to go to the hospital and get checked out. I had some illusion that it might have been some thing minor. As it turned out I had a small heart attack last night. When the doc did an angiogram and looked at my heart, he said the artery I just had stents put in in January is in bad shape. One stent was collapsing and there were 2 other blockages he stented. He told my hubby that eventually I will have to have a single bypass.
My question is, can I turn that artery around, at least the parts not already stented? Can I keep the stents I have in healthy? Can I prevent open heart surgery? With these protocols?

The meds I am on didn't do their job, so they are being switched. Also I am on a double handful of supplements, some of them for heart.

### caroline — 2013-10-27T05:15:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heart-disease.8573/post-99139>

Julie - am so sorry to hear all of the above....I hope you find answers......xo

you are in my thoughts and prayers..

### Inger — 2013-10-27T08:45:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heart-disease.8573/post-99140>

> [Julie5 said:](/goto/post?id=99136)
>
> So I spent yesterday and today in the hospital. I had angina for several days, then went to my primary care doc. He told me to go to the hospital and get checked out. I had some illusion that it might have been some thing minor. As it turned out I had a small heart attack last night. When the doc did an angiogram and looked at my heart, he said the artery I just had stents put in in January is in bad shape. One stent was collapsing and there were 2 other blockages he stented. He told my hubby that eventually I will have to have a single bypass.
> My question is, can I turn that artery around, at least the parts not already stented? Can I keep the stents I have in healthy? Can I prevent open heart surgery? With these protocols?
>
>
> The meds I am on didn't do their job, so they are being switched. Also I am on a double handful of supplements, some of them for heart.

Julie.... I personally is convinced our body is able to heal in amazing ways. It just depends on, how far are you willing to go, how much comfort and old way of living are you willing to leave behind.

Run away from EMFs. They are poison for your heart. I speak from own experience. I have not a strong heart at all, and I can feel it in my heart when I have been in to much of EMF. It is scary!
What material are the stents in your heart made of?

Nude sunbathing, or at least as much bare skin as you can (nude is magic and makes huge difference in gaining energy from the sun IMHO)

Earthing with your bare feet on the ground or swimming in a river etc. if you can manange, if the temperatures allow, try to get as much bare skin in touch with the grass/earth as possible.

Super strict diet. No dairy, nuts or eggs. A lot of raw seafood and seaweeds. Try raw heart (grassfed or wild), I think it helps strengthen a weak heart, at least it helps me. Sea-buckthorn [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hippophae\_rhamnoides](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hippophae_rhamnoides) is known to help healing your condition too, I think it is a great thing. Eat the whole raw berries if you can get any, or take some juice every day. Great for supporting heart. You might find other herbs too good for cardiovascular health.

I think go careful wit CT. Increase it slowly, not too much in one setting. Start with face dunks and go slow. Take cold showers but only on legs and arms at first. Increase the amount very slow. Keep it cool at home, try to be much outside with as little clothing as you are comfortable with = at the edge of freezing. Do long slow walks in the forest etc. Maybe to put an icepack on your heart is a good idea. I think it will help healing. But only when you are warm otherwise. Because if I get too much cooled down in my whole body I feel not too well in my heart after, like it has troubles working when trying to heat me up again.. so I think if you have a sick heart maybe be careful to get too frosty... like shivering-for-long-after ice cold!.

Sleep... you know how important it is for healing. Avoid stress at any costs.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-27T16:29:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heart-disease.8573/post-99148>

How do you scope your life? The size and outlook of any situation can be impacted by the lens you are looking through. When we examine tiny things we use a microscope, when we look for new things we look via a telescope. When we are buried in our own small world of one we can lose ourself. We can surface to change the setting we are on using a periscope. When you discover where you are, then you can focus on who you are. Who you are is tied to your connection to others as the energy that exists between folks. They feel and value your connection. When this bond is not present we use a stethoscope to check our wellness quotient. If you want to find the secrets of "your universe", think in terms of energy, frequency and vibration connecting you to others. The more connections you find, the more life support you'll have coursing through your veins. Today, kindle the flame you've tended your whole life and let the wind scatter it to those you care about. Share your warmth with others, because this is what determines your life course. Today is today, only today. Tomorrow...it will only be yesterday..............yes you can change the course of that artery but you must begin to get things right more than you get them wrong.........otherwise you'll run out of time.

Thanks. I will. I also went to my fabulous doc this morning for a follow-up. He called ME. He reviewed my labs from the hospital, compared them to last month's, reviewed the prescriptions, and told me NOT to go on them. He said everything I need to do can be done with the ketogenic diet and some supplements. He wants me to have a heart panel done monthly for awhile until I get my numbers where they belong. I'm so glad I found you, for you are leading me on a journey of health.

### caroline — 2013-10-29T00:04:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heart-disease.8573/post-99296>

Awesome news Julie!! Stick to this Doc like glue ...

This truly is a journey to wellness and optimal health...... we have a front row seat! How lucky we are ...

When your hands/heart are open .... you do receive gifts.......

# Thread: Cold adapting but still have a few issues
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adapting-but-still-have-a-few-issues.8578/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kjgraffin@yahoo.com — 2013-10-27T23:44:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adapting-but-still-have-a-few-issues.8578/post-99186>

Since my thyroid has really improved, I found myself looking forward to colder weather for the first time I can ever remember. I've been getting out and hiking with the temps in the 50's and I can't believe how great it feels. Just last Fall I was turning on the heat if it got colder than 72 in the car. Im loving being able to enjoy the cooler temps, but I'm still having a couple of issues that I can't figure out.

My ears start hurting after being out walking for about 20 minutes. They hurt down deep and not just on the outside. At first I thought it was when my heart rate went up, but that doesn't seem to be the case. It is really painful and I can't figure out why. Also, I can tolerate lower temps when I'm outside better than when I'm inside. Why is that?

Thanks for any thoughts.

### caroline — 2013-10-28T00:30:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adapting-but-still-have-a-few-issues.8578/post-99196>

Do you wear a hat when outside? you are much more active outside? and getting grounded and sunlight........just being in nature is wonderful.

### kjgraffin@yahoo.com — 2013-10-28T02:09:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adapting-but-still-have-a-few-issues.8578/post-99201>

I don't wear a hat. I used to wear ear muffs and am thinking I need to wear them again, but I would really like to know why it bothers my ears so much.

I love hiking. I've been wearing my Juil sandals so that I get lots of grounding!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-28T03:21:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-adapting-but-still-have-a-few-issues.8578/post-99205>

ear muffs would be fine

# Thread: Apartment Living - Celiac Disease - Am I Screwed?

### IoDiner — 2013-10-28T01:09:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/apartment-living-celiac-disease-am-i-screwed.8579/post-99198>

Hey everyone,

I was dx'd with celiac disease at the end of 2012. I've been researching health and diet, got into paleo, then ketosis, and now here. I've been testing alot of Dr. Kruse's ideas and they have been working! I would not be here or be paying attention unless they did something for me.

So I'm wondering how badly am I screwed if I live on the second floor of an apartment in a big city surrounded by lots of EMF, assuming I follow the rest of the protocol? I do CT, very strict ketosis, eat seafood everyday, etc. How important is it for me to move?

Up until very recently I was still skeptical of the grounding, until I started practicing it. I was working on a 3rd story at my job, and I could feel how draining it was. My energy was being sapped like crazy. I couldn't wait to get back down. Somehow I was of aware the height the whole time. Btw I never would have noticed something like this if I was still burning sugar. I also can't help but realize that, although I've always had issues with digestion, I got the celiac after the first year I moved into the apartment.

I have been following Dr. Kruse's recommendations and they have really helped me along with ketosis. I feel better than I have in years. But if the EMF and not being grounded is that big of a problem for me then I would consider moving back to a house with family. How major is EMF and apartment living? I have just started to notice these little things but I realize that I am only about a year out from my gluten free diet so I can't really make any big decisions right now. I'd like to hear some thoughts from the forum.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/apartment-living-celiac-disease-am-i-screwed.8579/post-99330>

The easiest way to figure the time (hour & minutes) conversion is to pick a city in the zone given in the email i.e. in this case Central - so I picked Houston. Then Google "time in Houston now" and a clock will come up telling you the current time there.
So, when I consulted Mr Google at 9.36am NZ time he told me it was 3.36pm Houston time. Which meant it was 2h 24 minutes time until the Webinar start time - of 6pm, or midday here.
Does that make sense?
FWIW the hosting site was one hour wrong. But Mr Google didn't let me down.

# Thread: Interesting Air Polltion article
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-air-polltion-article.8586/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### HoneyChild — 2013-10-28T20:23:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-air-polltion-article.8586/post-99271>

Particulate Matter in the air (or PM) can be very bad for our health. Staying away from traffic as much as possible is one way to keep our exposure levels down, but what about in our actual houses? More often than not, houses are on roads â€“ and roads carry traffic. So is there a way that we can help reduce the concentration of PM that creeps into our houses?
The Big Air Pollution Experiment

Michael Mosley and surgeon Gabriel Weston help test a new pollution filter developed by Professor Barbara Maher at the University of Lancaster: silver birch trees.

We set up a row of 24 young silver birch trees in tubs along the pavement of the busy A9 in Lancaster. Behind the trees were 4 terraced houses, and next to those, 4 identical terraced houses that didnâ€™t have birch trees between them and the traffic on the road.

At the beginning of the fortnight experimental period, Barbara and Gabriel cleaned the TV screens and computer monitors inside each of the 8 houses. These were then left on standby for the next 2 weeks, and acted as very good dust-traps for the PM pollution.

Two weeks later, Gabriel and Barbara came to wipe down an identically sized area of the screens in the front room of each of the houses. Back in the lab, the magnetic properties of each of these wet-wipes was then measured in order to assess the quantity of (iron-bearing) particulate traffic pollution on each one, giving us a measure of the level of pollution in each of the 8 houses (4 protected by trees and 4 not protected by trees).

The results were astounding: the fortnightâ€™s pollution in the 4 houses with trees was 50-60% lower than in those without.

So how were the trees giving so much protection?

Electron microscope images of the leaves of silver birch trees show why they are so good â€“ they are covered in tiny hairs and ridges which help trap the pollution particles. Their sparse structure also helps keep the air circulating and flowing past the leaves to filter it effectively (rather than trapping pollution near the ground as bigger and denser trees do). Each time it rains, the PM pollution is washed off the leaves, allowing them to start trapping more. So whilst the birch trees are in leaf, at least, they are providing an excellent pollution filter. And given that heart disease (which is seriously affected by particulate air pollution) is Britainâ€™s biggest killer, a couple of small silver birch trees in your front garden â€“ or planted along the pavement by the local council â€“ could be a huge boost to your health.

[http://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p01dgd9c/features/pollutionexperiment](http://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p01dgd9c/features/pollutionexperiment)

### HoneyChild — 2013-10-28T20:26:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-air-polltion-article.8586/post-99272>

However, by fitting people with personal pollution monitors, Rossa’s team have managed to find simple ways in which we can all reduce our PM exposure when outside:

They found that children were at risk of exposure when being dropped at the school gates. They breathe fast and being shorter, are nearer the exhaust pipes of cars. At the school gates, cars are often left idling and particulate pollution can build up. So a quick drop-off, and fewer cars at the school gates is important.

People are exposed to a lot of pollution when driving. This could be because air is drawn into the car from directly near the exhaust fumes of the car in front. So leave as much room as you can behind the car in front, and keep windows and air vents closed when in heavy traffic.

When walking, keep on the side of the pavement furthest from the traffic, and choose quieter back streets when possible. And when cycling, also avoid the most traffic-heavy roads if you can.

[http://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p01dgd9c/features/pollution](http://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p01dgd9c/features/pollution)

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-29T00:26:30+0200

This means they capture more of the energy from the photoelectric effect than you can imagine. You need to re listen to the Sept 2013 webinar. I tell you why these metals are so damn important.

### caroline — 2013-10-29T00:52:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-air-polltion-article.8586/post-99303>

Where I live now - there are lots of conifer trees etc. and shrubs and flowers and grass and the ocean is 10 minutes away.
There are a ton of birds ... and every morning I wake to the sound of birds singing and I can go out on my balcony and see the ocean. There is such a sense of peace and calmness..... now I know why...

there are lots of units around but everyone is so friendly here and there is a great mix of people of all ages. I have always said that this is the nicest place I have ever lived .... so everyone is getting a huge payoff.

I have always consistently and persistently said, since I came here, that there is so much positive energy here....

From the first seconds of looking at this apartment - I knew this is where I wanted to be and I was also positive that I would get it. I filled out the paper work and dropped it off at the office and knew I would hear from them before I got home..... and I did!

So - we really have to pay attention to our initial instincts and our feelings of energy and wellness.

I think vibrancy and energy are very tangible if we allow ourself the experience.

This is all in hindsight of course ..... but now I understand it ....That in itself is Quantum magic....

When we collect this information in the form of electromagnetic radiation from sunlight we are recollecting part of the beginning of the universe.......to recreate how the big bang happened. In the beginning.......all matter was life as a vase. At the big bang God dropped his vase. The parts of the vase scattered.........now we are the fundamental particles and single atoms of that dropped vase that are trying to put the vase back together........both cognitively and scientifically..........to do it........we need a lot of energy to overcome the entropy, the randomness, the chaos of the single event when God dropped his vase..........

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-29T03:14:07+0200

# Thread: Treating Chronic Lymes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/treating-chronic-lymes.8590/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Liveea — 2013-10-29T04:46:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/treating-chronic-lymes.8590/post-99319>

I got my labs back from Igenx. Positive for Lyme's. I will have had it for 28 years.

I read Dr. Kruse's blog on Lyme's. My doc is going to make sure that the CD57 test for Lyme's was run or get it ordered. (I guess the full Lyme's blood work panel may not have included it.)

So, he said we would be treating with 3 different antibiotics. It would take a minimum of a year. I asked him about the neurotoxins...(I have been in debilitating pain 24/7 for well over 26 years). He didn't really dismiss my comment but implied that the antibiotics would be it.

I am going to print the blog for him. It is pretty straight forward for new Lyme's. How would I educate or help myself get the best care? (Mind you, Mayo Clinic didn't know what to do with me. So, he is the most literate doctor I have run into in 28 years. He is my one hope.)

Optimal??? IV or oral antibiotics? I don't care. I just want to get well.

Thank you for any input. Treatment may be starting as soon as this week.

(More background health information on my profile)

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-29T05:25:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/treating-chronic-lymes.8590/post-99323>

CT 6 blog tells about the alpha MSH link to immunity........and EE 7 tells you about MHC1 and how it controls immunity both keys to getting better. Brain gut 2 tells you where we got MHC1.

### Liveea — 2013-10-29T06:05:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/treating-chronic-lymes.8590/post-99326>

Thank you. Btw- I ordered your book and looking forward to joining the health force

### caroline — 2013-10-29T06:09:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/treating-chronic-lymes.8590/post-99327>

I am so happy that you are finally getting answers ......that is a heck of a long time to be so sick and in so much pain ...with no answers.

But ..... you are here now.......xo

If you could write a review for JK's Book - he would like that.....

# Thread: Clarification on JanSz's question about olive oil in Ask Jack
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clarification-on-janszs-question-about-olive-oil-in-ask-jack.8595/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hemming — 2013-10-29T16:42:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clarification-on-janszs-question-about-olive-oil-in-ask-jack.8595/post-99347>

We're supposed to eat a lot of seafood and eat mainly MUFAs with it such as olive oil and avocado as saturated fat blocks the absorption of DHA. But how does that tie in with that Jack says that olive oil is not a staple for him? If we eat a lot of seafood then we would automatically be eating a lot of olive oil/MUFA with it. Should we instead eat tallow or lard with the seafood?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-29T19:24:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clarification-on-janszs-question-about-olive-oil-in-ask-jack.8595/post-99363>

Once your are LS, ie low cytokine levels, high tissue O2 content, the fats can and should move to the saturated fats like butter........but MUFA's are perfectly fine with seafood......because it has the other parts MUFA needs to remain stable......iodine and DHA.

# Thread: BJâ€™s Roller Coaster Ride
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 277
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-10-30T16:23:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-99420>

I finally decided itâ€™s time to start a journal and stick with it . . . though Iâ€™m sure I wonâ€™t be updating it too often since I really try hard to limit my computer time. That being said, I feel like Iâ€™ve hit a plateau recently and am just â€œstuckâ€ in my journey and could really use some help sorting some issues out.

Iâ€™ve been blessed to see some amazing improvements in my health in the two years Iâ€™ve been following Jackâ€™s blog. Prior to that I had no success on the GAPS diet, a strict anti-candida diet and just plain paleo. In fact, I actually think I got worse on GAPS  Some of you may remember my post earlier in the summer where I said I was finally getting back to my healthier days. I was feeling great, had increased energy and everything just seemed to be going well.

The problem is it never seems to last for me, which is why Iâ€™ve called this journal my â€œroller coaster ride.â€ I feel like Iâ€™m constantly on this loop with so many ups and downs and right now Iâ€™m definitely on one of the low points. Though I have seen steady improvements in certain areas there are other areas where I feel like Iâ€™m just constantly chasing my tail and they reappear over and over again!

It also seems like every time I remove anything from my life, be it related to diet such as a specific food or limiting EMF, when I reintroduce it in any way I find I am now SUPER sensitive. This has caused me to be EXTREMELY limited with my diet for the last 2+ years. Iâ€™ve had (and still do have AFAIK) problems with oxalates, sulfites, salicylates, histamine, etc, etc. I can honestly say my diet is about as good as it can be and I do not stray at all from what I know I need to do.

I know diet is only a small step in this journey, but just to get it out of the way Iâ€™ll detail what/how I eat because I canâ€™t imagine what Iâ€™m consuming could possibly be part of the problem so Iâ€™m hoping it will be easy to eliminate this as a cause for the recent issues:
\- All grassfed/pastured organic meats â€“ mostly beef, some pork and lamb
\- Lots of wild King salmon sashimi, oysters, shrimp, scallops, sardines, etc.
\- Only carbs are avocado, mushrooms, sea veg
\- Thatâ€™s pretty much it â€“ NO dairy, nuts, eggs
\- I drink a combination of Spring, well and mineral water ~ 1 gallon/day

Iâ€™ve been eating all my meals outside while earthing â€“ even now when the mornings are 32 degrees! I strictly follow the Leptin Rx and have for nearly 2 years now. I havenâ€™t had a single snack that entire time. I try to finish eating 3-4 hours before bed, so I think Iâ€™m pretty good when it comes to the Leptin Rx.

Hereâ€™s one of my problems Iâ€™ve had consistently since starting the Leptin Rx â€“ I get extremely warm after some meals â€“ there seems to be no rhyme or reason to this. One day sashimi will make me start to burn up, the next day it will be after some pork roast, but then Iâ€™ll eat the very same thing the following day and nothing! It also doesn't seem to be an effect from my field either as it sometimes happens at work when I'm surrounded by wifi, but sometimes doesn't; sometimes it happens at home when wifi is turned off. I live in an area with 2-3 acre lots and while I can detect my neighbor's wifi, it is a very low signal. We only allow ours to be turned on for about an hour a day at the most.

My sleep was great over the summer, but in the last month it has royally sucked! Worse than itâ€™s ever been. I wake in the middle of the night burning up. My bedroom is a nice, cold 59 degrees at night and I wake up HOT at 2 AM! We cut the breakers for the bedrooms, sleep on a 20-gauss Magnetico and keep the window open so it's nice and chilly, but none of this seems to be helping.

Iâ€™ve also started passing gallstones and/or liver stones again, which causes me to become constipated. I've had this happen several times in the past as well. I asked Jack about gallstones in an Ask Jack post and my takeaway is the problem is related to dehydration, possible gut bacterial infection (H. Pylori?), low vitamin A/melatonin â€“ problems with the circadian cycle.

So how do I fix this?! It seems like a neverending circle. My sleep sucks right now, so of course my melatonin is out of whack. I had a negative H. Pylori test. I know my BUN/creatinine level sucks, but I'm drinking at least a gallon of good water/day. I am religious about wearing my blue blockers. I put them on as soon as I wake up in the morning and donâ€™t take them off until I go outside in the freezing temps to eat breakfast and wait for the sun to rise. They go back on anytime Iâ€™m on my computer or inside with the lights on. What else can I do?

Two things I changed before the stones recurred: Decreased my Naturethroid because I thought maybe I was taking too much and this was causing the excessive overheating. Turns out it wasnâ€™t, so Iâ€™m back on my normal dose and getting labwork done later this week to confirm. Second change was increasing the amount of fat I was eating. As soon as the weather started to cool I found myself craving it like crazy and I would eat 4-5 tablespoons with my BAB, but then the stones started . . . how is all of this related? I canâ€™t figure it out.

I woke at 2 AM last night and just cried. I really thought I was getting somewhere with my health. I knew sleep was important before Jack even hit it in the webinar, but now that I know just how important it is I'm a mess when I wake up at night because I know what it's doing to me to lay there for hours and not be able to get into a good, deep sleep. What I find strange is that I have no problem falling asleep and am not usually quite as hot then. it's when I wake in the middle of the night that I'm totally burning up and just can't fall back to sleep.

If you've been able to read this far, thanks. I'd love to hear any suggestions you may have. I'm not the most patient person and don't do well at taking 2 steps back for every step I move forward. I'm so tired of being elated by progress only to nosedive into a massive depression because it doesn't last.

### BJK77 — 2013-10-30T16:27:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-99421>

Another thing I thought I should add is that I eat 2 meals per day that are seafood. I also eat sea veggies with my non-seafood meal and with one of my seafood meals. I think I should be getting plenty of iodine . . . question is am I assimilating it?!

Also, I did have my house checked for EMF and readings were very low. I am so sensitive though that even typing on my grounded laptop for 5 minutes causes my right hand to go ice cold and numb. I am exposed to more EMF at work, but I only go in 3 days a week so I was hoping I could manage that by offsetting it at home with CT, earthing, ketosis, etc.

### cantweight — 2013-10-30T18:41:37+0200

Oh BJ I feel you pain!!! Been on the same roller coaster too long. I told my DH that its like every time I figure something out and start to get good results from it my body figures out what I am trying to do and stops me dead in my tracks. I went to a new Dr the other day and told her "If one more fat person tells me I should just give up wheat, that they did and dropped 50 lbs...I will scream!!!" lol

Are you sweating with the body heat that is waking you up? Have you checked to be sure there is no fever indicating infection?

Maybe one of the cooling pads that Randy sleeps on could help or the cooling bandana on your head? What about dropping your magnetico to the floor under your bed till you get back in your sleep groove?

Interesting....I have the same thing with the right hand after having cut my pc/phone time down...maybe we are just aware now where we were desensitized before? Hopefully....

Dont be depressed though...you have done and are doing huge things!

### Inger — 2013-10-30T20:16:54+0200

How nice you have a journal now BJK77! I am so sorry your sleep sucks, that is a nightmare for sure.

59 degree F is too hot for me to sleep in... that is about the summer nights here and I like it way colder. I have around 44 degree F in my bedroom at night right now - with a thin duvet - but when the temps get colder it goes below freezing in there because I have no heating on and window wide open always. Is it possible for you to get it colder in there? 59 degrees F is what I have in my apartment living area all winter long

Maybe you also would sleep better alone, I do. When hubs stay over I sleep not so well at all... he turns around a lot and goes to pee and we try to both fit on a single magnetico pad lol I still manage to sleep somehow. But I get so hot when close to him. Alone I sleep so much better to be honest. Good is I mostly sleep alone!
You should try to sleep in a tent BJK77! Could you do it in your garden maybe? Under some trees?

Are you eating lots of avocados? I know some people do not do well on those, I have forgotten why. What else are you eating for fat.

I just want it all to end!!

Thanks for all the suggestions! I'll have to check into the cooling pads or bandanas. I don't know if I've seen those before.

Some months ago I had problems with feeling very ill and I could barely eat anything for about a week. I pretty much lived off water and sea salt. Then I developed muscle weakness where I would be exhausted just walking from one side of the room to the other. Then I remembered that I probably should take some potassium. The only form of it I had was low salt so I filled an empty capsule or two with it and within an hour I was completely back to normal.

### BJK77 — 2013-11-04T17:16:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-99804>

Thankfully my sleep is getting a bit better! I think it may be due to wearing my blue blockers more often. I am abit confused about how/when to wear them though. I just got a new pair for me and DH from lowbluelight.com and they came with instructions basically saying you should ONLY wear them a few hours before bed and that you need to be very consistent with when you wear them or else you run the risk of trying to reset your circadian clock too many times and that can cause even more damage. I thought I had read on here that we should always wear them when exposed to blue light though?? Am I remembering wrong? For the last few days I've been wearing them whenever I'm on my laptop or using a light other than an amber bulb. I'm pretty sure this is what's helping my sleep, but I don't want to be making things worse!

It also helps that it's getting a bit cooler in the evenings (low 40's) and we keep the bedroom window open, so I'm not quite as warm as I had been at night. It's strange though because I do still find myself getting warm nearly every evening after dinner. And even with the window open and my room feeling like it's freezing, I still can only use one light blanket and I wake very warm. I don't know why this is happening! Body temps are supposed to drop at night, right?!

I'm still strugging to fully understand the concept of transition metals too. I read the latest blog once, but need to go back and re-read it again and again. In my transition metals post, Jack said my work environment (next to a machine shop that does welding/grinding) is probably my main issue. I KNOW I feel worse at work. In fact, I often find myself burning up and getting totally overheated in the office and by the end of the day I'm just exhausted.

My gut says I need to just up and quit, but of course life isn't quite that simple  I've got a lot of soul searching to do to figure out where I need to go from here . . .

Part of me senses that this is all related to me not being able to get into the stinkin' PPP! I listened to the PPP webinar earlier and Jack said there are 2 reasons people can't enter the PPP: 1) carbs are too high; and 2) dehydration.

My BUN/Creatinine ratio sucks, so yes I guess I am intracellularly dehydrated. No surprise to me really as I often feel very dry and thirsty when I'm around too much EMF. The thing is I'm hardly exposed to any major EMF anymore! My house is good. Work is the problem . . . 2-3 days a week is all I go into the office though. Am I being unrealistic to think and \*hope\* that 4-5 days without that exposure is enough for my body to strengthen itself to be able to deal with the few days I am at work?!

ETA: I asked Jack about not being able to enter the PPP and just saw his response that it's either serious EMF risks or really horrible glutathione levels due to poor cysteine utilization. I suppose this means I need to go back to the leaky gut rx. Anyone have the Cliff Notes version or can easily explain to me the issue with low glutathione and poor cysteine utilization? What does that mean? How does that happen and how do we correct it?!

### Bonniebelle — 2013-11-04T22:22:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-99834>

I think this is part of that methylation pathway and I don't completely understand the methylation pathway and I haven't seen the PPP pathway webinar yet. But I did find this article.

[http://www.immunehealthscience.com/cysteine.html](http://www.immunehealthscience.com/cysteine.html)

In order to make glutathione in our bodies we need 3 amino acids...glutamate, glycine, and cysteine. The article says that many diets are deficient in cysteine.

"Your body can and does produce some cysteine on its own from another amino acid – methionine, which is also a sulfur-containing amino acid. Methionine is an essential amino acid, meaning it is not produced by the body but comes from diet only.

The process of transformation of methionine into cysteine is a multi-step process, very complex and requires the presence of certain enzymes and B-vitamins. The process of transforming methionine into cysteine can be interrupted by a number of things.

Some of the cysteine that does get produced from methionine can be used for manufacturing glutathione inside the cells. Unfortunately, methionine also converts into homocysteine in the body.

Not sure if any of that is helpful or not. What was your homocysteine level? Since some of the methionine gets converted into homocysteine rather than cysteine, maybe that is what Dr. K is talking about??? I would like to learn more about this so I need to definitely do some more reading!

Is there anything that you can do in your work environment to protect yourself from EMF exposure?

### BJK77 — 2013-11-06T18:27:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-99994>

Finding myself getting more and more depressed lately. I dreaded this happening all summer long. I felt better over the summer than I had in years and I was afraid once I lost the sun it would all be gone . . . and I think it is  I don't know if it's just the change of seasons or if something else is going on, but I feel like I'm right back where I was 3 years ago and I just can't make sense of anything.

My leaky gut is obviously rearing it's ugly head again . . . no idea why. I'm afraid something is up with my liver. Smells (perfumes, chemicals, etc.) are once again making my extremely sick - headache, nauseous and actually leading to a constant sore throat. Why am I unable to detox these things?! I fear I'm not absorbing nutrients like I should be. Two epi-paleo meals a day should have me loaded with goodies, right?!

I'm also assuming what's going on with my right hand/arm is a form of neuropathy. I just can't figure out why it's getting worse now. Could it be tied to my liver/leaky gut issues? It used to be just my hand would get ice cold and tingle when using an electronic, but now it's actually radiating up all the way to my elbow and it lasts for a very long time even after I stop using my mouse/laptop.

I had made so much progress in the last couple of years and I don't want to go backwards now! I'm finding myself so depressed that I just want to up and leave - everybody and everything! This is not like me at all.

### sooperb — 2013-11-06T18:47:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-99997>

Hold on there, all is not lost I'm sure, you maybe need some investigations. It's possible that the problem with your arm is a mechanical one, maybe research it a bit to see if you can tie it in with overuse or a strain. At the moment it's isolated so it could just be something simple that can be fixed. Maybe your feeling down is an anticipatory reaction (is that a word lol?), you expected to feel bad and now you do, a kind of self-fulfilling prophecy? Hang on to how good you were feeling, you can feel good, you did feel good, hey, that feeling is still in there, dig around a little and recover it

Have you added anything new to your diet or have you dropped something, maybe that has had a knock on effect? I don't know what you are supplementing with but I notice you don't eat nuts, maybe your selenium is low, would a couple of brazil nuts a day be too difficult to add in? Cantweight said she has/had the same issue with her mouse hand so you're not looking at some rare new syndrome lol, my mousehand goes cold too, I changed to a track pad, it's a better way for me so maybe you too? Don't let this defeat you, have a go at finding an answer. I'm sure someone else will be along with more suggestions, you aren't alone with this you know.

BJ I am struggling along with you...scratching and clawing not to fall apart right now. I really think magnesium is a major culprit and vit A. I cant get my magnesium level up to save my damn life. Have you tried either soaking in it or spraying mag oil on your skin? I am going to try the oil next. I just get crazy loose stools from mag and that is really unhelpful in my attempts to hydrate. So freaking frustrated right now. I am so dry it is uncomfortable my skin is scaly and eyelids are so dry it hurts. My legs seize with cramps, any amount of physical exertion is met with extreme muscle fatigue....Ive even had some fibro like burning in my back....something I never thought I'd see the return of. Ugh. I want to sleep constantly and my sex drive took a nosedive...that was my biggest source of fun so now I feel a little depressed and deflated.

I have been mixing things up trying find that sweet spot again....I made a green smoothie today in a desperate attempt to feel anything better and it just made me feel hungry...grrrr. Sigh....

I have been trying to recall when I felt great and mimic what I was doing at that time. If I stumble upon something wonderful i will let you know....hang in there

Worse yet, I see my toes swelling and feel the pain radiating further and further in my arm, not to mention I'm passing gallstones/liver stones like crazy. Things are just not right.

I can't think of anything new I'm doing or anything I've stopped other than getting plenty of good sunshine. I can only assume my body is now just losing way more energy than I'm taking in. Over the summer I was able to do more earthing and got energy from that plus plenty of sunshine.

Thanks for the encouraging words though . . . it's always helpful to not feel so alone! I'm blessed to have a very supportive hubby, but I feel like a broken record talking to him about all of this over and over again. He really tries to listen and he is very compassionate about it all, but I still feel like such a bother.

> [cantweight said:](/goto/post?id=99999)
>
> BJ I am struggling along with you...scratching and clawing not to fall apart right now. I really think magnesium is a major culprit and vit A. I cant get my magnesium level up to save my damn life. Have you tried either soaking in it or spraying mag oil on your skin? I am going to try the oil next. I just get crazy loose stools from mag and that is really unhelpful in my attempts to hydrate. So freaking frustrated right now. I am so dry it is uncomfortable my skin is scaly and eyelids are so dry it hurts. My legs seize with cramps, any amount of physical exertion is met with extreme muscle fatigue....Ive even had some fibro like burning in my back....something I never thought I'd see the return of. Ugh. I want to sleep constantly and my sex drive took a nosedive...that was my biggest source of fun so now I feel a little depressed and deflated.
>
> I have been mixing things up trying find that sweet spot again....I made a green smoothie today in a desperate attempt to feel anything better and it just made me feel hungry...grrrr. Sigh....
>
> I have been trying to recall when I felt great and mimic what I was doing at that time. If I stumble upon something wonderful i will let you know....hang in there

Sorry you're struggling too! I so know that feeling of desperately trying not to fall apart. I find myself randomly crying several times a day right now and that's so unlike me. I don't do pity parties, but I just feel so torn. I'm tired of all the ups and downs and just want to get off this crazy roller coaster ride already!

I did try Mg oil before. I'll have to give and soaking in it that another shot. I can't remember how I reacted to it.

I'm just so tired of feeling like I'm doing everything right only to be slapped over and over again. I get my hopes up when things are going well and tell myself I've finally found my sweet spot and then BAM! I'm back at square one and feel at an all-time low.

I've worried about my job for a long time and just don't know what to do about it. It's not the actual work that's a problem - it's the environment. I don't know how they would react to me asking for a different office. I don't even know if that would make a difference. If I just up and quit, I won't have the $ to eat the way I do or be able to afford the supplements I'm taking (which thankfully are few these days). Heck, we'd be pretty much broke since it would literally cut our income in half. I know money shouldn't be the ultimate deciding factor, but if it's going to add another stressor to the mix that has to be factored into the decision!

I wish I could convince my employer to let me work from home permanently. I do it a few days a week and have done so for several years. There has never been a problem with me getting my work done, so I don't know why their so reluctant to do so, but they are

### BJK77 — 2013-11-07T15:57:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-100070>

My sleep is finally improving. I'm getting a good 7-8 hours per night and I'm actually dreaming every night! That is so rare for me. I'm attributing the change to wearing my blue blockers more frequently. I still wake warm, but at least I don't wake until 7-8 hours after falling asleep, so I'll take it

This is what I find so frustrating though - I'll see an improvement in one area, but everything else seems to be crashing. I've had trouble for the last year or two with my toes swelling and literally turning dark purple/almost black. Yesterday the toes on my left foot were so swollen and sore it hurt to walk and put any pressure on them. My son accidentally and lightly bumped them last night and it hurt so bad I screamed! I think I can finally correlate this with eating any food that contains a moderate to high amount of oxalates and then going to work! Strange as it sounds - I had a bit of dark chocolate yesterday morning - and by a bit, I'm talking one tiny bite! - and then had to work most of the day in the office. If I eat a tiny bit of dark chocolate and don't go into the office, they might swell and discolor a tiny bit, but nothing like this. This is also the absolute worst it's ever been, so something is definitely going on. I've eaten chocolate before and gone to work and they swelled somewhat, but I can't even begin to describe how bad this was!

I assume it's all tied to the fact that my gut is a mess again and my liver is unable to function properly. That of course leads me back to square one again and asking what changed to make this suddenly come back when my gut was healing so well?! I finally got through the Tim Jackson webinar and sent him a request for info about working with him. He seems to really know his stuff and I need to get a handle on methylation/leaky gut/hormones, etc.
