### Flora — 2013-11-07T20:14:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-100111>

Hope you don't mind me chiming in BK77, but have you thought it could possibly be gout? It's caused by a build up of uric acid and would def. tie in with you passing stones as well as having really painful toe joints. Apparently it's more likely as we age and can be caused by eating foods high in purines - which include liver and sardines - and I imagine you probably eat those a fair bit?

I read this link to the UK health service site but I'm sure you'll find lots more on Google. [http://www.nhs.uk/Conditions/gout/Pages/Introduction.aspx](http://www.nhs.uk/Conditions/gout/Pages/Introduction.aspx)

Just a thought - but hope it may help.

I really enjoy your journal and I do feel for you, as it's so frustrating when you're trying to do everything right and you hit a big boulder on your path!

### Shijin13 — 2013-11-07T22:56:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-100124>

BJ

I've been meaning to drop in and give you my perspective on what's going on but it seems Life is getting in my way these days! ughh.

Like you, and a lot of other people, I have great successes followed by a downward spiral. I've just accepted that that's how my body is dealing with everything I'm throwing at it. I'm doing everything I can in this given moment, and I've decided in this moment that's enough, b/c if I give any more I'm going to be stretched so thin - my body's only priority will be pregnenolone steal.

My last set of labs I've got some great improvements, and some really crappy nose dives. but hey I'm still making progress. I too have a very low homeocystine level - and in a consult with Tim Jackson - he pointed me to my CBS defects - [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cystathionine\_beta\_synthase](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cystathionine_beta_synthase) I got a lot of good info out of my consult with Tim and I'm going to be looking in to a few other things again - like parasites (it looks like my little friends might be back), and looking at viruses - that I haven't cleared. So I've got more things to work on... but right now my priority is focusing on Chaos and Havoc and their issues. I'll continue on my journey - but I need to get them better.

Its a challenge - what with Chao's hashi's dx, next set of labs are being run this weekend (but ped endo won't run full labs, just tsh - I did badger him into running TPOs & TABs). And we've reached a point w/Havoc that we have to go have tubes put in b/c he's not clearing the chronic ear infections at all with or without antibiotics - so the docs have just said no more antibiotics for him; add on to the fact his hearing has decreased by 50% since last summer...not a good thing - we don't know if its permeant.

i'm finally out of the Pentagon on a daily basis but sometimes I need to go back in - depending on client needs. It seems there are times I have a good vitamin A cycle, and other times its bad. I think as each of us keeps pulling strings - we find as we fix one thing we knock out another. its a delicate balance trying to get it right and sometimes we all feel like the only thing successful we're doing is running into the same damn brick wall...

but we're not... we'll get there. I think Tim's webinar is going to give us some additional things we can all do to get into optimal health.

hang in there...

### Bonniebelle — 2013-11-07T23:13:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-100127>

Sorry you are having these challenges! I know that is soooooo frustrating when you feel like you are taking a step forward and then taking several steps back! Not sure whether your problem is a mechanical issue related to repetitive motion or related to EMF/Environment....maybe it is a little of both. Could the arm pain be carpal tunnel syndrome? or perhaps it is coming from your neck? (neck pain can cause radiating pain down the arm as well) Have you ever had a neuromuscular massage? It is also sometimes called Trigger Point Therapy. It is a form of deep tissue massage which consists of alternating levels of concentrated pressure on the areas of muscle spasm. The massage therapy pressure is usually applied with the fingers, knuckles, or elbow. Sometimes pressing on one area of the body actually produces a sensation of pain in another site. Sometimes that can give you a handle on where the pain is actually coming from. Not sure if massage therapy would be in your budget, but it could help eliminate toxins, and help with muscle pain.

I had never heard of magnesium oil until finding this site. Sounds interesting, I just heard about it from a sleep post on another site and they recommended Ancient Minerals Magnesium Oil and Lotion. Has anyone tried that before?

I drink dandelion tea for my liver and take liver supplements. I figured that my liver could use a bit of help, don't know if either of those are really necessary though.

You are doing great though with all the changes you have made and following the diet! Don't get discouraged! Just put on your detective hat and try to figure out what may be related to the symptoms you are having. Have you tried icing your arm?

Hang in there!

We may be doing everything perfectly - w/Jacks protocols and anything can take us off the rails. viruses, parasites, stresses (financial, emotional, physical), nutrition (food/supplments), environmental toxins, mold, non-native EMF from our environment. like I told Jason over on facebook:

"Jason's question to Jack is epic, as is Jack's answer. Everyone should read it. My take on JAck's response is that in the athletes its the drastic change in temp during the exertion when warm adapted - the body can't support the quantum tunneling, and the proper unfolding of proteins to leverage the water in the mitochondria to power cellular functions like a superconductor - and the cell is stuck btwn trying to activate cytochrome 2 and bypass cytochrome 1, but b/c it can't its stuck some where "in-between" with the result being an inability to use the krebs cycle - which causes cellular death b/c there's no ATP production AT ALL. For you & Chaos - I think its tied to both Cold and Light. the light signals a seasonal change - impacting your Vitamin A production, and your T4 Conversion to T3 so you can leverage Vit A & T3 to convert LDL to your steroid hormones. the problem comes in as temperature changes and your body attempts to transition to the PPP - it can't b/c you don't have the Progesterone to support entry b/c of dehydration. You also don't have enough Iodine/Iodide to support T4->T3 conversion. You need water, iodine/iodide, & fat to enter ketosis. You need ketosis, and Pg, and structured water to activate the PPP so you can make NADHP+ and bypass Cytochorme 1 and activate cytrochrome 2. **For those of us who are off within the Quantum Health model - we can be perfect in all we do - but one thing can knock us out. Light, Water, Magnetism in balance support sleep/autophagey within the parasympathetic nervous system. Anytime that's off - your guts, cortisol, hormones and thyroid will be off... any stress can knock you out.** for you I think Light is one of your biggest triggers which means - your vitamin A cycle is directly impacted and with that your efforts to reach ketosis are challenged b/c you're body is attempting to tell time/space but can't. which means you're struggling to bypass cytochrome 1 to leverage cytochrome 2 - but can't - so you end up in limbo - just like the athletes in Jack's response....."

Given your experience when you were on vacation - I think Light and EMF are your two biggest drivers - but the next question is what else is off for you? what is making those so pivotal for you. You know what I find really easy is looking at others experiences and slicing through them - but when I look at my situation or Chaos and Havos - I have a harder time - mostly b/c its so personal. I think that sometimes its hardest to look at yourself objectively. At least it is for me. There's so much I want for not only myself health wise but for my family. Trying to balance all of that its very important... and I'm still trying to figure all that out...

I don't think any of us are chasing our tails - I think we're each on our own individual journey - and while that journey isn't going where we want it to go in the time we want it to occur - its exactly what we need - physically, emotionally, mentally and spiritually. we will all get there. I know Jack's told me that I've gone off the rails - a couple of times. and I told him yeah? so what its my journey. My path won't be exactly like his - its mine. Just as your journey is yours. Like the Dr Seuss book "OH, the Places You'll Go!" - we have bang ups and hangups and sometimes end up in the waiting place. But its possible that happens to allow our bodies to recover and prepare for the next assault of healing.... nothing is ever a straight line. its an intricate fascinating beautiful dance - we've forgotten in the hurry up need this NOW environment of the 21st century. Which I think is what Peter talks about in his posts.

You and I will get there. We'll set our children up for success and we'll figure out how to mitigate the crap we're living in. We'll reconnect with nature, we'll reconnect with other people on a level we're desperately seeking. we'll change not only our sphere of influence, but we'll change our world as well. all because we're on this journey.... hang in there...

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-11-11T17:39:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-100429>

If I were your boss, and knew that you had been sick over the last few years ...... then I heard that you were considering quitting your job, that might persuade me to let you work from home fulltime. The rationale is having "most" of you is better than completely losing you.

IMO, you should approach changing your job location like this:

1) spend time meditating over ways you can make a creative offer to your boss so that it is win / win
2) a creative offer might include you working from home full time ... but ... offering to come to the office for important meetings as needed (i.e. several times per week for an hour or so ... or some modification) ...... In reality, many people find that working remote 100% is not good for their career. As has been said in the forum recently, there is no replacement for face to face contact. .... You are creative; you can develop a good proposal .... consider it an interview for a new (and better) job.
3) have your meeting, and offer your proposal. Do not say it unless really necessary, but you should be prepared to give your 2 weeks notice if your proposal is rejected. Your health depends on it.

### BJK77 — 2014-01-28T16:42:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-107157>

Quick update since I try to stay off my laptop as much as possible these days. I've definitely become more sensitive to EMF than even before, which makes it very difficult to stay current with the blogs and goings on at the forum

Since my last post, I've been diagnosed with chronic lyme, babesiosis, a stool parasite and 4 dental cavitations with 3 more infections under teeth where amalgams were replaced in the last few years! Fun times! Oh - and a mold infection in my sinuses . . . . can't forget that! I'm having major issues with oxalates, which I think Dr. Kruse has inferred is an EMF problem, which I am painfully aware of. I understand this all means I have poor redox, but what that really means I have no idea!

What makes one have poor redox and another not? Is it an internal stressor? Could it be the jaw cavitations that I was unaware of for years that my body is constantly fighting? I'm hoping to have them "cleaned out" though this is extremely expensive and obviously there are no guarantees that this will make a difference.

I'm going to be seeing a Dr. Klinghardt-trained practitioner tomorrow and am really hoping he'll have some good guidance for me. This is all so overwhelming. I'm hanging onto my job by a thread and can't afford to lose it because then I won't be able to afford any treatments! I'm still doing all of Dr. Kruses's protocols, but honestly with very little success - obviously because my poor body is suffering so badly.

I have consulted with Tim Jackson and am hoping his words of wisdom and suggestions may help at least a little.

Just thought I'd check in with a little update. I miss contributing and reading more on the forum, but it's just not in the cards for me right now.

Anyone deal with jaw cavitations and have any great advice/suggestions? It's a very expensive surgery to have done and not really know if it's going to make a difference. I've looked at the "tooth meridian chart" and see that excretion of oxalates is a problem stemming from several of the teeth I have issues with, so I'm hoping there's some truth to that and this will all help me to heal . . .

### Inger — 2014-01-29T09:28:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-107263>

So sorry you are struggling so bad BJK77! I wish I could help... The only thing that pops up in my mind is.. I do not really see why you hang on to your job if that is what is making you sick? It is kinda like... a bad circle... bad job... money for treatments... more bad job... more money for treatments... as all the treatments might not even work as the bottom issue is still there...

Is there any change to get another job? Maybe like in a kindergarten, where you can be outside with the kids much every day? There must be some other options... if not... could you just stay home and heal, and live very cheap for a while? Then you could be so much outside everyday, just sleep as much as you like... just relax! So your body could finally heal. In the summer you could tan nude on the grass a lot and stuff like that. And your hubby could work and you could do all the homework of course but that is not too much and you could still have lots of time to concentrate on your healing.

And I think if you have borrelia you might want to go zero carb. Very strict. That is what I would do anyways. No veggies even, nothing. I know about someone who have healed 100% from very serious borrelia doing this. She still eats only beef. She does so well now! She cannot eat anything else at all or she will not feel well but that is ok, their whole family eat only grassfed beef for years and they are crazy healthy! You should try it! I bet seafood would be great too of course, but just no carbs at all.

You know... it is pretty funny but it is actually very nice to not be so attached to food, it is very liberating I think! Especially if that WOE makes you super healthy too!

Oh... and do not ditch CT if it is crazy cold outside, you could roll in the snow? Just have socks and gloves and a hat on! I dip in the river even if it is -20 degree C. I just dip short but I do it.
Or just be outside, bundle up and spend time in the daylight outside at least 15 to 30 minutes / day. I think this is huge in winter. We really really need it. But I think you are doing this already AFAI remeber

Okay.. these are just some suggestions IDK if they are way too crazy or weird but they are just what popped into my mind what might help you get better...
I know there is a way BJK77, there always is (((hugs)))

Hi Inger!

I didn't see your response earlier. Thanks for your always encouraging words  I know it sounds like I should just quit my job and I know Jack has said I need to "sh!t or get off the pot," but it's always easy for others to tell someone else what to do!

I'm currently on a medical leave, so I'm going to use the next few months to really try to get myself better and see if I can't then continue to work. If not, well then I'll know for sure. For now, however, it seems to me I'd only be replacing one stressor with another. If I lose my job, I can't afford the dental surgery that I'm told I need to have done, which will be over $10,000!! I'll also lose my health insurance and the insurance for my kids.

I have been looking for other jobs, but literally everything seems to expose the workers to some level of EMF! All the schools around here have wifi. I was offered a job in the cafeteria at my son's school, which of course isn't exactly my dream career or anything, but I would have done it in a heartbeat if it meant less exposure.

Like I said, I am currently on medical leave at least. I only go into the office 4-6 hours a week and we have also moved my office so I'm no longer near the machine shop that Jack said was probably causing my problems.

So, we'll see what happens . . .

### BJK77 — 2014-02-06T23:23:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-108754>

I should update too to say that I saw the Klinghardt-trained practitioner who did ART testing on me and says I am "infested with mold." He claims it's all through my body and of course put me on a ton of supplements. He's sure this will "do the trick." I don't know if I'm quite as convinced.

After reading EE12, I'm a bit concerned about taking more detoxing agents. I've always known I just don't detox well. In that blog, Jack says "Clearing a toxin does you no good if you cannot generate a redox potential." Well, my redox potential sucks! This I know for sure. Problem is I don't know how to fix it. I've been listening to the webinars - and plan to ask Jack some questions despite him thinking I ask too many  I've gathered that I need to do the following to improve my redox potential:

-   Stay ketotic (my diet is totally tight)
-   Get hydrated (I'm working on this. Like an idiot I had been adding Celtic sea salt to ALL of my water. I thought I had asked Jack about this before and he said he did that when going into the OR. I assumed this was a good thing if you were going to be exposed to non-native EMF and went at it full force. BIG MISTAKE. I now know better!)
-   Make sure I'm getting enough cysteine
-   Somehow I need the power of sunlight right now too, which is not easy to do this time of year in the Northeast! Suggestions?!

Those are the four key areas Jack said are tied directly to redox potential in the webinar: water, ketosis, certain amino acids (cysteine) and sunlight. So, I'm going to go full speed ahead with each of them.

Is it too much to think that I can get rid of this mold in my body, do all of the above and then be able to continue with my job? Is that unrealistic? I thought that's what we all were trying to do here - get optimal and live a good life. I'm not being facetious or rhetorical here. I'm really wondering whether I'm being unrealistic. I guess if I need a reality check, in my mind at least, it's that this may not be able to happen in the next few months while I'm on medical leave. I'm guessing I need more like years to accomplish this, huh?!

### BJK77 — 2014-02-21T16:37:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-111547>

Just some rambling thoughts for the day. It helps to put this down so I can look back on it later and if anyone has any input, please chime in!

I've been brainstorming why I crashed so hard when Fall hit this year. This summer I felt the absolute BEST I have in years - still had issues with some foods and a limited diet and some other issues, but my energy really rocked! Sleep was good too. Fast forward to the beginning of Fall and sleep went to hell, energy levels dropped, temperature regulation became a MAJOR issue - before when my thyroid was off I'd be chilled, now I'm almost constantly in a state of having my upper body feel like it is totally burning up and the night sweats are unbearable. I had a few week time period where I had a constant sore throat, a strange pins and needle/sore feeling in the soles of my feet in the mornings, motion sickness, neck stiffness, heart palpitations and I became chronically constipated.

Had Lyme testing done and one lyme MD says it's positive (even though only 3 bands were positive) and another lyme MD says it's not. I did also get a rash on my foot at the end of summer, so I don't know if there's a connection or not. I also had a blood smear done that clearly showed parasites in my red blood cells. The one Lyme MD says that was babesia and it would explain the night sweats.

What I keep coming back to is that my redox potential must have been improving over the summer when I was feeling so good. What happened to make it slide back down so fast once Fall hit? Could the changing seasons be enough of a factor for that to happen? And so severely? I know I never feel AS good over the winter, but I can't remember crashing so hard either since I first got sick.

I kept telling myself the last two years that I was at least improving somewhat, but looking back on it now I'm not sure that I was. Instead, I was cutting more and more out of my diet and life and still getting more and more sensitivities. I guess if the Klinghardt practitioner is right and my body is loaded with mold (which I'm really beginning to believe), that would make sense that I'm just so overloaded with toxins any additional burden is too much for my body. Considering the fact that I cannot seem to detox anything, this "stuff" just builds up and has nowhere to go!

The Klinghardt guy put me on a ton of supplements (lots of binders - clay, chlorella, Pectaclear, some homeopathic drainages and supports and he wants me to take some anti-yeast things that I haven't even been brave enough to start yet). I'm also taking Welchol from the Lyme MD.

I feel like I'm starting to get some of my old symptoms back that I had before we did the mold remediation in the house - air hunger, more ear/throat congestion than normal. So I'm wondering whether all of these binders/detox agents are stirring things up again. I also put out some MMS in the basement as was suggested to me to kill mold spores. I didn't really think it through though and am now wondering if that would have just killed the mold spores and released mycotoxins and that's why these symptoms are getting kicked up again! I've been told though that MMS should get rid of everything. In fact, it's one of the things the Klinghardt guy wants me to take to detox the mycotoxins from my body, so I guess if it removes it from my body it would remove it from the air too, right?

It just seems very coincidental that I got very sick the last two days after putting the MMS out. However, I also only recently added in the Chlorella and Lithium that Tim Jackson suggested too and stopped my coffee enemas . . . so maybe it was just a build up of toxins. I hate not having a clear answer!

Nothing I can do, but keep moving forward. My dream is still to move south. I need the sun. We had hoped to do this through my job, but obviously that's not going to happen now. DH may have the opportunity through his work, but that won't be for another year or so. In the meantime, I need to get through this winter somehow. I'm wondering if I got so much worse because it's been such a damp, snowy winter and it's so hard to get outside or air the house out. Regardless, I'm formulating a plan to figure things out and keep trucking on:

\- I'm going to have the house tested again for mold so I know for sure if it's a problem here or not (Jack always says it's the environment and it may be, but we've had the house gone over twice now for EMF and it comes back very low and now that I'm not at work more than 4-6 hours a week I think it has to be something else)

\- Work on improving my redox potential. I know this is huge. I realize if my RP was decent I would be able to deal with mold or anything else, so this has got to be priority #1. I'm going to go back through the RP blog and look at each item and make a plan for how I can work on the ones that I know suck for me!

\- I'm trying to find a doctor that will follow Ritchie Shoemaker's protocol to detox the mold.

\- Need to consult with Tim Jackson again to find out why I can't detox. Everytime I've tried in the last few years I end up crashing. I honestly can't say I understand all the methylation issues . . . it's just too complicated for my brain right now.

### Josh — 2014-02-21T17:07:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-111551>

For MMS consider that it is essentially bleach. It will beat you up if your liver is already overloaded. COffee enemas are rough without enough transition metals such as molybdenum and manganese. Sounds as though you may have gotten reinfected at the end of the summer. This is hard to catch when one is already in the thick of long-term stuff. Having practiced radical detox for years with partial results, I now understand that the blessing is in immune regulation. Detox costs a lot of energy and immune regulation requires a good deal of energy so it is a trade-off. One needs to detox enough so that it does not interfere, but the overall energy balance needs to be maintained to have immune regulation/function and congitive function on top of regular physical function. Detox is the easiest to sell because people tend to notice something quickly enough to believe it is worth it. Tonification and strengthening sound good but are much harder in practice and finally balancing is quite elusive. Dr. Kruse is showing me a path to the "simple but not easy" path of health creation and maintenance that I have suspected is possible. The devil is in the detail....

### Josh — 2014-02-21T17:33:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-111553>

If you are working with Tim, I would recommend that you discuss it with him and agree upon a trial in concert with your other therapies. Remember the old adage "too many cooks spoil the stew". It sounds as though no one has explained what your baseline is and the way to determine the proper gradient for you and what your recovery goals look like. Without this, I find people get confused and it becomes one more stress on top of the pile. The relative value of each therapy/intervention and its goal and purpose in light of the larger goal and purpose of getting better and understanding how to stay better. ### BJK77 — 2014-02-27T15:39:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-112449>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=111797)
>
> The magnetico is not a detox problem........you environmental Ca efflux is. Magnetico helps that. When your redox is in the toilet you feel like a rat in a maze and have no clue where to go. Begin to control your environment. then water..........then food. Food is never the first answer. It is just the easy answer for people because of their beliefs. Fear melts like ice when we allow love's divine light to shine upon our ignorance. The 3 legged stool is that light.......begin to let it shine on your semiconductors. That will help your redox potential faster than any of these thoughts I see BJK77. You must come to grips with the real problem. Hard is hard for us all.

Thank you for this Jack. I needed to hear it (yet again!) and I am really working on it.

My husband and I are sitting down this weekend and formulating a plan for how to make this work on a single paycheck. We will find a way somehow. I think I get so caught up in the traditional thinking of needing meds, supplements, treatments, etc, etc and I'm losing sight of what JK's protocols give us: the power to take control and regain our health by using Mother Nature instead of becoming dependent on manmade things.

So, yes - as stubborn as I am (and I admit I really, really am) I do get it. ENVIRONMENT 1ST! I've taken all the "easy" steps - though some may not consider them to be so easy! We've eliminated wi-fi at home, my smart phone is gone, electricity use is even very minimal these days. I'm diligent about following the light cycle. We had engineers out to the house twice now to check it out and both times they came back with very low EMF readings. I don't think my house is a problem, unless there's a mold issue, but I even think that's doubtful. We will be doing an ERMI test this weekend to find out for sure, but I don't think we're going to find much. After having the basement remediated before (and even then it was fairly low levels), we got rid of the carpet in the basement and most other porous things and got all new furniture. I don't think EXPOSURE to mold is my problem. I think my body's REACTION due to my poor redox is the issue.

So, of course it comes back to work. Is 4-6 hours in an ofice environment too much exposure to EMF for me to be able to heal?! Apparently it is. I had hoped not, but the longer this continues the more I'm realizing the continuing damange I'm doing to my body. It is so very hard to wrap my mind around the fact that a couple of hours in an office can destroy me the way it is! I accept it now, I do, but it still seems so crazy to me!

One of my main concerns that constantly lingers in the back of my mind is that it seems like the more I limit anything (foods, chemicals, electricity, etc.) - in this case EMF - the more sensitive I become, so if I quit my job, what happens? Do I need to live like a hermit and never leave my house because I'll become even more sensitive to EMF? I'm not being facetious here - I'm quite serious. There is wi-fi everywhere these days. What happens if I can't go to the grocery store? I've read stories of people who are extremely sensitive to all these things having to move to Arizona and live outside in a tent!

Even after we figure out a plan for me not working for a short time, I know this can't be permanent. I need to put food on the table for my kids and pay the mortgage, so I'll have to find a job I can handle at some point. I've been actively looking at options, but like I said above - everything involves electricity! Going into my kids' schools makes me swell and dehydrate, so schools are out. My body goes crazy at the doctor's office, so obviously nothing medical. Not to mention, there are smart phones everywhere! I've got some things I'm considering though. I make a really awesome lotion that friends/family/teachers who have been gifted it before just love, so I'm looking into starting a small business and selling that at farmer's markets this summer and maybe even local health food stores . . . any tips from someone who has a small business on how to get started?

We are still hoping to move south, but of course it won't be through my job now. DH's company is looking to expand though and has mentioned there may be an opportunity for him to go to FL, but it won't be for 12-18 months. It would alleviate so much stress & anxiety to have him transfer versus having to find something new, so fingers crossed it does pan out and I can take the time waiting to work on getting better. I'm lucky enough that may parents are planning to go with us. I'll still hate to leave behind the rest of my family as we are all very close and it will be so very hard to uproot my kids from their friends, but we're determined to make this move. We've agreed to give it a year to see if it works out with his job and if not, then we'll start looking on our own. I know I need the sun more than anything.

### Josh — 2014-03-21T17:34:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-116957>

Some CT for the mind:

> This mind cannot enter on the path of yoga because yoga means a methodology to reveal the truth.
> Yoga is a method to come to a non-dreaming mind. Yoga is the science to be in the here and now.
> Yoga means now you are ready not to move into the future. Yoga means you are ready now not to
> hope, not to jump ahead of your being. Yoga means to encounter the reality as it is.
> So one can enter yoga, or the path of yoga, only when he is totally frustrated with his own mind as
> it is. If you are still hoping that you can gain something through your mind, yoga is not for you. A
> total frustration is needed – the revelation that this mind which projects is futile, the mind that hopes
> is nonsense, it leads nowhere. It simply closes your eyes; it intoxicates you; it never allows reality to
> be revealed to you. It protects you against reality.
> Your mind is a drug. It is against that which is. So unless you are totally frustrated with your mind,
> with your way of being, the way you have existed up to now, if you can drop it unconditionally, then
> you can enter on the path.
> So many become interested, but very few enter because your interest may be just because of your
> mind. You may be hoping now, through yoga, that you may gain something, but the achieving motive
> is there-you may become perfect through yoga, you may reach to the blissful state of perfect being,
> you may become one with the brahman, you may achieve the satchitananda. This may be the cause
> why you are interested in yoga. If this is the cause then there can be no meeting between you and
> the path which is yoga. Then you are totally against it, moving in a totally opposite dimension.
> Yoga means that now there is no hope, now there is no future, now there are no desires. One is
> ready to know what is. One is not interested in what can be, what should be, what ought to be. One
> is not interested! One is interested only in that which is, because only the real can free you, only the
> reality can become liberation.
> Total despair is needed. That despair is called dukkha by Buddha. And if you are really in misery,
> don’t hope, because your hope will only prolong the misery. Your hope is a drug. It can help you to
> reach death only and nowhere else. All your hopes can lead you only to death. They are leading.
> Become totally hopeless – no future, no hope. Difficult. Needs courage to face the real. But such
> a moment comes to everyone, some time or other. A moment comes to every human being when
> he feels total hopelessness. Absolute meaninglessness happens to him. When he becomes aware
> that whatsoever he is doing is useless, wheresoever he is going, he is going to nowhere, all life
> is meaningless – suddenly hopes drop, future drops, and for the first time you are in tune with the
> present, for the first time you are face to face with reality.

Osho, "Yoga the Alpha and the Omega": vol 1, #1

### BJK77 — 2014-04-17T23:04:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-122239>

Finally have something positive to post! At least I'm taking it as a positive  I'm not back where I was last summer yet, or even last winter for that matter, but after quitting my job and being out of the office environment for a couple of weeks now I do think my dehydration is finally improving. I'll have to run the labs to see for sure, but I don't find myself with an extremely parched mouth all day now. It still happens on occasion, but not constantly. I also find I can eat smaller lunches and dinners. Before this would happen when I was not at work for a few days, but as soon as I'd go back I would be constantly starving again and eating HUGE meals. I'm guessing this was related to the dehydration. Now I eat 8-10 oysters for lunch and sometimes have a 1/2 or whole avocado with them and I'm good to go! Dinner is usually a few scallops and I'm good and full.

I do wake super hungry the next morning, but that just makes it easy to get my BAB down. The only thing that concerns me is a few oysters and scallops aren't going to help me put my weight back on, but all good things come in time . . .

My worst lingering issue for now is my problem with temperature dysregulation. It always gets worse in the evenings, so it really affects my sleep. It starts in the morning when I wake warm, but after drinking my typical 32 oz of ice water before breakfast I start shivering like crazy. Once I take my hot/cold shower, I normalize for a little while. In the evening, the back of my neck feels like it's on fire and sometimes my face too. I'm told this is probably due to mold . . . or at least a biotoxin illness of some sort.

I'm also finding taking activated charcoal 3 times a day to really be helpful. I assume it's binding to toxins. If I miss a dose or a day I really feel the difference.

I've also learned through many trials that ketosis is not for me . . . at least not at this point in my life. I have tried to go high fat several times in the last few months and each time I've crashed hard. I get even more dehydrated and my electrolytes get imbalanced leading to the heart palpitations I've been having. I was glad to hear Jeremy say it didn't work for him either. I know Jack always says it's about your own n=1, but I just always felt like it was impolied ketosis was best for everyone. I think I do much better on high protein, but I do worry about the effects of high ammonia and urinary sulfates from that.

I'm dealing with temperature dysregulation also. Typically I get it after riding in a car for hours, or running errands. The last couple times it was cloudy during the drives. I get chilled. Grounding and hydration helps, but it sure is easier to warm up if the setting sun will peek out for a few minutes. Or if it ever rains here, going out in the rain works pretty good to help me tolerate the cold, though it confuses DH.

### BJK77 — 2014-04-30T15:44:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-124045>

A few quick updates to my roller coaster ride . . . it's still as bumpy as ever with so many ups and downs!

My former employer has asked me to come back for 8 weeks and they will allow me to work from home, which is what I wanted in the first place and they denied it! I decided to go ahead and do it on my terms. The extra money will help tremendously right now with looking for a house and deciding our future. Not to mention, I'll be able to hang on to my Gold Klub membership for a little while longer!

As I posted in another thread, I have an opportunity to go to New Orleans - or maybe I should say the Gulf Coast for a week in May. A lady I used to do some work for a very long time ago somehow heard that I've been ill and considering moving south. We've kept in touch over the years, but I never mentioned anything to her about being ill. She has to present at a conference and suggested I come along for some sunshine. The deal is I would help her prepare for her presentation and she would pay my flight and rent a house for us. I talked to her a bit about EMF and my concerns and she was more than willing to make an hour drive into the city from a beach area to stay there.

So now I'm trying to decide whether I should go or not. I don't know if the plane ride and the mess at the airport will do me more harm than good. Laying on a beach soaking up the sun certainly can't be bad, but is one week enough to really deal with the plane/airport?! That's the question. Plus, most beach towns are still bad for EMF I would assume, so I'd need to find something where we can shut off wifi and not be exposed to the other houses that are bound to be close by . . .

We are still looking at a move. I just ordered meters so we can re-test our current location and a few new areas we're looking at. I know I'd still love to go south, but the jobs are just not there, so at this point I'm just trying to do the best we can.

BJ I am going through the same thing right now. I have struggled with the questions you are asking yourself and still trying to find my way....

I had some health issues and was mildly degrading from living a modern lifestyle with SAD diet in the nineties....fibro at 25, galbladder gone at 28...but I was always able to regroup and diet and exercise....improve my redox potential, until I moved into my super wired "smart" house. I fell apart in months after living there and have many neighbors that did the same. So I think I was teetering on the edge but EMF gave a huge shove into a scary place.

I have now moved into a much better field, am more connected to nature, and am eating well. Now it seems I have succumbed to mold. I have spent months wrestling over the "right" thing to do. Mold remediation is a huge expense! I have decided not to do it. I may feel stupid about this a year or 2 from now but I just feel like I am going to spend the rest of my life chasing down what ever "thing" is currently getting at me or I can put my time and effort and money into making my body so strong that these things dont phase me anymore. I am not sure my approach is going to be the right one...going with my gut here.

I think it will be damn near impossible to live a mold free existence. I can see myself spinning my wheels and spending money I don't have trying to achieve that.

### BJK77 — 2014-05-01T18:02:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-124340>

I think it's finally sinking in for me. It's ESS as I tell my DH - plain and simple - Electron Steal Syndrome. I know I need to build myself up and to do that I need to collect and "catch" as many electrons as possible. I'm just wondering how I could be so damn healthy last summer - ok, well not optimal, but still the healthiest I had been in years and then crash so very hard as soon as the end of summer hit.

I'm wondering if it was just the fact that I was continuing to work and being exposed to that awful environment. I had been earthing outside at my house every day - eating all my meals outside - and I felt fantastic. I had so much energy, I could eat more foods than I had been able to in the last 5 years, my body comp was great, etc, etc. That makes me find it hard to believe it's my house or my home environment. I just ordered a new EMF meter, so I'm going to test it yet again and see what I find.

There was a house we were considering putting a bid on, but the owners denied our request to do a mold test. I know mold itself isn't the issue, but the fact that the owners won't allow us to test just makes me distrustful of them at all, so that's out for now. We're still looking at options both locally and south - DH sends a resume out to any job he is even remotely qualified for in the south and we're looking for an "optimal/perfect" house around here too - we figure it's best not to rule out anything. We're going to continue exploring all options.

### Hope — 2014-05-02T03:45:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-124475>

I think if Dr Shoemaker read this thread, he'd talk about a scientific evaluation of CIRS, and as part of that, genetic susceptibility. I have that yucky gene sit. Never sick in Europe growing up....until I got very sick living in a moldy environment for I am sure more than 10 years.

People who are well, or are not susceptible (who have no ESS) may be able to be OK with mold - genetically, it's 75% of the population that is OK on the mold front...but if you are sensitive, it's best to remove mold, however difficult.....it's worth it. Dr Shoemaker and even celebrity MD's like Mark Hyman says so too.....just like reducing EMF exposure.

BJ saying no to a house that doesn't let you test is a huge sign there could be issues.....people are nuts and greedy and awful and afraid. You did the right thing. But if mold is your issue, or part of your story addressing it is key.

Look at Dave Asprey. I LOVE his story. He has the yucky genes, too....and he had the mold removed. Did a bunch of stuff....and bam. Look at him.....amazing.

I hope you do test...your house and your body. If nothing else, it's a great motivation to do more....

It's not about 'believing it's my house'. It's just like lab tests - you need to actually test. I am not sure why you don't just do that. It's $125 and takes a week. Then you know if mold is in the house or not. If it is, and you spent more of the time outside and felt better, that doesn't negate anything at all...except that the outside time mitigated the possible indoor issue. I honestly wonder about those genes. I know it's all about expression, but still.....

### Hope — 2014-05-02T03:51:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-124478>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=124244)
>
> I'm curious about the mould connection. Before humans could build houses to the standard they do now, they must have lived in some quite interesting buildings, from huts to mansions, all without the benefit of central heating to keep them dry and mould free. So, how did they do it? If the mould is a threat, is it specific types of mould or just any mould? Are there ancient moulds that we might have lived in symbiosis with at one time but which have mutated because of other condition changes, weather or imported wood or airborne chemicals? It's a bit of a conundrum don't you think? Penicillin is a mould too which brings great benefit to many and there's plenty of cheese these days with mould as an intrinsic ingredient. Just musing here, feel free to join in

Soo, bad indoor plumbing - especially in the US, where stuff is so yuckily and cheaply made - is a big issue. And particle board, OSB....it's all crap and mold is very attracted to it. You can have mold anywhere but it's worse in American houses......especially newer ones built....it's awful stuff. But sadly it's all about the almighty dollar.

Some mold affected people in the west end up living like before indoor plumbing - one house to live, one house to bathe.....etc so no indoor plumbing. Some people get really extreme. Others live in trailers. I know of one woman in AZ who lowered her C4a - inflammation indicator - from >20,000 to 451, being what she calls an extreme avoider. It is amazing.

### Hope — 2014-05-02T03:57:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-124481>

> [cantweight said:](/goto/post?id=124477)
>
> Hope how is your mold test 125 and mine is almost a grand???? I am looking at thousands in remediation costs. Please share...thanks!

You're not doing the right one, then, Christi. Only a couple labs do it right. The HERTSMI-2 is $125, a cheaper version of the ERMI that Dawn mentioned. Key is to test DUST, not air as air is NOT reliable. I did this in PA and got a negative but there was TOTALLY mold in that house there. Check out mycometrics.com. Get the Shoemaker book that Martin recommended to you. And the HLA if you can...insurance will cover it. You will learn a LOT.

One woman I know.....her husband did all the remediation work. Her son has the same yucky genes as me. They lived in hotel rooms, motels, and tents for 9 months while he did the work evening and weekends. They redid the whole house till they found the ultimate culprit in the bathroom. AMAZING story.....but her son, and also she, are MUCH better now, in fact back to normal. So now she is making money from home....internet businesses...so as to avoid having to work in moldy buildings.

Did you do the test yet? If not, get out of it...don't waste your money!!

People will charge you an arm and a leg...and still not do the work right. Not worth it. Dr Shoemaker's work isn't for everyone, but boy has he helped a bunch of us. And he is going to help me too. I totally believe even the little CSM I have taken - while still in mold - has majorly improved my digestion, and my hydration levels.....

Good luck to you and hope we can meet one of these days - outside

### BJK77 — 2014-05-02T15:52:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-124557>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=124417)
>
> If your flower pot had 19 holes in it and you kept doing what you been doing........by adding a regular template to your pot what would happen to the flower? It wont grow.........you got to plug the holes. Your environment has been making Fenton hydroxyl free radicals.............constantly. I been telling you that for 2 yrs. I feel like an echo chamber.

Actually Jack, you haven’t been telling me to move for two years. At my first consult you said I was an “easy fix.” Nothing that I’ve done in the past 2 years has been easy. That’s not a complaint, just a fact. I would do it all over again plus more if it helped me to regain my health. You also told me point blank that my problem was probably the fact that I was working next to a machine shop and being exposed to transition metals. I quit my job. That wasn’t easy – again, not a complaint, just a fact.

You also tell us to question everything and TEST – not to assume anything. I had an EMF Engineer test my house and my yard and he told me everything was low. In fact, he said my house was one of the lowest he had tested. I’m in a rural area in the woods with good water. I’m not near any cities. I eat my damn electron dense oysters every day and yet things are getting worse and worse. Yes, I believe in the effects of EMF. Yes, I believe I am very sensitive to it right now and I know I am very sick. I am f’in scared out of my damn mind! We’ve been looking to move, but obviously it takes time to find a better choice. The last thing I want to do is swap one problem for another.

I also cannot wrap my head around how I could get so much better last summer in my home environment. So I am getting another EMF meter to test my home and neighborhood again. I was freakin’ rockstarish last summer right here – so I can’t get how this can be so bad now.

I know you say EMF, EMF, EMF and like I said, I do believe you; however, I also think there are other factors. I think I blew it big time doing daily coffee enemas and depleting my Manganese and Molybdenum such that now I can’t tolerate any sulfur veggies. I also wonder if the coffee wasn’t moldy (as it’s known to be) and that increased my sensitivity to the mold. I do believe there are other factors at play here considering I felt fantastic last summer. I don’t think my high protein diet is helping my dehydration at all either. Yes, I do get dehydrated when around a lot of EMF, but it also happens after a big protein meal when I’m sitting outside earthing and sunning for hours on end. The dehydration actually lessens in the evening when I’ve had a lighter meal and by that time I would have had accumulated a ton of EMF exposure, so I have to believe something else is at play confounding the EMF issue. I have also found with certainty that trying to increase my fats too much causes dehydration issues for me. Given that Jeremy said he almost ended up in the hospital and many others have come forward on the forum and said they can't function on high fat, there's obviously something to this too. And I tried forcing the high fat so many times this winter thinking it would be best that I've no doubt I messed with something - electrolytes or something.

The other thing is that some things are improving with being out of the office. The overheating and burning up of my upper back/neck has really calmed down the last few days. My bowels have actually started to move – not often enough and not optimally, but considering nothing happened on it’s own for 6+ months, it’s a start. The heart palpitations I was having seem to have gone away with a touch of saltwater in the mornings. I’m trying to focus on the positives.

I applaud everything you’re doing. I appreciate so much what you offer and I know you are extremely generous with your time. You say you put emotion aside, and I’m sure you have to, but at the same time it’s obvious that you are a very caring person. I’m not trying to be argumentative or difficult or ridicule any of the great things you’ve done. But before I make another HUGE leap that is supposed to get me better, I need to be sure it’s going to work. If I’m gonna die anyway, why uproot my kids and ruin their lives?! They’ll need the close family that’s around us if something is going to happen.

I know you were sick – I know you ahd the meniscus tear and you were fat. But have you ever been sick to the point of desperation?! Have you ever truly thought your family would be better without you and felt like you just need to make yourself disappear? Until you’ve felt that, please try not to judge those of us who are sick. My husband begs me to fight and I feel like that’s all I’m doing. My fight is running out.

### NeilBB — 2014-05-02T16:26:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-124570>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=124562)
>
> "I know you were sick – I know you ahd the meniscus tear and you were fat. But have you ever been sick to the point of desperation?"
>
> Uh.......yeah. I closed a successful practice and moved my family when they did not want to live to save them..........I think I get it.

Desperation can be a wonderful thing if you harness it and act on it promptly and appropriately. Despair provides an unambiguous signal that corrective action is required. Immediately.

Despair is a psychological mechanism that can energize you to do things you would otherwise be incapable of doing. It trivializes inhibitions, provides sharp focus and a sense of prioritization and screams loudly for aggressive action. The most important changes in my life have been implemented out of a major sense of desperation.

Inaction and resignation in the face of desperation is the killer, NOT the desperation itself.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-05-02T16:26:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-124571>

When your body reacts it is usually a mitochondrial problem......what occurs there? Electrons go in and protons come out. The electrons from food you can control.......but what comes out is 100% tied to your environment. What I find interesting is you even saw how the free electrons/photons from the sun improved your situation last yr. That is a hug tell where your problem lies. You need to be focused on that data point.

What controls what comes out? The ideal functioning of the cytochrome proteins and water around the mitochondria.
What can change things in mitochondria? Two change programs......autophagy and apoptosis. Both require optimal sleep. If sleep is off change is not possible. If change is not possible it must force you to look at your environment WHY.

### sooperb — 2014-05-02T16:29:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-124573>

Bj when I first read your journal I was hugely moved by your story. I really feel for you that it's been such a battle and that you haven't made the progress you hoped. I think everyone who comes to this site learns about something they didn't know either from Jack or even other members. We all have our own stories but I get the impression that not many of us face the same challenges in regard to their health, being fat and unhappy about it isn't nearly so bad as being sick and unhappy about it, especially when the answers aren't making a difference. I suspect there are more fat and unhappy folk here than really sick and unhappy folk and that's where it starts to divide, truly understanding the desperation and worry isn't easy if you haven't been there. It seems that there have been a some members here who have followed all the advice, had the tests, moved home, thrown out a lot of stuff and generally thrown a lot of money at their issues without result. Maybe, just maybe, your prescription isn't here at all? Moving house is a huge upheaval and not something that most folk can afford to do nor is it always possible. A successful neurosurgeon isn't going to find it that difficult compared to someone who needs to find a job with a salary that allows them to live as they do now.

"Inaction and resignation in the face of desperation is the killer, NOT the desperation itself."
I don't honestly think BJ fits into that category, she seems to have tried to fight her situation with everything she can muster. As we say in the UK, it's not for the want of trying!

I was able to improve somewhat - even before finding Jack - and as I said last summer was awesome. ### NeilBB — 2014-05-02T17:38:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-124614>

> [BJK77 said:](/goto/post?id=124557)
>
> I know you were sick – I know you ahd the meniscus tear and you were fat. But have you ever been sick to the point of desperation?! Have you ever truly thought your family would be better without you and felt like you just need to make yourself disappear? Until you’ve felt that, please try not to judge those of us who are sick. My husband begs me to fight and I feel like that’s all I’m doing. My fight is running out.

BJ, you can't stop fighting! None of us can. But you don't have to be "punching" all the time. Fighting can include punching, ducking, backpedaling, headlocks, fancy footwork, hanging out in your corner, etc. A boxer can burn himself out if he is throwing punches constantly. You have to pace yourself. Sometimes you have to sit back and observe the opponent. Sometimes you have to avoid him for a while. Sometimes you can punch him in the head, sometimes in the gut, depending on what he does. Sometimes if you try to punch him, he punches you first and knocks you down. And then you are bruised and the fight gets harder. So you gotta pace yourself. Fight intelligently. Take a step back and observe the enemy. Pause. Meditate. Breathe. A thought can change your DNA, that's science now, not hooey. Take it from a reformed pessimist, optimism seems to have healing power by itself... Stay positive...

Did two more weeks to be safe and I have never had an issue with babesia since. My daughter did really well on Lariam also, did not work for my middle son and my youngest was too young for it. Both boys were treated successfully with Coartem....also an anti-malarial.

Babesia is not a tricky diagnosis...it is or it isnt. It is a pathogenic parasite that invades the red blood cells and can be easily identified by PCR.

I don't know if it's common to rent HBOC's, but there is a local Amish family near me that does so. They have a fascinating story. Their son drowned in a pond and he was resuscitated only to have major brain damage. They've been using HBOT for years now along with a good diet and some herbal remedies and have made wonderful progress with his abilities. They are such strong believers in HBOT that they purchased several and rent them out to be used. They're even working with a lady in AZ who was bedridden with Lyme for 5 years. She says HBOT is what helped her to recover and now she is helping others to get financing to purchase the chambers. Talk about paying it forward . . . I can't wait until I'm better and can do something like this!

The Amish family just purchased 4,000 acres in Tennessee where they plan to move and open a clinic offering HBOT to anyone who can benefit. They're going to do this all by donation and whatever a patient can afford. It's amazing!

### BJK77 — 2014-06-10T15:01:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-130339>

So we took the Magnetico off the bed about 2 weeks ago just to see what would happen. I also started doing HBOT around that time. Sleep improved for a few nights and then got a little worse for a bit, but not as bad as it had been. I assumed it was some detox from the HBOT as I've talked to others with Lyme doing HBOT and they had similar experiences.

Just added the Magnetico back on Sunday night (only 10 gauss this time) and the last two nights have been horrific for sleep! Waking around 2 AM and can't fall back to sleep at all. I just read Jack's post that he believes those of us struggling with the Magnetico do so due to a lack of DHA. I just don't know how I could possibly be low in DHA. Ever since the epi-paleo rx was released, I've been eating at least 2 meals a day of seafood - a dozen+ oysters/day, scallops, salmon, shrimp, clams, mussels, you name it!

As I said, I'm headed to the OBX soon for a glorious 12 days!!! I plan to eat ALL fresh, local seafood and I won't have my magnetico, but I'm anxious to see what this does for me along with lots of sun and ocean, of course

And for now the stinkin' Magnetico is going back off the bed. Poor DH he has had to move that thing on and off so many times now

### BJK77 — 2014-06-10T15:50:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-130347>

Going back to my journal, I see that my EMF sensitivity started after I got the first Magnetico - 10 gauss. Things really started to go downhill after I added the next 10 gauss. I don't know . . . maybe the Magnetico has little to do with it, but it's just not feeling right to me these days.

In a way, my simplistic mind is going back to wondering if (because I'm such a poor detoxifier due to my trashed redox) maybe I'm building up too many toxins even from the seafood - mercury, etc. Considering I can't handle any sulfur foods at all anymore, that would go hand in hand with what Andy Cutler says about mercury toxicity and Dr. Bonlie does say the Magnetico detoxes metals. I can't handle chlorella for sure - makes me crash HARD. The sulfur issues started shortly after adding the 2nd Magnetico - within about a week or two.

Then again, my simplistic mind wants so desperately to believe it is all about electrons. I want to believe all I have to do is continue to eat oysters, get sun, stay connected to nature, mind the light cycle and all will be well. This is why I'm so anxious to see how I do this week at the beach. Thankfully we found a house to rent that the owners will allow us to turn off wifi. Some people had a fit when I asked to shut it down! My hope is we won't be picking up the neighbors' wifi too much since we're supposedly in an area where the houses are on larger lots and a bit more spread out.

Depending on how this trip goes, I'm also planning one in the Gulf for later this summer/early fall.

we eat and process sunlight in our mitochondria........plants do it by an older way with 39 steps all quantized. Want to see some more homology between photosynthesis and us? Photosynthetic cells contain chlorophyll and other light-sensitive pigments that capture solar energy. In the presence of carbon dioxide, such cells are able to convert this solar energy into energy-rich organic molecules, such as glucose. These cells not only drive the global carbon cycle, but they also produce much of the oxygen present in atmosphere of the Earth. Essentially, nonphotosynthetic cells use the products of photosynthesis to do the opposite of photosynthesis: break down glucose and release carbon dioxide.

In plants, photosynthesis takes place in chloroplasts, which contain the chlorophyll. See........red and blue is absorbed and used..........just like us........different mechnism but the goal and mechanism is directional accurate.

### ATL_Paleo — 2014-06-10T19:17:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-130376>

Sounds like a review of Brain Gut 11 is in order [http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-11-is-technology-your-achilles-heel/](http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-11-is-technology-your-achilles-heel/)

**THIRD GRADE MATH QUESTION: 48% vs 10%**

Humans have the largest brain of any mammal. We have 8 lobes in our brain. We have 2 frontal, parietal, temporal, and [occipital lobes](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Occipital_lobe). The Occipital lobes in the back of brain control most of the neuron circuits for light and vision. This implies that 25 % of our brain is tied to light circuits. It gets more interesting for us. The other six lobes have areas within them called the association cortex. Light also wires directly to these areas too! What does this mean? It means if you’re a human with a big ass brain 45-48% of your brain is wired to light circuits. We recently discovered two visual systems in humans. There are two pathways for sight in the retina. One is based on classic photoreceptors (rods and cones) for regular vision, and the other, newly discovered, based on photoreceptive ganglion cells which act as rudimentary visual brightness detectors. These circuits are very important for circadian signaling of day and night. Your hypothalamus is in control of accounting for energy by accounting for electrons from food. These electrons are sent to your mitochondria’s inner membrane to generate energy. Your hypothalamus integrates and yokes sleep and metabolism as I laid out [here a year ago](http://jackkruse.com/why-sleep-and-leptin-are-yoked/). The hypothalamus only makes up **1%** of the total volume of your brain. Moreover, the outflow tracts of the leptin receptors in the hypothalamus only project to **ten percent** of the rest of the neurons in your entire brain.

One is based on classic photoreceptors (rods and cones) for regular vision, and the other, newly discovered, based on photoreceptive ganglion cells which act as rudimentary visual brightness detectors. The amount of non-native EMF that many office workers are subjected to is unreal.

Thus, my takeaway is that many people would gain more health benefits by changing their job location instead of locating their residence. (unless it is obvious they live in EMF hell like in an upper level condo).

### freesia — 2013-11-07T01:32:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transition-metals.8611/post-100024>

One approach, for aluminium at least, might be to try drinking silica rich mineral water (per the aluminium studies at Keele University UK).
I haven't read all of this but here's a discussion on longecity:
[http://www.longecity.org/forum/topic/65798-silicon-rich-mineral-water-purges-aluminium-from-body/](http://www.longecity.org/forum/topic/65798-silicon-rich-mineral-water-purges-aluminium-from-body/)

I changed from drinking S Pellegrino to a NZ Water (pre hole-in-the-ozone vintage) which contains high silica. It's a bit pricey compared to the imported Italian stuff (crazy!) but high aluminium levels is part of my big picture. You may be able to find something local to meet your criteria....that is high silica, and low to zero fluoride and chloride.

Aluminium can cause problems.....here's just one study I found [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/15486972](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/15486972)

Maybe worth considering?

### BJK77 — 2013-11-09T23:03:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transition-metals.8611/post-100289>

I posted a question to Jack about taking in too much cysteine and got a wonderful, lengthy response that I appreciate more than I can say. I feel that Jack is trying to educate me on this and I'm embarrassed to say I'm just not getting it all. Of course the cysteien ties right back to transition metals, which is where I'm having trouble really conceptualizing what he's talking about. I'm going to re-post his response below to see if anyone here can break it down a bit for me. I'm so grateful for his time in trying to get this through to me. I just wish I could get it to sink in!!

I just printed it out and am going to go through it line by line to try to break it down myself, but any input would be very helpful!

The reason I asked about too much cysteine is because I did some google research after hearing Jack talk about it on his latest podcast and saw some references to cysteine toxicity causing kidney stones and swelling. If you've read my journal, you may recall my toes are swelling like crazy and I have major issues with oxalic acid. My toe swelling got so bad this week I was having trouble walking from the pain. I realized I was eating a lot of duck and pork this week and saw that it was high in cysteine, so it just made me wonder if the two are related . . .

Jack's Response:

Cysteine is best assessed via food and not supplement form. Food provides the proper natural balance and helps you avoid the natural imbalance inherent in the use of anything out of its evolutionary package. Remember even good things can become bad because the dose makes the toxin. Food provides the best medicinal dose. You CAN NOT out supplement a bad environment or diet. Consumption of foods containing cysteine, or its precursor methionine, is not likely to cause toxicity symptoms. When you use cysteine of its precursors out of the evolutionary context, cysteine/cytosine can become brain excitoxin that can cause damage to brain cells in susceptible individuals.

Chronic, low level exposure to toxic transition metals is an increasing global environmental problem. The symptoms associated with the slow accumulation of toxic metals are multiple and rather nondescript, and overt expression of toxic effects may not appear until later in life. The sulfhydryl-reactive metals (mercury, cadmium, lead, arsenic) are particularly insidious and can affect a vast array of biochemical and nutritional processes. These metals react with the thiols side groups of cysteine for detoxification and eventual clearance. The thiol side chain in cysteine often participates in enzymatic reactions, serving as a nucleophile. The thiol is susceptible to oxidization to give the disulfide derivative cystine, which serves an important structural role in many proteins. A nucleophile is a chemical species that donates an electron pair to an electrophile to form a chemical bond in relation to a reaction. Protein primary and secondary structure is determined by its DNA and RNA code. This is an example of how the nucleic acids act as a Maxwell Demon, meaning they do multiple things at once to save energy while lowering entropy. It turns out cysteine's side chain groups (denotes as an R group). Tertiary protein structure refers to the complete three dimensional folding of a protein. Stabilization of a protein's tertiary structure may involve interactions between amino acids located far apart along the primary sequence. These may include:

A. weak interactions such as hydrogen bonds and Van der Waals interactions.
B. ionic bonds involving negatively charged and positively charged amino acid side-chain groups.
C. disulfide bonds, covalent linkages that may form as the thiol groups of two cysteine residues are oxidized to a disulfide: 2 R-SH � R-S-S-R. (R being the side chain or group on the protein backbone) This is where protein bending in all neuro immune disease (see EE 8) begin.

Some individuals do not metabolize the amino acid correctly because of an inborn error or metabolism or chronic toxic insidious exposure, and, as a result, may be at risk for certain neurodegenerative diseases, including multiple sclerosis, amylotrophic lateral sclerosis (Lou Gehrig's disease) and Alzheimer's disease. (another EE 8 alert)

The pro-oxidative effects of the metals are compounded by the fact that the metals also inhibit antioxidative enzymes and deplete intracellular glutathione. The metals also have the potential to disrupt the metabolism and biological activities of many proteins due to their high affinity for free sulfhydryl groups. Cysteine has a pivotal role in inducible, endogenous detoxication mechanisms in the body, and metal exposure taxes cysteine status. So BJK the key in all this science is not the cysteine.......but in the chemistry of the transition metals in reaction with normal biologic proteins.............When you begin to supplement you lose the stoichiometry of the chemistry and this pushes the biochemical reaction to either end of the spectrum where the DOSE MAKES the TOXIN. This is why you have to understand the context of things.......biology is not a science of linear thinking as most believe. Moreover, humans think linearly and not stochastically. (QB 8 alert) This is why the reality of the world rarely makes intuitive sense to people. QED works stochastically and this is why most of the counterintuitive things QED says and has proven stumps people steeped in biology.

High doses of oral N-acetyl-cysteine, such as those given to patients with acetaminophen (Tylenol) toxicity, can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Intravenous administration of N-acetyl cysteine can cause allergic reactions in a small percentage of people, characterized by skin flushing, a drop in blood pressure, irregular heart beat, and respiratory distress. Accidental overdose of intravenous N-acetyl-cysteine has proven to be fatal.

Why not do your own n=1 experiment?? Remove the potentially offending food and/or supplements for a week or so. If symptoms resolve, you have identified the source.

Silica is a common additive in the production of foods, where it is used primarily as a flow agent in powdered foods, or to absorb water in hygroscopic applications. It is the primary component of **diatomaceous earth**. Colloidal silica is also used as a wine, beer, and juice fining agent.\[3\]

In pharmaceutical products, silica aids powder flow when tablets are formed.

Microwave-Assisted Carbohydrohalogenation of First-Row Transition-Metal Oxides (M = V, Cr, Mn, Fe, Co, Ni, Cu) with the Formation of Element Halides.
Berger M, Neumeyer F, Auner N.
Source
Institut fÃ¼r Anorganische Chemie, Goethe-UniversitÃ¤t Frankfurt , Max-von-Laue-StraÃŸe 7, 60438 Frankfurt (Main), Germany.
Abstract
The anhydrous forms of first-row transition-metal chlorides and bromides ranging from vanadium to copper were synthesized in a one-step reaction using the relatively inexpensive element oxides, carbon sources, and halogen halides as starting materials. The reactions were carried out in a microwave oven to give quantitative yields within short reaction times.

### QuantumRadical — 2013-11-15T04:44:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transition-metals.8611/post-100746>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=100739)

>
> Something is off and I need to figure it out......

Audrey, Nothing is linear. Today I hated getting into the tub. I had to reach deep down to get up the fortitude to just step in. Water was 48 degrees. Shivered madly for two minutes, then it all got real calm. I became mindful about my surroundings, not thoughtful. I willed my body to just relax and enjoy the cold. And so it was. I went for 31 minutes and only got out because I had another engagement. Could easily have stayed another 15 or 20 minutes. Each time you get into the cold, you are at a different starting point (what us physicists call an initial condition), so why would you expect the following results to be identical to the last set of results? Not going to happen. What you are looking for is that, over time, results indicate in a positive direction (better health, less inflammation, etc). Maybe you should just take a break from it all, then reengage.

Embrace the cold! Why do we ask for ice water at a restaurant instead of hot water? But add flavoring, we ask for hot coffee or hot tea or whatever. We crave cold, but we are brainwashed to a warm adapted environment. Cold water is way more satisfying than plain hot water. But change it's condition (flavoring, for example) and our bodies revolt from the expected value.

Randy

### QuantumRadical — 2013-11-16T04:02:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transition-metals.8611/post-100800>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=100754)
>
> Thanks so much Randy for the pep talk! You articulated that so well ...... now if you could just help me with transition metals........I wish I could even get a question together. Someone asked JK in the "ask Jack" section and he kinda got shot down....that is what I don't understand too.......

Transition metals are easy. They are very sensitive to EMF in how their electrons orbit in the various electron shells. And depending upon which shell the electrons enter (because of the EMF interaction kicking up or down the electron to the various shells), the energy available for transfer (or some other interaction with neighboring protons and electrons--after all, we are made up of electrons, protons, and neutrons, and photons (emf)) changes. If the body is expecting a certain amount of energy for some type of activity, but gets a different energy because of an altered energy state due to non-natural emf, well, that is the definition of a mismatch. And as we have come to learn, these mismatches are not at all good for us.

That is a simple as I can get it. It is all about how transition metals (and how they are "distributed", that is, size of, distribution of, etc.) are able to interact with photons and how corresponding electrons transfer between transition metal electron shells, and then how they subsequently interact with the system writ large. Ultimately, the interaction of transition metals with emfs dictate how proteins fold, and that also is controlled by the special characteristics of water. If they fold improperly, well, just another mismatch.

And don't forget, all of these interactions are quantum and therefore probabilistic and not predictable. it just depends. So the outcomes may be different--that is, one person may be healthy and another contracts cancer. We are all different because of our quantum nature.

Very over simplified, and probably scientifically shallow or even incorrect, but it is my basic model for explaining this. Much more complex at the mathematical level of course. I think JK does a great job of simplifying the science without losing the science.

Randy

### QuantumRadical — 2013-11-16T05:40:53+0200

On missing basic biology. Don't worry. Most biology textbooks aren't addressing any of this. The study of how protein's fold is at its beginning. Huge amounts of money are going into how the process of protein folding unfolds (nice use of wording if I say so myself--ah, did it again!) HaHa. Unless you bring in QED, it is like studying math without the symbols of math--you just don't get the same picture.

And even the biophysics textbooks I have that have recent copyrights, are still chasing the chemical reaction explanation for life. Usually there is just one chapter that merely gives lip service to quantum effects. Too bad, as we see here on JK's blogs and forum, it is so much richer in explanation if QED is folded in. But, again, unfortunately, most of us aren't schooled in quantum physics, nor have we really gotten the symbology right (that is our language and metaphors) as of yet. Remember, in the big picture of things, QED was the black art of theoretical physicists and mathematics was their language. Now we are trying to translate the math into what it means in our language and apply it to fields that hardly recognize these particular Laws of Physics. Those who know, know that the Cat is really dead!!

Randy

> Transition metals are easy. They are very sensitive to EMF in how their electrons orbit in the various electron shells.
