### Butters — 2022-07-23T13:17:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-314560>

Since beginning of july I changed my way of getting to work. I reactivated my old roadbike and ride my 12km (one way) to work. It has not only the effect of saving money (not using the car) but I have the opportunity to get more activity into my every day plus a little more direct sunlight (despite Im not riding upper body naked). The first two weeks were very hard but at the end of the 3. week now its getting easier. My endurance was very bad. I hadnt done any serious endurance work for a couple of years. Im adapting to the regular stress better and plan to use it as an HIIT opportunity. Kid no. 3 is on hits way but not yet hatched and it will change quite a bit in a few weeks.
Although I begin to like roadbiking again I know I dont have the time to invest much time in it and dont see the sense in it to ride hours and hours for what. My time is rare soon, so I have to decide to invest in the talents I have and that is clearly the music, not sports and clearly not bicycling  (know it from vo2 max tests in university). But I feel much better with that bicyling every day.

I have not checked any blood work since April, but I see that sleep is a prime factor in my life. Because I feel very different when I go to bed earlier and sleep good. And I have gotten to bed later and later over the winter month. Also I noticed it is not good to bring the kids to bed and fall asleep AND wake up 1-2hrs later and do other things, then go do bed again at like 12pm. Its definitely not good and I try now not to fall asleep with the children. Of course evening time is now the only time I have time for myself and to do anything despite work and family, so I have to watch out. But no sleep time equals no quality time for me, that is clear. Because all I do without sleep is much more uneffective and more random so I better have a good nights sleep and use my free time effective.
Randoms thoughts dropped...

### Butters — 2022-09-23T23:19:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-315995>

Biking was a good idea although it brought out, that I might have mild visual snow symptomx or something.
Because I also have to ride in the dark (morning oder evening) I noticed symptomps more than with car driving which are:

\-bluefield entoptic phenomenon for 5 years since floaters in both eyes happended at the same time, mostly in bright light
\-long after images especially from strong light sources (sun, glare, reflection, incandescent blub etc.)
\-short after images (parts of second) after looking at an object and look on an even area
\-halos around light sources (especially at night) but for 5 years too I think
\-self light phenomenon of the eyes in the dark, I notice it here and then when I go to sleep and make it pitch black (otherwise I dont see it)
\-since beginning this year little black points when I look at dark objects at day time in bright light
\-when I look at a lamp in the dark a few second and then look in the dark, the after image has some white fizzling dots for a few seconds
\-after physical activity (bike riding) when I look at a light source and then into a dark background I have a flickering image of the light source for a few second, like the retina wobbles, the flickering gets weaker over the time of 1-2 min. after end of the physical effort (so maybe a different problem) and finally disappears but this is nuts when it happends in the dark

### Butters — 2023-07-03T23:28:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-323202>

So Im now in my 4th week of eating Epi-Paleo. I realized 3 weeks ago that with my 3. child if gone far of the road and I think it was longer. So eating omega 6s like crisps, peanut butter, lots of ice cream, sweets, drinking beer and so on. Even almost ditched my holy rule of not snacking in between. Almost, because I did not that regularly maybe I got no real weight gain and remain leptin sensitive?!
But I had an sudden allergy coming up I think, something that flared just a little the last years but not much. But this year I had I think one heavy strike. Also the last ice cream I ate, I got horrible brain freeze which according to Jack is massive O6/3 disbalance. My cold resisistance also decreased, so it was time to change some things. Also I noticed I even didnt get to the evening without eating lunch, a thing that generally wasnt a problem the last years. So maybe a little leptin resistant?
So I started to get to see the sun (not the actual sunrise because now that is 5:11 am and I have a big tree line and a hill in the way, but as soon as possible I was sure to see at least 1-5min. Then eating BAB. Was strange to me, I wasnt used to it anymore. But now when I eat BAB I can get to evening without problem. I took some time. Umfortunately I got some gut issues with loose stool (I drank much coconut milk) which hasnt been solved yet, but got a little better. Maybe gut problem?! Posted already and got some reading answer from Jack, thanks. But the feeling of fullness and not having to eat lunch is a big relief, because I dont want to eat at my works place anymore (O6, carbs, conventional meat etc.). I didnt do that the past 3 weeks. I used lunch time to get almost 1hr of midday sun (I have 1hr lunch break) or do some weight training. But whenever sun is out, I prioritize it.
I feel it is a long way to go and things are happening slowly, but slowly they got me in that position right?!

The big thing for me nowadays is the sleep. With 3 kids many things have to be done and there is only so much time. Cheers to drezy for one of his posts on playing the guitar  I now know what you mean. But the sleep is the big thing.

Today I had a big migraine onset. Never had that before. Usually when I had it, it started with aura which faded after 1 hrs and sometimes headaches and speech problems. Now I had 4-5 aura onsets like over 4 hrs, when one was gone the next started. No big headaches and not so much speech difficulties but that aura incident was huge. Made me frightened. I dont know what the trigger was. But it fits in the frame I think, because of O6/3 imbalance and blue light.
One thing that left my curious is why I have had a phase, when I didnt had these migraines at all. In my puberty is started like with 14/15 and it ended with 16 when I started to do sports at a regular base. Then it was gone for almost 10 years and started again at 27. In all that time, which was study time I didnt gave a shit about light and food but have done a lot of sports (no chronic cardio). Why started it after this acute phase with blue light, not much sleep often, shitty food etc.? Can the body only stand so much and one trigger it was to flip the switch to reactivate it?

I need to sleep...

### Butters — 2023-07-11T15:25:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-323528>

First morning with a crisp wake up. I woke up and was ready to go, it was 5.45 am. Sunrise is 5.18am. Must be the BAB and bright light after waking up. Havent had this a long time. A have not prioritized sleep a long time and worked up to a deficit. Also finally more dreaming. Maybe placebo or not but I think my thinking gets better and Im generally more optimistic to different things.

Ive done some bike sprints from work to home the day before yesterday and experienced eye flickering at the end. I begin to notice that this happens everytime I either enter high heart frequency over longer timespan or accumulated over intervals. Its flickering in both eyes. When I look into light and then close the eyes of look into darker backrounds it flickers afterwards in the center of the eye. The flickering gets less and finally disappears when heart rate is coming down or other factors of high intensity getting less in the blood stream. So at the beginning after flickering lasts some seconds (maybe 3s). Its both eyes the same, so could it be the brain/nerve etc.? Is there not enough blood circulation or to high blood pressure in HIIT exercise or nitric oxide release switching something on?
Also when I jump or run I have like one spot in my field of sight in the middle that get darker with the bounce, I think both eyes, too.
Also some more visual phenomena in both eyes. Blue field entoptic phenomenon, flickering black dots when look onto dark backgrounds when surrounding light is bright (black t-shirt in bright sunshine), pulsating retina sometimes with strong heartbeat. All both eyes, as good as I can tell. So something is coupled here in both eyes. Otherwise it would be a big, big coincidence. Melanin in visual cortex or POMC defect there?
I just re and relistening the podcast about POMC, which is mind blowing, but so far I cant decipher what my problem could be.

I have good melanin on my skin and hold my tan way into the winter, so not much is sucked inside I would assume. There I am lost.

### Butters — 2023-07-14T16:40:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-323736>

So Im waking up even earlier now. Last 2 days was around 5.20am, which puts me right at sunrise. Problem: sunset is 21.27 pm. So ruffly enough time to sleep for the ideal 7.30 hrs. Yesterday it was only like 7hrs. Because on sunset its not dark enough. But good thing is, my body is still managing the clocks.
I noticed suddenly I get often cramps when waking up. Havent had cramps for years. Is my body depleting mg for some reason? Sucking it from the muscles to somewhere else?
My digestion has become much better, but some smoked fish I dont tolerate (mackerel). Stomach ache and diarrhea are the result. Same with mushrooms .
I noticed that laptop time prior to bed has significant influence on my sleep, despite using IRIS AND blueblockers and dimming with iris. Must be just the illuminance or maybe flicker from screen. I sleep much better without it. I just play guitar in dawn time, as long I can see something.

### Butters — 2024-06-15T12:39:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-335806>

On the hunt for better O3/O6, low deuterium and gut health, I came to the conclusion to eliminate certain foods and reintegrate to test myself, how I behave. So my littel 1=1 of gut health. Im not under the african sun 12/7 so, I have to tweak the food also.
I eliminated grains and noticed less appetite, need to eat lower food portions and less gas, better stool. I would have eliminated it anyway, but I now want to test to gain experience. This is also true for german beer . So no beer for me. We are on alcohol, so I have to test wine next. But I tolerated wine good in the past with plenty of water.
On my path I tested food like onions, garlic and recognized, they are not good for me either. Much gas etc.
Also my gut is happier, when I only eat in the morning and in the evening, respecting sunlight of course. Let my body have some digestion rest.

Now I happened to stumble on the so called FODMAPs - "Fermentable Oligosaccharides, Disaccharides, Monosaccharides and Polyols" and realized all the foods are in there. And most of these foods we are not supposed to eat either for most of the time.

On the test list is milk products and coffee.

On this shitty post I realized that the "perfect" stool should be a normal thing. BTW we have 2 cats and they are the example, they have always perfect stool, no need for toilet paper  except the food was rotten or something. And so it should be for us. Brain is better with better stool. Money is saved with saving toilet paper and time is saved. Everything is better with perfect stool. And because its "perfect" stool for me, I realized its not normal for me otherwise it wouldnt be special. That has to change. Oh and sleep is also better. So its brain/gut axis all the way.

Other topic - Im on the hunt for all day blueblocking options for prescription glasses. Unfortunately its not so simple in germany to get glasses simply tinted like in the US. You can only get prescription glasses with partial blueblock and they are not even tinted, so no real thing.
I want to get some yellow like glasses for everyday use, especially for work environment.
Only option I found is from prisma, who sell glasses with magnetic front clip options and various filters. Prescription glasses are not tinted but you can fit them in the frame and front clip the filters you like. But its only 2 frames to choose from. So not many style options here.

Maybe someone know other options in german to get tinted glasses so you can customized frames?

### Butters — 2024-06-16T12:18:45+0300

The acoustic guitar is still my go to solution to relax and let the mind rest. Grip the guitar, everything is literally gone.
I found a young band on youtube, which does incredible acoustic covers of rock songs, real inspiration. To me it even sounds better acoustic this way than electric + the outside vibe.
Unfortunately if you have a good acoustic guitar, you wont play it in the sun, because it will damage in direct sunlight. For that you have to downgrade a bit.
But the magic in acoustic guitar playing lies in the rhythm factor to me. Its like you play the drum the same time and then you get a feel of flow, almost decoupling the mind to another highter state. At least that it is for me, when I am playing. Its like flicking a switch.

### Butters — 2024-09-04T12:43:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-339237>

Experiment is running good so for. I now have some good homeostasis in my gut. My standard now is bristol 3-4. I know when I eat certain things (too much sugar or coffee). When I reduce it my stool is fine the next day. I now understand better that current situation means everything and a healthy gut can handle more than a unhealthy. I now can eat lots of coconut oil without getting the runs and without stomach ache. I eat more fish, try to eat at least 3 times a week a big portion like 300g each. Right now salmon is the best option so far. But it seems to have cured my "brain freeze" that I again got a few weeks ago when eating ice cream. According to Jack it was too much omega 6s.
What had I done? I stopped peanut butter completely AND ate more fish, sometimes shrimps.

Negative: I seem to have injured my knee a few weeks ago. Symptoms vary but I frighten a meniscus tear. Exam is coming soon. Medical consultation in rural regions of germany is difficult these times.

I bought a fire pit for making a fire here and then. Its also great for gathering with other people and will be nice in winter time for that.
And I bought more BTC. Try to take away a little every month.

### Butters — 2024-09-20T13:49:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-339755>

I noticed some things the past months. Despite I dont do any weight lifting, because of the knee issue, my body condition didnt changed that much or I am slightly more ripped. My hunger feelings are more controlable. Despite of me, most of the time eating only in the morning and evening I can go with little portion size and be satisfied. The was not always possible in the past.
May it be, that weight lifting (not crazy) was a more harmful stressor than I thought? My gut also is pretty good. I begin to get cravings after fish, which means its always the first thing that comes to my mind when I think about a meal.
My gut also tolerates products with higher fiber like onion, garlic and pumpkin better. It also tolerates some amount of coffee without giving me runs. Now its getting into autumn and I will change to more butter and fish, not so much coconut oil. Seasonally good, but good for my gut? We will see.
My knee is not rehabed, so I need to fix this first. Maybe I let the weight training go for winter and only try to do CT and find out how this changes me.

### Butters — 2025-05-24T11:21:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-353253>

For more than 1 months now completely sugar free. I need to eat more fat to compensate. But it works very good. For some time integrating again coconut manna and oil for fat energy and gut health. I think when I look at my past, I wrecked my gut pretty heavy. I now try to get everything to heal my gut, including sun of course, but also dont eat carbs other than vegetables in seasons (mostly). Situation in germany is becoming unfavourable. When once had a good health system. With massmigration and corrupt politics not anymore. So I have to care for myself more.
I still have the nail fungus problem and I think bad blood circulation in the feet is the problem here too. I tend to get some renaulds symptoms for some year now. Not heavy but cold feet often are violett (low oxygen). So that feeds the fungus, plus this is the reason I now have ditched the carbs completely because I think my gut is not alright and so my immunsystem. Otherwise I wouldnt have that problem.

I once bought a big package of grassfed bones to make my own bone broth and heal my gut. All went good, cooking not a problem. But somehow I dont tolerate the broth. I get bloating and gas and my stool is changing. So that is not as intended. I wanted it for my gut walls and stuff. When I drink a glass (250ml) I tend to feel very full but not satisfied. I doesnt fit well into coconut consumption as well. I can now eat more coconut oil without getting runs and stuff but broth AND coconut oil give me stomach ache. I tried to ditch onions but that wasnt it. Must be the mass of Kollagen? I dont know. Maybe I should try some other broth not self cooked but organic grass fed and see if its my broth that is giving me problems.
1-2 days after ending consuming the broth my stool got perfect. Like last summer. Not even coffee seems to intervene. I lowered my coffee consumption - also for the gut - leaving at least 2 hrs after waking up to not interfere with my morning cortisol, not drinking after 2 pm also.

Its now the time I begin to get some financial options so I invest in bitcoin etc. but money is still rare, so no major benefits. Back in the time 2020 when Jack said stack BTC I even hadn money for such things. Now I can do it - a bit. My long term aim is to get to the 6 figure Capital in 5 Years. In germany that is pretty tough because of the f\*\*\*\*\*g high taxes. Becoming socialist shithole here. My aim is to get out, but without capital no bueno - without health either.

God thank my kids are pretty healthy. Invested in my kids the past 9 years, thats the reason I had no major money supply. Gotta change that now. It all bounds together, Jacks posts make sense more and more now. I try not to look back, because past formed me as I am now.

### Butters — 2026-02-10T22:59:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-366651>

One month ago I had enough of normal and mediocre. I see so many people in my daily practise with sickness I dont wanna have and same time I can not do much about in my Position, yet. I learned not yet or in German "noch nicht" is powerful phrase.
Whatever I constantly got brain freezes when eating cold Things Like Frozen berries which I Like very much and remembered Jack telling Something about Omega 6s too much, dadada. Also my gut Problem had Not yet resolved since summer. The lyme Thing and so, which made me really think.
I thought I was doing well, I wasnt obviously. I dont want to waste time anymore. There is nothing to waste here in Germany nowadays. Centralized med is fucked, I see it everyday. I dont want to Go there.
Back to story I try to optimize what is possible with 3 Kids.
First was canged to counter my Omega 3/6 Balance I had because of too much peanut Butter stuff and maybe shitty light at Work. So I aimed at fish every day to get 5-6 days mostly sockeye Salmon. I literally stuff this down now. All seed crap in food gone including peanut. I do better with Frozen berries. Its Not completely gone but far better and I can Drink cold water without any Problem 500ml down and Go. So that is a Change.
Two weeks ago I Started CT again because my sleep was Not good. I know its light but ITS also Winter so no Major effect but maybe cold i thought.
Because of my shitty Omega 3s I Started with facedunks. Tap water is here now below 50 degrees, so ideal. At the second dunk I felt pain behind my forehead. I guess again Omega 3. It got better but ist Not yet resolved. Im working. But it had Major effect in my sleep. It was shocking deep, I got Dreams again right away and was more fit in the morning. But it ist Not near I was in the past. I will continue with the dunks for my head. Yesterday i took my first shower, man i was a wimp. Under 5min I had too Finish, today it was better, but my Regeneration is shitty. For 1hr after that I am cold. Cold Finger and feet (light Renauld History). Maybe i am Not ready. Im Not Sure If i Had cold urticaria, I have to Test Further. So far I am so Sure Not cold Adapted its Not funny.
Im currently on my way to find some glasses with Clip on Bluelightblockers for Work. That is going now. I try to add steps.

Enough for today

### Butters — 2026-02-19T19:16:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-367161>

I Started to implement one Thing at a time. Every week a new Thing so the mind doesnt get overwhelmed and past Things manifest.
I figured the bone broth wasnt a Problem. This past week, Coffee is gone. I admit it was mainly because of a diarrhea Bug, but now I am Coffee free and over the 2-3 days Adaptation Phase.
Ordered Multimeter to Test various shoes and Materials for grounding effect. I dont wanna Trust anymore but Test.
Ordered frames for glasses to try with option for various Blueblocker Clip ons. I dont wanna wait anymore for opthometrists to react in my questions for specific frames.
Meanwhile DHA feeding goes on.

I cannot tell you how how many calls I have had from people who had a Building Biologists (or other EMF Consultant) out to measure, received a VERY comprehensive 20 (or 50) page report . . . that they cold not understand so had no idea what to do to fix their nnEMF problem . . . # Thread: The easy guide to optimal road quantum mechanics
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-easy-guide-to-optimal-road-quantum-mechanics.8821/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-21T01:03:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-easy-guide-to-optimal-road-quantum-mechanics.8821/post-101168>

A Lazy Layman's Guide to Quantum Physics
Â·
Â· James Higgo 1999
Â·
Â·
Â·
Â·
Â· What is Quantum Physics?
Â· That's an easy one: it's the science of things so small that the quantum nature of reality has an effect. Quantum means 'discrete amount' or 'portion'. Max Planck discovered in 1900 that you couldn't get smaller than a certain minimum amount of anything. This minimum amount is now called the Planck unit.
Â· Why is it weird?
Â· Niels Bohr, the father of the orthodox 'Copenhagen Interpretation' of quantum physics once said, "Anyone who is not shocked by quantum theory has not understood it".
Â· To understand the weirdness completely, you just need to know about three experiments: Light Bulb, Two Slits, Schroedinger's Cat.
Â· Two Slits
Â· The simplest experiment to demonstrate quantum weirdness involves shining a light through two parallel slits and looking at the screen. It can be shown that a single photon (particle of light) can interfere with itself, as if it travelled through both slits at once.
Â· Light Bulb
Â· Imagine a light bulb filament gives out a photon, seemingly in a random direction. Erwin Schroedinger came up with a nine-letter-long equation that correctly predicts the chances of finding that photon at any given point. He envisaged a kind of wave, like a ripple from a pebble dropped into a pond, spreading out from the filament. Once you look at the photon, this 'wavefunction' collapses into the single point at which the photon really is.
Â· Schroedinger's Cat
Â· In this experiment, we take your pet cat and put it in a box with a bottle of cyanide. We rig it up so that a detector looks at an isolated electron and determines whether it is 'spin up' or 'spin down' (it can have either characteristic, seemingly at random). If it is 'spin up', then the bottle is opened and the cat gets it. Ten minutes later we open the box and see if the cat is alive or dead. The question is: what state is the cat in between the detector being activated and you opening the box. Nobody has actually done this experiment (to my knowledge) but it does show up a paradox that arises in certain interpretations.
Â·
Â·
Â· If you dare to think about it (you're not really supposed to), you have to believe one of the following things:
Â·
Â· MENU
Â· Your consciousness affects the behaviour of subatomic particles
Â· - or -
Â· Particles move backwards as well as forwards in time and appear in all possible places at once
Â· - or -
Â· The universe is splitting, every Planck-time (10 E-43 seconds) into billions of parallel universes
Â· - or -
Â· The universe is interconnected with faster-than-light transfers of information
Â· ----
Â· Full English Breakfast
Â· Coffee or Tea
Â· These are the results of the different interpretations of quantum physics. The interpretations all compete with each other. Otherwise respectable physicists can get quite heated about how sensible their pet interpretation is and how crazy all the others are. At the moment, there's about one new interpretation every three months, but most of them fit into these categories.
Â·
Â· What does it mean?
Â· The meaning of quantum physics is a bit of a taboo subject, but everyone thinks about it. To make it all a bit more respectable, it is better to say 'ontology' than 'meaning' -- it's the same thing. There are several competing interpretations and the one thing they all have in common is that each of them explains all the facts and predicts every experiment's outcome correctly.
Â· Copenhagen Interpretation (CI)
Â· This is the granddaddy of interpretations, championed by the formidable Niels Bohr of Copenhagen university. He browbeat all dissenters into submission (with the notable exception of Einstein) at a Brussels conference sponsored by a man called Solvay in 1927. Bohr thereby stifled the debate for a generation or two.
Â· The CI has a bit of a cheek calling itself an interpretation, because it essentially says "thou shalt not ask what happens before ye look". He pointed out that the Schroedinger equation worked as a tool for calculating where the particle would be, except that it 'collapsed' as soon as you took a peek. If anyone asked why this was, he would say, "shut up and calculate" (or he might as well have done).
Â· When you do try to take Copenhagen seriously you come to the conclusion that consciousness and particle physics are inter-related, and you rush off to write a book called The Dancing Wu-Li Masters.
Â· More recently, Henry Stapp at the University of California has written papers such as On Quantum Theories of the Mind (1997). Stapp's central thesis is that the synapses in your brain are so small that quantum effects are significant. This means that there is quantum uncertainty about whether a neuron will fire or not - and this degree of freedom that nature has allows for the interaction of mind and matter.
Â· What happens to the cat? You're not allowed to ask.
Â· Many Worlds Interpretation (MWI)
Â· The various paradoxes that the Copenhagen Interpretation gave rise to (famously Schroedinger's cat, and Einstein's dislike of "spooky action at a distance") led others to keep on trying to find a better interpretation.
Â· The simplest was put forward by a student, Hugh Everett, in 1957. He simply said that the Schroedinger equation does not collapse. Of course, everyone laughed at him, because they could see that the photon, for example, was in just one place when they looked, not in all possible places. But after a couple of decades, this issue was resolved with the concept of decoherence - the idea that different universes can very quickly branch apart, so that there is very little relationship between them after a tiny fraction of a second.
Â· This has led to what should strictly be called the 'post-Everett' Interpretation, but is still usually called MWI. It is now one of the most popular interpretations and has won some impromptu beauty contests at physics conferences. Unfortunately it means that billions of you are splitting off every fraction of a second into discrete universes and it implies that everything possible exists in one universe or another. This comes up with its own set of hard-to-digest concepts, such as the fact that a 500-year-old you exists in some universes, whereas in others you died at birth.
Â· In 1997, Max Tegmark at Princeton University proposed an experiment to prove that MWI was correct. It involved pointing a loaded gun at your head and pulling the trigger. Of course, you will only survive in those universes where the gun, for whatever reason, fails to go off. If you get a misfire every time, you can satisfy yourself -- with an arbitrarily high level of confidence -- that MWI is true. Of course, in most universes your family will be weeping at your funeral (or possibly just shaking their heads and muttering).
Â· What happens to the cat? It's dead in half the subsequent universes and alive in the other half.
Â· Pilot Waves, Hidden Variables and the Implicate Order
Â· David Bohm (1917-1992) was a very brilliant physicist and that's why people went along with him when he came up with an elegant but more complicated theory to explain the same set of phenomena (normally, more complicated theories are disqualified by the principle known as Ockham's Razor).
Â· Bohm's theory follows on some original insights by Prince Louis de Broglie (1892-1987), who first studied the wave-like properties of the behaviour of particles in 1924. De Broglie suggested that, in addition to the normal wavefunction of the Copenhagen Interpretation, there is a second wave that determines a precise position for the particle at any particular time. In this theory, there is some 'hidden variable' that determines the precise position of the photon.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-21T01:03:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-easy-guide-to-optimal-road-quantum-mechanics.8821/post-101169>

Â· Sadly, John von Neumann (1903-1957) wrote a paper in 1932 proving that this theory was impossible. Von Neumann was such a great mathematician that nobody bothered to check his maths until 1966, when John Bell (1928-1990) proved he'd bodged it and there could be hidden variables after all -- but only if particles could communicate faster than light (this is called 'nonlocality'). In 1982 Alain Aspect demonstrated that this superluminal signaling did appear to exist, although David Mermin then showed that you could not actually signal anything. There is still some argument about whether this means very much.
Â· Bohm's theory was that the second wave was indeed faster than light, and moreover it did not get weaker with distance but instantly permeated the entire universe, acting as a guide for the movement of the photon. This is why it is called a 'pilot wave'.
Â· This theory explains the paradoxes of quantum physics perfectly. But it introduces a new faster-than-light wave and some hidden mechanism for deciding where it goes -- to create an 'implicate order'. That's quite a lot of extra baggage, and scientists like to travel light. Worse still, Bohm went on to become a mystic, identifying his 'implicate order' with Eastern spirituality and spawning books like Fritjof Capra's The Tao of Physics . That's heretical behaviour in the eyes of any decent physicist.
Â· What happens to the cat? It's either dead or alive, of course!
Â· Consistent Histories
Â· The Consistent Histories interpretation, put forward by Robert Griffiths in 1984, works backwards from the result of an experiment, arguing that only a few possible histories are consistent with the rules of quantum mechanics. It's an interesting idea but not very popular because it still doesn't explain how a particle can go through two slits and interfere with itself. Roland OmnÂs, in The Interpretation of Quantum Mechanics (1994) wrote down 80 equations in a single chapter and came to the conclusion that the 'consistent histories' interpretation was pretty much the same as Copenhagen, with a few knobs on.
Â· What happens to the Cat? Again, you're not supposed to ask.
Â· Alternate Histories
Â· The Alternate Histories Interpretation is quite different, being similar to the Many-Worlds Interpretation, but with the insistence that only the actual outcome is the real world and the ones we're not in don't actually exist. Unfortunately this gets us right back to their being some kind of 'collapse'.
Â· What happens to the cat? Again, you're not supposed to ask.
Â· Time Reversibility
Â· Richard Feynman (1918-1988) was a genius who developed a new approach to quantum mechanics. He formalised its crowning achievement, Quantum Electrodynamics, which is the most accurate scientific theory ever devised. He also developed the Feynman Diagram, which represents the interaction of two particles as the exchange of a third particle. This diagram has time on one axis and space on the other and the interaction can be viewed as happening both in forward and in reverse time.
Â· An electron, on its way from point A to point B, can bump into a photon. In the diagram this can be drawn as sending it backwards not just in space, but also in time. Then it bumps into another photon, which sends it forward in time again, but in a different direction in space. In this way, it can be in two places at once.
Â· There is little doubt that a Feynman diagram offers the easiest way to predict the results of a subatomic experiment. Many physicists have seen the power of this tool and taken the next step, arguing that reverse time travel is what actually happens in reality. Victor Stenger of the University of Hawaii argues strongly for this ontology in his forthcoming book. Of course, for a layman, it is hard to understand why a photon bounces around in such a way that it appears in two slits at once.
Â· What happens to the Cat? It is both dead and alive simultaneously. We don't see this because of the macroscopic 'measurement problem'.
Â· Transactional Interpretation
Â· Like Stenger's, John Cramer's Transactional Interpretation relies on the fundamental time-symmetry of the universe. He argues that particles perform a kind of 'handshake' in the course of interacting. One sends out a wave forward in time, and another sends one out backwards in time.
Â· What happens to the Cat? Ermm...
Â· Gremlins
Â· A new interpretation, presented for the first time here, is that there are little green gremlins hovering around, going backwards and forwards in time, shaking hands and collapsing with mirth as they poke and prod subatomic particles in a way they calculate most likely to confuse us. This explains all of the observed experimental results, but it does introduce gremlins, and the need for a further theory about why they should want to confuse us. Using the principle of Ockham's razor, this interpretation will probably not find much popularity among the scientific community although it may be the basis for a new religion. Watch this space.
Â· What happens to the Cat? Depends on what the gremlins think will confuse us most.

### Josh — 2013-12-24T19:42:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-easy-guide-to-optimal-road-quantum-mechanics.8821/post-104210>

Somewhat shaky ground at the base...reminds me of the story where pundits were reputedly asked "what holds the Earth up?" They were answered something like "Giant White Elephants". When someone was intelligent enough to dare ask "and what do the elephants stand on?" The story says they were told "well, more Giant White Elephants..." I would be careful about writing Bohm off as a "mystic" until you make the attempt to digest J. Krishnamurti, also known as JK (ironic perhaps?) yourself. Forgive me the suggestion if you already have. He spent 70 years trying to get people to just take a good clear look at things. I am humbly amused that all exceptional physicists seem to reach the edge of the outwardly messy world of "mystics", yet few enter to take a good look around. Until everyone makes friends, the comedy will no doubt continue with physicists saying "he got lost in religion and mysticism" and so-called mystics bemoaning that "tragically so and so could not transcend the limits of the "mind"". Having had a peek from both sides, it is a great game that has been going on for a very long time...I do not use this as an excuse to not grapple with the physics of biology, I just need the perspective so that the "terror of the situation" does not depress me so much. A "mystic" once said that the easiest thing to do in the physical universe was to remove the neutralizing forces in things. He warned that it was an entirely different thing to put them back. Most of physics still looks like removing the neutralizing force and seeing what happens. In our attempts to find a mechanism-based rationale for "functional medicine" in quantum physics we are still largely bound to experiments that take away equilibrium in systems to see what happens. The results are then extrapolated back to what might be "whole" or "healthy" or functional". The question of the role of the "observer" is central to where most "mystical" systems start. Yi Lu Ping An....

# Thread: Question about cholesterol for my mom

### QiGuy1997 — 2013-11-21T02:40:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-cholesterol-for-my-mom.8822/post-101187>

My mom has recently got some blood tests done just to check-up and the tests turned up high cholesterol at 265 mg/dl . Her triglycerides showed 121 mg/dl, her HDL was 75, her non-HDL was 190 (including 166 from LDL and 24 from VLDL). I was thinking these high levels may be caused by low thyroid however her TSH was normal at 2.00 so that is probably not it. Any ideas on what could be causing this/ tips on what to do?

### nonchalant — 2013-11-21T03:02:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-cholesterol-for-my-mom.8822/post-101193>

Nice HDL. I would be a bit concerned about the triglycerides though. Perhaps reduce carbs a bit?

### QiGuy1997 — 2013-11-21T06:40:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-cholesterol-for-my-mom.8822/post-101220>

I've already thrown away all vegetables oils in the house and she's cut down her carbs. I'm working on complete elimination of wheat and what not. Hopefully when she gets retested the trigs/LDL will be down a bit.

### nonchalant — 2013-11-21T16:42:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-cholesterol-for-my-mom.8822/post-101254>

> [QiGuy1997 said:](/goto/post?id=101220)
>
> I've already thrown away all vegetables oils in the house and she's cut down her carbs. I'm working on complete elimination of wheat and what not. Hopefully when she gets retested the trigs/LDL will be down a bit.

I would be very surprised if her trigs are not down for the next test then. You should be prepared, however, for an LDL level that doesn't change much, or even goes up, QiGuy. LDL, if it is not oxidized/damaged, is actually good. The damage to LDL is caused by carbs and artificial fats like that vegetable oil. Do some research on the various types of LDL. Only small, dense LDL is bad.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-21T20:40:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-cholesterol-for-my-mom.8822/post-101272>

Get her drinking more water too.....and on some reduced CoEnz Q10 to push the envelope

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hunting-the-magnetic-field-of-the-earth.8830/post-101296>

### QiGuy1997 — 2013-11-22T06:16:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/responses-to-ray-peats-conclusions.8834/post-101309>

If any of you are familiar with Dr. Ray Peat, you probably know that most of his conclusions are in direct opposition with the conclusions of Dr. Kruse. Many of these things are hard for me to refute so I'm hoping the intelligent people of this forum will be able to address some of the key differences between the two and why Peat's conclusions are so different from Jack's. I have a few of my own ideas but they are by no means developed or complete
1\. Ray Peat says ketosis/fat oxidation is bad. He says it releases stress hormones, kills thyroid function, stresses all the cells of the body, and releases free fatty acids which damage mitochondria, promote inflammation and reduce cellular respiration.
2\. A high simple sugar diet is ideal. Sugar metabolizes easily, produces protective CO2, helps produce thyroid hormones, fuels the brain, reduces stress hormones. He essentially considers glucose to be the antistress hormone.
3\. All PUFAs are toxic in all amounts. PUFAs, including dha and EPA from fish, destroy mitochondrial function, cause oxidative stress by creating lipid peroxidation products, cause cancer, damage the brain, damage reproductive tissues, reduce cellular respiration, suppress the immune system, and more. Fatty fish, nuts, all sources of PUFAs should be avoided.

Essentially, stay out of ketosis, burn mostly sugar and avoid all sources of PUFAs, including fatty fish. Here are a few of his articles if you want to read his research in order to better understand his position.
[http://raypeat.com/articles/articles/fats-functions-malfunctions.shtml](http://raypeat.com/articles/articles/fats-functions-malfunctions.shtml)
[http://raypeat.com/articles/articles/unsaturated-oils.shtml](http://raypeat.com/articles/articles/unsaturated-oils.shtml)
[http://raypeat.com/articles/articles/fats-degeneration3.shtml](http://raypeat.com/articles/articles/fats-degeneration3.shtml)
[http://raypeat.com/articles/articles/salt.shtml](http://raypeat.com/articles/articles/salt.shtml)
[http://raypeat.com/articles/articles/unsuitablefats.shtml](http://raypeat.com/articles/articles/unsuitablefats.shtml)
[http://raypeat.com/articles/articles/fishoil.shtml](http://raypeat.com/articles/articles/fishoil.shtml)

I really don't want to give up my PUFAs from seafood, but if anyone is able to convince me that DHA is poisoning my brain, cells, and mitochondria it would be Peat.

### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-03-01T08:31:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/responses-to-ray-peats-conclusions.8834/post-156969>

Dr. Kruse is right on the money with most of everything he says; especially everything in the CT and BG series, but that being said, anyone who hasn't thoroughly read Dr. Peat's work, particularly his older work and his books, is doing a great disservice to themselves. You don't have to accept every last thing someone says. For instance, I think epi-paleo ketosis works for me, and has the most direct and indirect (and observational and anecdotal) evidence behind it, as opposed to any other diet, really. _Especially in our modern nnEMF+blue light rich environment.
_
But, Dr. Peat has been speaking about things like nnEMF, static magnetic treatment devices, magnetized super cooled water, solar radiation and how solar and lunar cycles and changes alter human behavior and brain physiology, since about 1970. That's about 45 years now. He also went to Russia to study with some of the best Russian neurologists, neuro-anatomists/physiologists, gerontologists, sociologists, etc. and he wrote about their research on brain encephalization and things of that sort in his books. Furthermore, everyone here is aware of Gilbert Ling. Want to know something interesting? Dr. Peat and Gilbert Ling have been in personal contact for decades, Ling has openly stated that Dr. Peat's work on 'the organism' and cellular energetics is fantastic, and lastly, **Gilbert Ling and Dr. Peat are going to be in a documentary together** soon, along with the likes of Mae Wan Ho. So, he's really no quack. His older work is just better, and he seems to have had a lot of favorable experiences with things he experiments with personally, so he probably has some biases.... though everyone does.

_Also, “If cancers were werewolves, the cancer industry’s search for more refined killing technologies might be going in the right direction. But the genetic doctrine of cancer’s origin is just as mythical as werewolves and vampires.”
\-Ray Peat, PhD

What's not to love about that, lol?
_
I hope my statements don't give off any false notions; I'm a firm believer in everything we all discuss here, and not one of those "Kruse vs. Peat" people - I like to study everyone and gather as much as possible, and Dr. Kruse is currently - by far - ahead of most everyone there is, but that doesn't mean when it comes to people with seemingly "opposing" ideas that we have to throw the baby out with the bathwater.

### NeilBB — 2015-03-01T18:41:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/responses-to-ray-peats-conclusions.8834/post-156983>

I'm not sure about Peat specifically, but I know that even the greatest and most experienced thinkers become susceptible to over-generalizations of principles and false-conclusion drawing due to incomplete information or the inability or unwillingness to control for all the variables. They also become invested, both emotionally and economically over time in specific ideas and therefore become less amenable to change when presented with novel info that conflicts with their prior conclusions.

Translation: "You can't teach an old dog new tricks." Some truth to that, unfortunately. It's how we seem to be built...

I agree strongly, don't ever throw out the baby with the bathwater. The most important thinkers in history have always been those who have been able to distinguish the babies from the old bathwaters, and build upon that base. It is essential for progression of knowledge...

### Jack Kruse — 2015-03-01T18:50:05+0200

-
    I agree with you not to throw out the baby with the bath water with anyone else's idea's. I love to disturb others and my own conformity with curiosity.........but the better that a person disturbs conventional wisdom with wisdom the more likely they or I will change their point of view to get the story more correct then it was when the began. Wisdom is like a turning wheel in this case.............the more you ask in curiosity, the faster the destination appears if you follow the path of the wheel.........Few do this consistently, because it is a deficiency in thinking. -   People who fail in this application get results, and their life suffers from these results...........or you do because you followed them with out discernment.

-   Dogma of experts, slows the wheel or progress in any field.........and when you are aware of this.......you can plan for it in how you study any topic. That is how i approach things here on this forum. When I see something wrong........I point it out. And let others decide who is right.........The mark of an educated mind is to consider something you really don't believe and then decide the course of action.
    The job of an educator to is show the wisdom but not tell them people what it means. Your job is improve the quality of the processes of thinking..........because when you do they will find the pot of gold nature hold for us.

### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-03-01T19:52:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/responses-to-ray-peats-conclusions.8834/post-157008>

I don't disagree with anything you say above. As I stated previously, I think you're far ahead of the game. I follow all your blogs and am becoming a member soon. I'm slowly adding in your protocols. What I was alluding to, however, is that if anyone hasn't _thoroughly_ investigated Dr. Peat's work, especially his older work, they are doing themselves a disservice. He used to recommend high fat diets, and told me personally he ate 50-60% fat up until about 2-3 years ago. His ideas on DHA are not perfect, and I follow epi-paleo principles, so it should be obvious where my thoughts lie here; but his ideas on hormones, carbon dioxide, regeneration, etc. are flawless and are in line with what is discussed here. He's spoken about progesterone and DHEA and estrogen dominance and all that since the 70's, and like I said, he's also discussed nnEMF, magentized water, etc. since the 70's too. He has papers on circadian biology and circadian zeitgebers, etc. If diet < environment, than it shouldn't matter what foods he recommends, because he also recommends mitigating EMF, red light therapy, and all that good stuff.

Regardless, I don't care much what people do or don't think about Dr. Peat and his ideas. So I'm not going to defend him. I'm here to learn and apply the work of Dr. Kruse, so I'll likely not pay attention to this thread and get to reading Ubiquination 4

### Jack Kruse — 2015-03-01T20:00:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/responses-to-ray-peats-conclusions.8834/post-157012>

"Your ignorance cannot become dead by accumulating knowledge. You can accumulate all the
knowledge available in the world, you can become an Encyclopedia Britannica, but that won’t help.
You can become a walking encyclopedia, but your ignorance will not be dead through that. Rather,
on the contrary, your ignorance will become hidden, secret; it will move to the deep recesses of the
heart. So on the surface you will be knowledgeable and deep down you will remain ignorant. This is
what has happened, and all the universities go on helping this.
Your ignorance is never dead; it is alive, working. And just on the surface you are decorated, you
are a painted being. Your knowledge is painted just on the surface and deep down you remain
ignorant. The knowledge, real knowledge, can happen only when the ignorance is dead. Before
that, knowledge will remain information – borrowed, not yours, not authentic – it has not happened
to you. It is not a lived experience, but only words, verbal, scriptural."
Vedanta: Seven Steps to Samadhi-Osho

### Jack Kruse — 2015-03-01T20:01:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/responses-to-ray-peats-conclusions.8834/post-157013>

"ignorance
is a mode of life, not a question of information. It is a way of life, a wrong way of life, that creates
ignorance. It is not just a question of memory, of how much you know, or how much you don’t know
– that is not the point."

Osho

Via Josh Ferber........very appropriate for this thread. There is a deep lesson here. I'm not interested in being liked........just correct. And my curiosity always is my best critic.

### patgrif@hotmail.com — 2015-03-02T20:48:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/responses-to-ray-peats-conclusions.8834/post-157190>

> [NeilBB said:](/goto/post?id=156983)
>
> I'm not sure about Peat specifically, but I know that even the greatest and most experienced thinkers become susceptible to over-generalizations of principles and false-conclusion drawing due to incomplete information or the inability or unwillingness to control for all the variables. They also become invested, both emotionally and economically over time in specific ideas and therefore become less amenable to change when presented with novel info that conflicts with their prior conclusions.
>
> Translation: "You can't teach an old dog new tricks." Some truth to that, unfortunately. It's how we seem to be built...
>
> I agree strongly, don't ever throw out the baby with the bathwater. The most important thinkers in history have always been those who have been able to distinguish the babies from the old bathwaters, and build upon that base. It is essential for progression of knowledge...

Neil said above: **"The most important thinkers in history have always been those who have been able to distinguish the babies from the old bathwaters, and build upon that base. It is essential for progression of knowledge..."**

### Tracy Guild — 2017-05-21T18:35:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/responses-to-ray-peats-conclusions.8834/post-214380>

No guru is ever 100% right. If they were, they would never change and grow and therefore would not be able to manage a changing world. I am less discouraged by the differences of opinion and more encouraged by the cross over agreements. Peat is a genius and I have learned so much reading his work on hormones, thyroid, liver and cells. I find so much value in it and validation of Kruse's work regarding biophysics. Peat is understated, and I think most people make generalizations from his work without reading the bulk and really listening to or understanding his meaning. They are in total agreement regarding sun, circadian biology, the ever important relationship between energy and water and emf. Peat advocates eating oysters and shellfish, organ meats. The main difference is the discussion about carbohydrates/fats and Kruse often says this depends on your n=1, so I don't at all see why these perspectives cannot exist in the same discussion. In several years time, Kruse helped me to get rid of arthritis, improve my vision and sleep better. Peat has recently helped me to overcome estrogen dominance and improve my thyroid function. I am keeping both. Love to both and to everyone trying to get their healthiest. Read all the things.

I currently eat similar to this.

Breakfast

75g raw liver
Half a tablespoon of coconut oil
Pomegranate
Red grapes
Apple
Salmon
Dulse
Raw Turmeric
Raw Ginger

Lunch

Raw smoked salmon
Mussels
Cockles
Prawns
4 x oysters
Kelp

Dinner

100g raw heart
Steak
Broccoli
Asparagus
Herbs
Bacon
Mushrooms

Cheers

### Inger — 2013-11-22T18:00:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-testosterone.8842/post-101373>

Nude tanning in real sunshine, no sunscreen. It must be completely nude, whole body. As much as you can. It is awesome for rising testosterone naturally... IMHO the most effective single thing you can do. Do it in fall and spring too in addition to whole summer.

CT. Icebaths and also just having it colder overall, like mild CT. Also an amazing tool... in combination with minding light cycles/avoiding fake light after sunset and a ketogenic diet.

There are some supps/herbs that are said to raise testosteron if it is low, like Ashwagandha, Boron, tribulus-terrestris... etc. but IDK if they work. I would go for the two above. I bet earthing and being in the nature are amazing too! And yes some heavy lifting once in a while should help too....

### Huck — 2013-11-26T13:35:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-testosterone.8842/post-101671>

Understand that this is my interpretation of what I have learned here, backed up by my experience (n=1).

You can try to supplement your way to better health, but without regular labs and the knowledge to interpret them, it is likely to be a waste of money. Some things, like vitamin d, are easy to supplement and easy to test, at least in US. Other things, like testosterone, are more challenging. I had serious problems when, with my doctor, we had tried to raise testosterone through supplements. But I was able to raise my testosterone just by getting lots of sun, without the problems I had with supplements.

The long term (18-24 months) approach is to follow Jack's protocols and get your "supplements" from natural foods. What many of us have seen is small changes that add up to big changes over time. The next 24 months will happen whether you follow Jack's prescriptions or not, so why not follow them and see what happens?

You need electrons and photons. Spend lots of time in the sun and ground whenever you can. Drink spring water and eat organic food. Minimize emf. I think you and I have the same problem with cold. I can't stand to be immersed in cold water, but spot icing isn't a problem. Maybe that is what you can start with.

I wonder if a trip to Stonehenge would help? At a minimum, it would help you focus on possibilities!

Being on this board, first reflex is to examine your lifestyle and environment and adjust it the best you can to follow natures laws as explained to us by dr Kruse.

You may have pituitary problems, but in the mean time your LH @ FSH are highish, but your testicles are not responding as expected.

but
should add HCG-shot=250iuEOD to prevent testicular and scrotum atrophy.

Magnesium Malate------gets to skeletal muscles and cardiac muscles 800mg/bed time

### nonchalant — 2013-12-08T17:13:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-testosterone.8842/post-102861>

Usually a chelated magnesium will have two numbers. The mg of the total dose, mg and chelate combined, and also the mg of just the magnesium alone, the lesser amount. You can usually find that on the nutritional info panel, with the %DV.

### Shijin13 — 2013-11-23T16:16:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/muscle-repair-success.8852/post-101473>

SO Last week I tore my calf muscle. quite severely, initially thought I tore my achilles tendon but didn't I've been pushing all of JAck's protocols hard. Yesterday I went into the chiropractor - to get adjusted and told her what happened. When she examined my calf - she was surprised how much healing had already occurred. in just 10 days from the injury.

She could tell where the tear happened - but she was surprised at how quickly things are repairing. She's never seen anyone heal from a tear as sever as I had - I basically shredded the both calf muscles; she said with all previous patients they needed crutches/canes for 3-4 weeks before being able to walk w/o assistance. She asked me what else was I doing besides Icing/CTing (She calls me her Ice client b/c of all the CT I do) she attributed the fast healing to CT, diet, and hydration.

she told me I needed to bottle it and sell what every I was doing - I told her its all out there on JAck's website. She thinks I'm already at 85% healed in only 10 days. I'm going to continue limiting my leg workouts for the next month but get back in the grove for leg work in Jan but I'll keep doing all of JAck's protocols.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-11-23T18:31:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/muscle-repair-success.8852/post-101477>

Great job on your rehab!! Of course we know that Jack's protocalls work. Your recovery is a great example.

As I read your post, the first thought I had was ... where all the things you do correctly, why did the injury happen? .... Most likely your field (maybe more incentive to make the move your want?)

Finally, I suggest making sure your calves are totally healed before resuming workouts. Likely going to be some scar tissue there. You might want to look into the Graston Technique. I recently started Graston through a chiropractor. In a short period of time, it has broken up scar tissue that massage therapy didn't impact. [http://grastontechnique.com/LocateaProvider.html](http://grastontechnique.com/LocateaProvider.html) .... Google 'Graston Technique' and you can find some good videos that show how it works.

Angie - I think there was a podcast from Dr. Bonlie (Magnetico) in last year's Optimal Reset where he talked about magnets and healing.

IIRC, he said use of magnets should follow acupuncture rules in terms of frequency and duration of use. The reason is that if you have limited resources, your body may prefer to heal higher priority damage before healing say a wrist or an ankle.

I think a good guide to the order of healing priority is the reverse order of what the body shuts down in sequence to preserve life - beginning with vasoconstriction of extremeties to preserve organs, then musculo-skeletal, then finally the heart and brain.

A classical example is bodybuilders who divert available resources to muscle hypertrophy - resources that may have been needed for more vital functions.

# Thread: a few questions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-questions.8853/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Power98dan — 2013-11-23T17:44:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-questions.8853/post-101476>

Hello all

I started reading about Dr Kruse and Leptin reset on the MDA monster thread. Not sure how I got there Read not all post but quite a few, then of course I was led here and I have again read a lot. I think I have the basics down, 50plus grams of protein for breakfast, less then 30 grams of carbs if you have more then 30 lbs to lose, no snacking, try and wait 4-5 hrs between meals, spread your carbs thru out the day, don't be afraid of good fats. I should also state I am around 90 lbs over weight, very familiar with paleo and have ate this way off and on for a couple of years. I also have tried Bullet Proof diet with BPC and IF. It worked but... My problem always leads back to sustainability, thus I think if I can get thru the reset, i can sustain this way of eating vs BPC everyday (although I do like BPC).

So far no issues, sometimes a little light headed or carb flu symptoms, but not many really (any sups to aide this if necessary?) I am fortunate I don't really have any issues other then fat. I guess one more issue would be poor sleep, some of this is caused by Rotor cuff injury more on that later, but some is just poor sleep

Thus some questions

1\. I assume if I still want BPC in the am with MCT oil or butter or CO this is OK as long as I eat my protein and don't snack?
2\. I assume I could eat a BPC for lunch, could this be in place of protein if this got me thru to dinner?
3\. Leptin sensitivity, I am still a little confused at how I can tell if my Leptin has been reset. I get the no cravings, after a very solid week of eating this way I have no cravings, but the sweating issue I am not sure if I am suppose to sweat more or less or how I am suppose to tell, I stopped exercising as the Dr suggest, thus how can I tell?
4 Sometimes I don't want any carbs period, but sometimes i do want a piece of fruit, i know fruit is not the best choice during reset, but if carbs are still less then 30 is this ok?

Been lurking for about a week, so far a pound a day weight loss

Side question back in May I lifted the trash the wrong way, and I tore a rotor cuff, went to chiropractor he agrees with my diagnoses and said it could take a long time to heal, and if it did not you may need an operation. It has been 6 months and it is not really getting much better. I don't want to get operation if at all possible. It feels better if I ice it, or apply DSMO, or if I take motrin or such, but I can't live on motrin.

Is there any supplements or anything at all I can eat or do that might help?

Final question, so much information on the website, would like to start reading some of the blogs and even maybe a webinar, again no side issues with health, most interested in weight loss and sleep any suggestions what blog post, webinars or podcast to start with?

Thanks in advance for your answers.

Dan

### caroline — 2013-11-23T21:58:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-questions.8853/post-101491>

Hi Dan and welcome! That is a lot of questions! yikes!

firstly - please start a journal .... it gives Dr. Kruse context and is great for commitment. Please tell us about yourself....
To find the journal section ... go to the top of this page and click on forum and then go down 5 categories and you will see journals. just start a new thread. I will watch for you!

Please read the easy start guides on the home page to make sure you get the details correct. It is pretty simple really.

so.....you can, of course, have a BPC WITH your BAB - it helps to get your fat/protein up. If that is what you have for lunch - fine. Make sure you eat dinner at least 4 hours before bed..and definitely no snacking!

I am not sure where you got the spreading the carbs thing from .... Only eat fruit in long lite cycles - when it is grown - the same for any carbs really. For me - I finally figured out fruit is just a sugar craving - so it is easier not to have any.

For your BAB you might have to even eat more protein and fat than 50 gr. I did and I am not a big woman. The idea is to eat a big enough BAB to knock out cravings... and it absolutely works!!!

Firstly - just make sure you are doing the Lepin RX using an epi paleo template correctly - there is a webinar.
Drink only spring water or R/O water...

Jack's podcast are great for getting quick information in an easy to understand format ... they are listed on the home page.
There is also a free webinar by Dr. Tim Jackson. If you can swing it - get the bootcamp ...it has been outstanding. Dr. Jackson is very easy to understand and he is covering a lot of ground in great detail
.

Do you have Dr. K's book? The Epi- Paleo RX: The prescription for Disease Reversal and Optimal health
it is available on Amazon in paper or kindle. It explains all of this in simpler terms and there is a great section on labs.

You definitely need Cold thermogenesis C/T ...for your shoulder ... there is an easy start guide and a webinar.

Please read a post today by Shiijin about this very thing.

Please don't worry about being Leptin sensitive yet .... you have other bigger fish to fry!

Your number one priority has to be to control the conditions of your existence .... what is your EMF exposure? at home? at work?

Get as much sunshine and grounding as possible....

Did I answer all your questions???? just keep reading and asking questions......there is a lot here to take in

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-23T23:31:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-questions.8853/post-101502>

1\. Leptin Rx
2\. CT protocol......
3\. Limit blue light at night.
4\. eat lots of pastured pork and eggs for the first 4-6 weeks add seafood at night.
5\. 1 to 1.5 gallons of non fluoridated water or spring water............

Dont ask.........I read all your stuff......that is what you need to consider. No booze or wine either......when you rise sun in your eyes. not behind any glass. you need to eat the sun (hint at something that is coming on the blog.

I've seen Dr. K write about the connection between vit A and vit D cycles when talking about dandruff. I am suffering from dandruff and hair loss currently so I've been thinking about what could help solve this, and remembered something Ray Peat wrote about concerning the topic of dandruff. He said that whenever he increased his metabolic rate he noticed significant dry skin, dry eyes and dandruff and he found that high doses of vit A took care of this every time.

My question is....Since eating so much saturated fat in winter months and having such a high metabolic rate during this time, could it be possible that we need that much extra vit A during the winter months as compared to summer?

It's hard to eat that much beef liver. Could a fermented cod liver oil supplement in the winter possibly be a good solution?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dandruff-hair-loss-and-vit-a.8858/post-102743>

The cure for excessive dandruff is easy.

Simply mix 3 or 4 drops of Lugols 5% iodine into a little water ( about 1 ounce of water ) and wet the scalp. Severe dandruff may need this treatment repeated for a few days. Dandruff instantly disappears for several months. Repeat procedure as needed to keep dandruff away. I find that I need this treatment 3 to 4 times a year.

This is far less expensive and more effective than dandruff shampoos. Also more natural. Every cell in the human body needs iodine, including our skin cells. This is an amazing treatment for dandruff, since I have been suffering extreme dandruff my entire life, and this treatment is the most effective I've ever tried, and I've tried everything. Amazingly, this treatment has also reversed my hair loss.

Grizz

# Thread: The Paleo fallacy of the land based food chain
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-paleo-fallacy-of-the-land-based-food-chain.8866/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-25T03:25:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-paleo-fallacy-of-the-land-based-food-chain.8866/post-101578>

Synthesis of arachidonic acid (20:4ω6) and docosahexaenoic acid (22:6ω3) from their precursors, linoleic acid (18:2ω6) and α-linolenic acid (18:3ω3), respectively. Plant seeds are rich in linoleic acid and green leaves in α-linolenic acid. Meat, eggs, and human milk are rich in arachidonic acid, while fish, shellfish, eggs, and small mammals are rich in docosahexaenoic acid. Docosahexaenoic acid is the precursor to neuroprotectins while arachidonic acid is the precursor to eicosanoids and leukotrienes which, on balance, are commonly associated with inflammatory processes. Conversion of linoleic acid and α-linolenic acid downstream is very slow. Synthesis of docosahexaenoic acid involves peroxisomes.
Natureâ€™s preference for DHA in the brain is strikingly demonstrated in large, vegetarian land mammals. The omega-3 (ω3) DPA is the dominant ω3 PUFA found in non-neural tissues and is thus abundantly available in the body, much more so than DHA (Crawford et al., 1969). However, neural membranes even in these large mammals still specifically conserved DHA. During evolution of the land mammals, this retention of DHA composition was associated with economy in brain size relative to the body, with a logarithmic reduction as they evolved larger bodies (Crawford et al., 1993). The structure of the brain is 60% lipid. Neural cells have a particularly high membrane content of DHA. In different mammalian species, the content of both arachidonic acid (20:4 ω6, AA) and DHA does not vary: it is brain size that varies (Crawford et al., 1976, 1993; Figs.2.1 and 2.3). DHA is rapidly and selectively incorporated in neural membranes and is concentrated at synaptic signaling sites (Suzuki et al., 1997). It is the most unsaturated of cell membrane fatty acids (Jump, 2002). Some DHA can be synthesized from alpha (α)-linolenic acid, but the process is highly ratelimited (Sprecher, 1993, Sprecher et al., 1999; Plourde and Cunnane, 2007) and, moreover,α-linolenic acid is oxidized at a very rapid rate of 60% in 24h compared with only5% for DHA (Leyton et al., 1987; Freemantle et al., 2006). In 1972, Crawford and Sinclair first published evidence that DHA itself was an independent determinant of brain growth and evolution (Broadhurst et al., 2002). DHAdeficiency studies in rodents (Sinclair and Crawford, 1972; Benolken et al., 1973; Galliand Socini, 1983; Weisinger et al., 1999; Catalan et al., 2002), chickens (Budowski et al., 1987), and primates (Fiennes et al., 1973; Neuringer et al., 1986), and visual and cognitivetrials in human infants (Carlson and Werkman, 1996; Martinez and Vasquez, 1998; Birchet al., 2000) support the concept that DHA is essential to brain development and function. Moreover, in collaboration with Professor Pierre Budowski at the Hebrew University of Jerusalem, we described competition existing between ω6 and ω3 PUFA and showed thattheir balance is critical for brain development and structural integrity (Budowski and Crawford, 1985).

BOOM!!

Human Brain Evolution, 2010: Cunanne and Crawford.

### JanSz — 2013-11-26T23:54:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-paleo-fallacy-of-the-land-based-food-chain.8866/post-101694>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=101578)
>
> Synthesis of arachidonic acid (20:4ω6) and docosahexaenoic acid (22:6ω3) from their precursors, linoleic acid (18:2ω6) and α-linolenic acid (18:3ω3), respectively. Plant seeds are rich in linoleic acid and green leaves in α-linolenic acid. Meat, eggs, and human milk are rich in arachidonic acid, while fish, shellfish, eggs, and small mammals are rich in docosahexaenoic acid. Docosahexaenoic acid is the precursor to neuroprotectins while arachidonic acid is the precursor to eicosanoids and leukotrienes which, on balance, are commonly associated with inflammatory processes. Conversion of linoleic acid and α-linolenic acid downstream is very slow. Synthesis of docosahexaenoic acid involves peroxisomes.
> Nature’s preference for DHA in the brain is strikingly demonstrated in large, vegetarian land mammals. The omega-3 (ω3) DPA is the dominant ω3 PUFA found in non-neural tissues and is thus abundantly available in the body, much more so than DHA (Crawford et al., 1969). However, neural membranes even in these large mammals still specifically conserved DHA. During evolution of the land mammals, this retention of DHA composition was associated with economy in brain size relative to the body, with a logarithmic reduction as they evolved larger bodies (Crawford et al., 1993). The structure of the brain is 60% lipid. Neural cells have a particularly high membrane content of DHA. In different mammalian species, the content of both arachidonic acid (20:4 ω6, AA) and DHA does not vary: it is brain size that varies (Crawford et al., 1976, 1993; Figs.2.1 and 2.3). DHA is rapidly and selectively incorporated in neural membranes and is concentrated at synaptic signaling sites (Suzuki et al., 1997). It is the most unsaturated of cell membrane fatty acids (Jump, 2002). Some DHA can be synthesized from alpha (α)-linolenic acid, but the process is highly ratelimited (Sprecher, 1993, Sprecher et al., 1999; Plourde and Cunnane, 2007) and, moreover,α-linolenic acid is oxidized at a very rapid rate of 60% in 24h compared with only5% for DHA (Leyton et al., 1987; Freemantle et al., 2006). In 1972, Crawford and Sinclair first published evidence that DHA itself was an independent determinant of brain growth and evolution (Broadhurst et al., 2002). DHAdeficiency studies in rodents (Sinclair and Crawford, 1972; Benolken et al., 1973; Galliand Socini, 1983; Weisinger et al., 1999; Catalan et al., 2002), chickens (Budowski et al., 1987), and primates (Fiennes et al., 1973; Neuringer et al., 1986), and visual and cognitivetrials in human infants (Carlson and Werkman, 1996; Martinez and Vasquez, 1998; Birchet al., 2000) support the concept that DHA is essential to brain development and function. Moreover, in collaboration with Professor Pierre Budowski at the Hebrew University of Jerusalem, we described competition existing between ω6 and ω3 PUFA and showed thattheir balance is critical for brain development and structural integrity (Budowski and Crawford, 1985).
>
> BOOM!!
>
> Human Brain Evolution, 2010: Cunanne and Crawford.

Two questions:
1; **how to get rid of excess EPA**? I used to eat fish & krill oils. For the last almost two years I know better, but my EPA is continuously overwhelming my Omega3's
my EPA=348 instead of desirable 57

2; Specifically, how to calculate Omega6/Omega3 ratio?
Assumed ideal test results= all analytes are exactly in mid range of desirable range of values, I calculated
TotalOmega6/TotalOmega3 =11.9
and
AA/DHA=7.2
none of those ratios are close to 4.

there is a misinformation going on that results in people eating (Omega3) supplements:
fish oils, krill oils, EPA, DHA (which is generally, most often, wrong, unless supported by blood analysis)

**Quote from Ed Kane**
#1---excess of any kind of omega3 will suppress Omega6, specially AA, Arachidonic Acid (which is critically important)
#2---excess of Omega6 WILL NOT suppress omega3

Results below are based on LabCorp/Mayo Clinic analysis

Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dna-not-always-destiny.8870/post-101586>

This gives new meaning to the warning in genealogy that you never know who you might find hanging from the family tree.

[http://blogs.smithsonianmag.com/sci...scientist-who-discovered-he-was-a-psychopath/](http://blogs.smithsonianmag.com/science/2013/11/the-neuroscientist-who-discovered-he-was-a-psychopath/)

_Why has Fallon been able to temper his behavior, while other people with similar genetics and brain turn violent and end up in prison? Fallon was once a self-proclaimed genetic determinist, but his views on the influence of genes on behavior have evolved. He now believes that his childhood helped prevent him from heading down a scarier path.

â€œI was loved, and that protected me,â€￾ he says. Partly as a result of a series of miscarriages that preceded his birth, he was given an especially heavy amount of attention from his parents, and he thinks that played a key role._

While MTHFR isn't named, it can be associated with multiple miscarriages.

His DNA apparently determined the architecture of his brain and yet his epigenetics could overcome even that.
