### HoneyChild — 2013-11-25T13:26:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-101599>

Basically I'm 31, have no libido and intercourse is painful. I get swollen toes from time to time and this summer developed xanthelasma around my eyes (basically little orange blobs under my skin). This is supposed to be related to oxLDL and heart disease!

I started leptin reset this time last year along with cold showers and occasional cold baths. I've always been naturally slim however I ended up losing more weight than I expected and now I think I'm underweight. Whenever I do cold baths I seem to lose even more which scares me and I stop doing them.

I also started having problems with waking up in the night hot and thirsty but this appears to have stopped since I cut back on the fruit about a month ago.

I do my best to keep technology to a minimum, I work from home, don't have wifi or cordless phones. My magnetic field at night is very low and I have a magnet sitting on the floor under my bed. I wear UVEX glasses in the evening.

The only supplements I take are maca and magnesium spray on my feet.

The problems with where I live are: live couple of flights up, city population about 1.2 million, nearest cell phone masts are about 1/4 mile away. We're desperate to move however I'm struggling to find the ideal home away from other houses.

Link to my test results and brainstorming...

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?8790-Labs-in-Is-it-my-field-or-what](http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?8790-Labs-in-Is-it-my-field-or-what)

### HoneyChild — 2013-11-25T13:31:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-101601>

It looks like H. Pylori has been linked to xanthelasma, elevated cholesterol and raynaud's...

**An uncommon lesion: gastric xanthelasma**

Xanthelasmas were found to be associated with chronic gastritis, gastrointestinal anastomoses, intestinal metaplasia, and H. pylori infection.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/16245231](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/16245231)

> Infections
>
> Another cause of high cholesterol profile is infection. Multiple studies have shown associations between bacterial infections like Chlamydia pneumoniae and H. pylori, which is the bacterium causes duodenal ulcers, and viral infections like herpes and cytomegalovirus and elevated lipids. (6) For example, H. pylori leads to elevated levels of total cholesterol, LDL cholesterol, lipoprotein (a), ApoB or LDL particle number, and triglyceride concentrations as well as decreased levels of HDL. (7)
>
> Several mechanisms have been proposed to explain the association between infections and elevated blood lipids. Some evidence suggests that viral and bacterial infections directly alter the lipid metabolism of infected cells, and other evidence suggests that lipids increase as a result of the body’s attempt to fight off infection. Other evidence suggests that LDL has antimicrobial properties and is directly involved in inactivating microbial pathogens. This has been confirmed by studies showing that mice with defective LDL receptors—and thus very high levels of LDL—are protected against infection by gram-negative bacteria like H. pylori. (8)

[http://chriskresser.com/what-causes-elevated-ldl-particle-number](http://chriskresser.com/what-causes-elevated-ldl-particle-number)

**Helicobacter pylori eradication ameliorates primary Raynaud's phenomenon.**

The study shows that H. pylori eradication causes a significant decrease in clinical attacks of Raynaud's disease. The reduction of vasoactive substances determined by the eradication of the bacterium may be the pathogenetic mechanism underlying the phenomenon.
[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/9724144](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/9724144)

Use the heads for smoothie and make something nice from the rest? That is how I do. That way you will not have to drink large amounts of the yuckiness.. and you get a nice tasting dish to enjoy after you get the smoothie down! Also, to pour the smoothie through a sieve helps big (you might need to help and push with a spoon or it will not go through... at least if it is thicker). That way it feels like a rich fatty chocolate when you drink it with closed nose. I always close my nose. I taste nothing. I imagine it is chocolate! And with nose still closed I rinse my mouth and put something nice tasting in it and only then I stop holding my nose.
Makes the whole thing not yucky at all. And you get the very best part of the fish raw  eyes, brain...

Think about it like your medicine. Your hormone mood booster! That is what I do. I just had pieces of raw heart and liver with pieces of raw beef fat for breakfast. Nothing else. Just plain, fingerfood. It was yummy! And how it makes me feel... priceless!

Never think something is not possible, there is always a way

### HoneyChild — 2013-12-01T18:07:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-101965>

The problem is that I absolutely detest the taste of sardines, so I have no idea how to "enjoy" them. I hoped that drinking them as a smoothie would be the answer.

I love smoked fish like mackerel and herring but they don't come with the heads, so I'm not really sure what to do. I thought of buying some flat fish like plaice or dab and using their heads for a smoothie. I'm sure that would work too.

I noticed that the sardine smoothies I had yesterday were surprisingly filling and I didn't end up eating dinner until 7pm which is very late for me in the winter. I simply forgot about food. I guess it's the electrons

I'm also relieved that the smoothie didn't make me vomit, I've vomited so many times this year it's not funny. I think sometimes it's because of the raw meat and fish I've eaten. My body needs more time to adjust to this new way of eating it seems. I ate raw lamb chops a few weeks ago and woke in the middle of the night to be sick and was ill all day. So I'm now a bit more cautious about raw stuff, even although I have no problems with actually eating it.

Raw lamb heart is absolutely lovely and tastes like steak to me, although for some reason I hate it when it's cooked. I'm going to see if my local butcher has some heart tomorrow.

I was just thinking if you have gut issues ........

Maybe I have said this before..... sorry if I am repeating myself ....... Dr. Jackson considers that 95% of people have gut issues - and to not even bother testing at first - just start fixing your gut. I worry about people trying to eat raw unless their stomach bacteria can handle it.

of course I am no expert ..... not even close.

### HoneyChild — 2013-12-14T16:32:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-103401>

In October I started studying for a degree in health science. There are 7 books in this module and at the end of each book we have to do an online test plus there are a few written assessments too. The first online test I did I rushed it a bit and only got 63% however with the other 2 I printed the questions off and took my time. I ended up getting 92% and 100%

I really only need to get at least 40% to get into the final exam and it's the result I get in the exam that really matters. So hopefully I can continue to do as well as I have been so far.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

This is quite a big deal for me really. I've never considered myself an academic type. I remember in primary school I never did well at all. For some subjects we'd be split up into bottom, middle and top groups and I was invariably in the bottom group. I remember thinking it was strange that all the youngest kids in the class were in the bottom group but all the oldest kids were in the top. It didn't seem fair to me and I was the youngest. One of my friends was 13 months older than me!

I'd also come home with report cards saying things like "can barely cope with the most basic of work." No matter how hard I tried it made no difference, it was very demoralising. By the time I got into secondary school I felt there wasn't much point in trying, "I have two options, either I can try hard and get nowhere or I can not bother and still get nowhere." I choose the second option.

So I suppose the point in all this is that we can can change, nothing is set in stone.

### caroline — 2013-12-15T01:33:56+0200

I am so happy to hear you are fully engaged now .... and what awesome marks! once you get some of your issues sorted - you are going to soar. Do you know that you could likely do consults with Dr. Jackson? at least I can. Email him and see if he deals with a lab where you are. Even though I have a great Doc and he is very interested in all this - he just doesn't know enough but he sure is willing to listen and excited to follow along with what Dr. Jackson will do. I always have copies of all tests sent to him and then I make an appointment and we go over them. He always tells me how much he learns. Other then that - he just simply doesn't have time - he is run off his feet! It is not his fault - he was never taught any of this stuff!

I think all of us have thrown so much money at supplements and various protocols and doctors etc. etc. ....and still not getting answers. After listening to Dr. Tim's bootcamp \[and all the things that Dr. Kruse is teaching us\] - he is giving us so much information and a plan to help ourself.

### HoneyChild — 2013-12-17T21:26:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-103678>

We're spending Christmas Eve with my family and have been asked to make a starter (not a soup) and then we're spending Christmas with my BF's family and I'm making a dessert so I've been trying to find recipes that are paleo-friendly that everyone can enjoy.

So far I've done a practice run of these recipes and they turned out pretty good. I made the frittatas in muffin trays but mine must be smaller than in the recipe as I ended up making more than I expected. I might just make one big one and cut them up instead on the day.

Mini mushroom and spring onion frittatas
[http://www.goodtoknow.co.uk/recipes/537066/mini-mushroom-and-spring-onion-frittatas](http://www.goodtoknow.co.uk/recipes/537066/mini-mushroom-and-spring-onion-frittatas)

The chocolate mousse was amazing and I used dark brown sugar as there's supposed to be more minerals, of course this is only a treat not something to have every day! Surprisingly easy to make and seriously delicious.

Makes 2

2 medium eggs
60g chocolate (at least 70% cocoa)
2 tsp sugar (or to taste) - **I used just over 1 teaspoon**

1\. Break the chocolate into pieces and put in a bowl over, but not touching, a pan of simmering water. When the chocolate begins to melt, turn the heat off. Separate the eggs.

2\. Whisk the egg whites into soft peaks, add the sugar, and whisk briefly.

3\. Mix the egg yolks quickly into the melted chocolate and then whisk in a third of the egg white. Fold the rest very gently into the mixture until just combined (be careful not to overmix), and then put into bowls and refrigerate for at least four hours until set.

[http://www.theguardian.com/lifeandstyle/wordofmouth/2010/jul/22/how-to-make-chocolate-mousse](http://www.theguardian.com/lifeandstyle/wordofmouth/2010/jul/22/how-to-make-chocolate-mousse)

### HoneyChild — 2013-12-20T15:31:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-103909>

The other day I bought some sprats and made them into smoothies over the last couple of days. I also bought a dozen langoustines and ate them for dinner last night. I read that you can boil the shells up, liquidise them and the strain them through a sieve. So that's what I did today and it didn't quite go to plan.

I put the shells and liquid in the food processor but I must've done something wrong because the liquid kept shooting out the sides of the lid. No matter what I did water just kept getting everywhere, on the work surfaces, all over the floor... and worst of all - all over me!

So I transferred what was left into the blender I make my smoothies in and that was OK. Finally I now have langoustine broth and will use it to make some sort of soup once my BF comes home with some vegetables.

Sometimes I seriously wonder why I do all of this! LOL.

### HoneyChild — 2013-12-22T17:19:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-104042>

On Friday night I thought I'd go through the property listings again to see if there's anything I've accidentally missed... sadly there was nothing. So I started looking at the really expensive houses (hey a girl's allowed to dream isn't she??). I saw a 6 bedroom Georgian house that looked like it came straight out of a Jane Austen novel. Then I noticed that it's being sold in two lots...

Lot 1 - 6 bed Georgian house, cottage, stables, tennis court and 20 acres of land
Lot 2 - 3 bedroom house with 1 acre

The small house is at the top end of our budget but looks pretty much perfect for us. I phoned the estate agent to make sure it's actually for sale and was told that the owners are living in the small house and would prefer to sell the whole estate as one lot or sell the big house first. She took my name and phone number anyway.

I then checked the website for where all the mobile phone masts are and my heart sank, there are 2 masts nearby. Although when I measured the distance it's about 700 meters which is about 0.4 miles. AFAIK you're supposed to be at least 0.25 miles away so I supposed it's not too bad.

Here's a screenshot, the arrow points to the house and the two blue markers indicate where the masts are.

### HoneyChild — 2014-01-09T13:45:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-105146>

Finally got my 23andme results back and it seems I have the following problems

Homozygous (+/+)
MTHFR C677T
MAO-A R297R
MTRR A66G
MTRR A664A
CBS A360A

Heterozygous:
VDR Bsm
VDR Taq
BHMT-08

Health risks:

T2D - 28.8% (compared to 20.7% average)
Age-related macular degeneration - 22% (compared to 7%)
Psoriasis - 15% (compared to 10.1%)

I also carry one gene for Hemochromatosis.

### HoneyChild — 2014-01-09T14:17:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-105147>

It turns out I'm also resistant to norovirus, which I find very interesting. Over a year ago my BF was very ill with it yet I didn't get the slightest symptom.

### HoneyChild — 2014-01-13T17:01:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-105546>

Still having fun clicking around my 23andme account. Apparently in Asian-American women having this result means you're not likely to see a reduced risk of breast cancer from drinking black or green tea. It looks like it's because my COMT enzyme works pretty quickly.

COMT V158M rs4680 GG

"Tea contains compounds called polyphenols that are thought to prevent certain cancers. A protein encoded by the gene COMT is capable of deactivating some polyphenols, and different versions of the SNP rs4680 in this gene are known to affect activity levels of the protein. The authors of this study examined whether some versions of this SNP render tea more or less protective against breast cancer. They genotyped 589 Asian-American women with breast cancer and 563 healthy controls and compared their tea intake and genotypes. The authors found that women who drank black or green tea had about half the odds of breast cancer compared to those who didnâ€™tâ€”but only if they also had at least one A at rs4680. Women with the GG genotype did not have any protection against breast cancer from drinking tea."

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/query.fcgi?cmd=Search&db=PubMed&term=14612555](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/query.fcgi?cmd=Search&db=PubMed&term=14612555)

### HoneyChild — 2014-01-22T21:13:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-106639>

Despite the fact that fish smoothies are pretty damned disgusting I still feel strangely compelled to continue drinking them. I don't know why, it's not like I feel anything in particular. I just keep thinking about them and wondering when I'll have them next or when I should be buying more.

I'm down to my last 4 sprats (very young herring) so I'll need to buy more, I can't bear the though of running out of them. It's so strange, I'm really trying to work out why my body wants them so much. I know they contain iodine, omega 3 etc, I'm just curious to know exactly why my body keeps telling me to keep drinking them. I don't feel this way about any other foods or supplements etc.

I could drink sprat smoothies everyday, I'm just a little concerned it might be too much of a good thing. I bet there's a lot of calcium in them because of the bones.

### sooperb — 2014-01-22T21:41:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-106641>

Sprat smoothies, I'm not sure they'll catch on in a juice bar

I've never, ever wanted to eat kale, fit only for cattle imo. Yet, I decided that because of it's amazing properties I should try and try it, hold nose, tiny spoonful, prepare to vomit. I couldn't have been more taken aback, not only did I not get toxic shock, I liked it, nay, loved it to the point I was eating it raw. Tonight I even added it to homemade chilli in place of the beans. Mmmmmmm. I've plenty left. Hey, maybe I could blend it with some sprats?

It's the perfect location as it's right out in the countryside yet within an hour of the city centre. It's a little closer to other houses than I would like however it's also next to big fields. Also it's a safe distance from power lines, mast etc.

### HoneyChild — 2014-01-29T19:14:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-107326>

I really don't like talking about bad stuff but a few weeks ago I had some inflamed pre-menstrual zits and then had menstrual cramps. I thought both of these problems were long gone so was disappointed that they'd reappeared.

I believe this was caused by the stress of Christmas with my family as it caused my sleep to become really disrupted. I'll not go into the details but my Aunt drinks way too much. She had a bottle of vodka in addition to a lot of wine and cocktails. If I even drank half that much I'd end up in hospital getting my stomach pumped!

I made some comment she didn't like and she totally flew off the handle. She slammed doors, sulked, gave us the silent treatment. All this from someone who's supposed to be in her 50s! It's really upset my Mum too and her heath has suffered (and her health isn't great in the first place.)

Now my Aunt is claiming I told her she's vindictive and manipulating. I never said anything remotely like that. TBH, I think she can't remember what happened so is just making stuff up. She's also saying that my other Aunt told her she's Hitler (I'm not sure if she really did say that or not). My Mum really just wants to cut her off but is concerned that it will upset my Gran.

Anyway, I've written more than I intended. I just thought I'd bring up the point that stress/poor sleep can end up affecting your health despite how good your diet might be. Luckily my sleep is much improved so hopefully my body is back to normal now.

### caroline — 2014-01-29T23:13:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-107351>

I don't know how to deal with that either .... people close to us can hurt us so much. I believe we shouldn't let someone so negative and hurtful have any influence in our life - but that is much easier said than done in certain situations.

How do you deal with someone close to you who has a trashed brain .....

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-29T23:32:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-107355>

How do you deal with them? You don't........That is how I deal with my own family disruptors. If you are not good enough for yourself who can you help? People forget this basic premise. Take of yourself before anyone else. This is why on a plane you put your mask on before anyone else's.......Just because some one is family gives them no free pass in my life. Evaluate the people in your life and then demote, or promote or terminate. Ultimately you are the CEO of your life. I tell my family and kids this all the time: You want to come into my life, the door is always open. You want to get out of my life, the door is also open.

Just one request- don't stand at the door ...........you're blocking the traffic.

### sooperb — 2014-02-05T18:32:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-108492>

"I believe we shouldn't let someone so negative and hurtful have any influence in our life"
I second that, which is why I decided to let my relationship with my sister dissolve. Under the guise of humour she has made many hurtful and unkind remarks to the point that other people were noticing and commenting. She then posted such a comment on the FB of a mutual friend. At that point I decided enough was enough. I didn't respond to a couple of her emails, all about her and her new grandchild, and have had no other contact apart from I send a christmas card if she sends me one and similarly a birthday card.

I don't miss her, I don't feel bad, nor has she tried to contact me to find out why. I feel a little sad about it on one level, as you age your start to lose friends and family so generally value those who are left all the more but I don't want her in my life if she can't switch off the unpleasantness. I don't even know why she does it although I know I'm not the only one she is unkind about.

Octopus, hmmm, I don't think I would. Any squid I've ever had has been like chewing strips of bicycle tyre!

### sooperb — 2014-02-14T17:42:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-110203>

"With my family it all seemed to kick off over the handing out of the Xmas pressies!"

I'm sorry to say that I think that is awful, how can they value "things" over people? How can people snub relatives, by choosing to make it obvious by the size of their gift? As for Christmas generally, that's another occasion I wish didn't happen. It causes monumental grief, fall out and financial stress every year, is it really worth celebrating? Or at least couldn't we celebrate by sharing good food, bonhomie, a pretty tree and love? I don't care about getting presents, in fact at my age I'd rather not have them, but I love sharing time with my family even if they don't love sharing that special time with me (that's the sister again lol).

### HoneyChild — 2014-02-14T18:33:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-110229>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=110203)
>
> "With my family it all seemed to kick off over the handing out of the Xmas pressies!"
>
> I'm sorry to say that I think that is awful, how can they value "things" over people? How can people snub relatives, by choosing to make it obvious by the size of their gift? As for Christmas generally, that's another occasion I wish didn't happen. It causes monumental grief, fall out and financial stress every year, is it really worth celebrating? Or at least couldn't we celebrate by sharing good food, bonhomie, a pretty tree and love? I don't care about getting presents, in fact at my age I'd rather not have them, but I love sharing time with my family even if they don't love sharing that special time with me (that's the sister again lol).

Well, I've not really gone into details here. Basically for years I've felt my Aunt takes over Christmas. We always have to do what she wants, when she wants, listen to the music she wants, where we should all sit etc. She's always so vocal about what she likes and what she doesn't like. I've always felt the family gets treated like a sheep being herded into a pen, everyone goes along with it for an easy life.

One year my BF and I played some guitar and whilst we were playing she kept shouting out the she wanted us to play "sexy Santa songs" (whatever the hell they're supposed to be!) and out of the corner of my eye I could see her attempt to pole dance without a pole. Well, my BF felt very insulted that she behaved like this - was it really that much to ask for her to sit down and shut the eff up for 20 mins??

Anyway, at Christmas it was getting kinda late and felt like the day was never going to come to an end. Then she decided we should all sit in a circle in order to give the presents out. Sorry, but that was the last straw for me. So I snapped "oh hurry up, just hand out the presents."

She aggressively retorted "that's it, I'm done with you, you never do anything for this family!"

I thought this was a very odd response so I shouted back that I'm just fed up with all her rules and we always have to do what she wants.

So that's a longer explanation of what happened. That's when she basically started her tantrum of slamming doors and giving us all the silent treatment.

Afterwards she complained to my Mum about how I spoke to her and my Mum just did her best to make light of it "oh it's allright, I think you're making too big a deal of it." Well, my Aunt wasn't happy with that and she that this would affect their relationship. My Mum said it wouldn't and tried to calm her down.

My Mum has had a rough couple of years so she said "after the year I've had I just wanted a nice quite Christmas" to which my Aunt replied "after the year you've had?? What about me, I've had a hip replacement!"

The thing is, whenever my Mum talks to my Aunt about all the problems she's going through my Aunt turns around and says "I'm a positive person, I only like to talk about nice things, we've talked about that enough, I think we'll change the subject."

So of course my Aunt has no clue about what my Mum's going through - she always changes the subject!

There are lots of "home truths" my Mum wants to give her but feels she isn't mature enough to handle it so she bites her tongue instead. My BF is convinced she has the mental age of 12.

My Aunt has no hold over me so she's been taking it out on my Mum instead. The other week she said "we'll need to catch up but I'm not ready yet." My Mum though what the hell is that supposed to mean?? My Aunt's really acting like she's the injured party in all of this, she's the biggest drama queen I've ever known.

### sooperb — 2014-02-14T18:46:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-110236>

Your aunt has self esteem issues I think which manifest in her always needing to be in control. If she isn't then she has to divert attention back to her by raising a scene. Maybe there are issues from years ago, maybe she is just very insecure but rather than sit in a corner hiding, she puts on a brave front (her positivism) and hides behind the smokescreen she has created where she feels safe. She isn't going to want to hear about anyone else's problems, that would turn the focus away from her. It's a bit like she has to be the centre of attention to convince herself she's still alive, rather like the old adage "I had to kept pinching myself to make sure I wasn't dreaming".

I suspect that if your mum was to avoid her and keep her at arm's length she would up the ante by accusing your mum of something, make it very personal and then the act would be that she might as well be dead because no one loves her and she doesn't know what's she's done to deserve such a selfish family. It's ironic that her behaviour is creating that exact scenario and, like a runaway bus, she can't stop it. I feel sorry for people like that but at the same time they are very difficult to like. Is there anyone prepared to talk to her and maybe point out some home truths but without making her feel worse about herself? Otherwise she will create the situation that she is desperate to avoid, being alone.

Oh, I know you're right. I really don't think she's happy with her life and is very insecure. For instance, she keeps coming out with stuff like "for every person who doesn't like me there are 9 who do." (She actually said this not long before the bust-up.)

Are you a counsellor or something?

\--
I'm doing psychology as as a mandatory module as part of my health science degree. I was at the intro tutorial on Saturday and we all talked a bit about ourselves and why we were doing the module. There was a guy there who said he's particularly interested in learning more about children, especially autism. He started working with children at a place his mum works. At first it was just a job to him but now he's more interested learning more. He said he's had problems with OCD and just missed out on a diagnosis for Asperger's however his brother was diagnosed with it.

I noticed he had a big can of one of those energy drinks sitting on the desk in front of him. Sigh. They're really not good, I wonder what else is wrong with his diet/lifestyle.

\--
In case anyone is wondering what's happening about that house I was talking about before, I don't think we're going to view it any time soon. Apparently they can only show people around on Sundays but whenever we tried to book a viewing we'd be told that it wasn't suitable. Very odd. The house is a bit close to other houses for our liking so we'll keep looking. This is traditionally the time of year when properties come onto the market so hopefully something will come up eventually.

### sooperb — 2014-02-19T15:27:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-111170>

I can do you a house by the seaside in Cumbria, all newly renovated  It's a terrace though so won't meet your requirements of being distant from neighbours, the neighbours are silent and it's in a lovely spot, 2 mins literally and you're on the beach, beautiful sunsets, wonderful walking and yet only a 15 minute ride into the nearest town. 3 beds, through lounge, bathroom downstairs (I'll bet I'm not winning here lol).

""for every person who doesn't like me there are 9 who do." QED lol. No, I'm not a counsellor but I'm told I have very good insight so maybe I should be. Bit too old to start I guess.

Some of these kids have been abused. She's one very inspiring lady!

### HoneyChild — 2014-03-22T19:47:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-117209>

There's not been much coming onto the property market so far this year so we eventually decided to have a look at this house today and liked it a lot more than we expected. I forgot to take my Trimeter so I'm not sure what the reading will be although I didn't see anything of major concern. There's a street light right outside which my BF isn't really keen on, says we should take the meter out with on for the second viewing. The garden seemed a bit damp and probably needs some attention, maybe create a pond which is something I've always wanted!

> [HoneyChild said:](/goto/post?id=106744)
>
> We've been searching for a house in the country for quite a few years now but ultimately have been waiting for finances to be in place first. Now we're seriously looking and a house I was interested in a few years ago has come onto the market again. It's on the very South West edge of a little village I really had my heart set on. It's the perfect location as it's right out in the countryside yet within an hour of the city centre. It's a little closer to other houses than I would like however it's also next to big fields. Also it's a safe distance from power lines, mast etc.

### HoneyChild — 2014-05-06T12:07:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-125445>

Looks like things might be moving on the house-hunting front. The house we're had our eyes on for a while (see above) was basically put to one side as we felt it wasn't 100% what we were looking for, ideally we wanted something that was completely on its own. Spring is traditionally the time of year when properties start coming on the market so we decided to wait for something better but to be honest none of the properties have been any good, at first they look ideal but then we find out it's in a really damp location or right next to a major road with Kawasaki motorbikes screaming past at 90mph.

So we decided to have another look at this house and we're putting in an offer. My BF should be doing this today but I'm at my parenents at the moment so it's not great timing. Then we'll need to get our own place on the market but luckily properties like ours are in very high demand so should sell quickly.

### HoneyChild — 2014-05-06T12:13:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-125448>

Small victory yesterday, went to the supermarket and bought 6 oysters for 60p each plus I got 3 lamb hearts which were reduced in price. I sliced two of them up yesterday and placed them on a wooden board in the fridge and I had a third of the other one with some scrambled egg/crab meat for my breakfast this morning. I'm a little nervous of eating oysters again because of the seemingly random vomiting I had last year but I feel brave enough to try them again. All I need to do is find a suitable knife to open them otherwise I'll need to steam them. Wish me luck, lol.

### sooperb — 2014-05-06T18:17:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-125510>

Moving house is always so exciting, if a lot of work. If you worry about eating raw oysters, why would you be wanting to eat meat that's high?

### HoneyChild — 2014-05-06T18:51:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-125515>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=125510)
>
> Moving house is always so exciting, if a lot of work. If you worry about eating raw oysters, why would you be wanting to eat meat that's high?

My vomiting last year seemed pretty random, so I don't know for sure that it was the oysters. I know that a lot of them apparently contain norovirus but I'm genetically resistant to norovirus so who knows. I don't seem to have any problems with raw meat so maybe things are improving in that department. Just need to keep pushing forward to see what my body is capable of dealing with.

### caroline — 2014-05-07T01:41:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-125558>

I have thrown up a few times from oysters ........ no idea why ....but I continue - and I love raw meat!

### HoneyChild — 2014-05-07T11:31:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-125613>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=125558)
>
> I have thrown up a few times from oysters ........ no idea why ....but I continue - and I love raw meat!

I just keep telling myself "what doesn't kill you will make you stronger" although it's a difficult instinct to overcome as there are obviously evolutionary reasons behind it. There was a wildlife documentary where they're training animals not to eat poisonous toads with a lot of success...

> Poisonous sausages containing toad meat are being trialled in the Kimberley (Western Australia) to try to protect native animals from cane toads' deadly impact. The Western Australian Department of Environment and Conservation has been working with the University of Sydney to develop baits to train native animals not to eat the toads. By blending bits of toad with a nausea-inducing chemical, the baits train the animals to stay away from the amphibians. Researcher David Pearson says trials run in laboratories and in remote parts of the Kimberley region of WA are looking promising, although the baits will not solve the cane toad problem altogether.

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cane\_toad](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cane_toad)

### HoneyChild — 2014-05-12T21:00:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-126502>

I put my liver (gluconeogenesis) to the test on Saturday and completely failed. I wasn't able to eat any lunch due to circumstances and felt symptoms of low blood glucose/high cortisol.  I would be terrible at IFing. Coincidently, I also had a flare up of an old tick bite on my leg which isn't something I've noticed in many months, don't know if it's related.

Also been really busy the last few days with getting our property ready for being sold, nearly there just have to wait for an electrician to upgrade the circuit breaker. Then we can get a surveyor up and the property officially on the market. An estate agent was up today and said properties are selling in 4-6 weeks just now.

sounds like everything is moving along nicely ........

### HoneyChild — 2014-06-04T22:04:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-129676>

Very quick update, we've just accepted an offer on our property which is pretty good going considering it only went on the market 6 days earlier! I'm so glad that's dealt with - I've never done so much vacuuming and dusting in my life!

Now I just have my health sciences exam tomorrow morning which is the first exam I'll have done since school. Can't say I'm looking forward to it but I feel quietly confident.

### caroline — 2014-06-05T04:47:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-129733>

seems like everything is coming together nicely .....

quantum jazz!

### HoneyChild — 2014-06-06T14:09:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-129953>

Thanks guys, looked out the window yesterday morning to see that the "under offer" banner had been put up, what a nice sight!

The exam went well yesterday (I hope anyway). I was fully expecting there to more technical questions but they were actually pretty basic, kind felt they'd been dumbed down to be honest.

There was one question for Paleo folks though  It was about how people's health declined since the advent of agriculture and we were to give 2 possible reasons. Well, I didn't think talking about "quantum coherence" would go down well, so I had to wrack my brains for something a bit more "conventional." I couldn't remember what was discussed in the text book but I remembered watching a documentary where they talked about how the women had to grind grain all day to make enough flour for a loaf of bread. This was back-breaking work and affected their spines, so I wrote about that. I also wrote about living in close proximity to animals would cause the spread of pathogenic bacteria and parasites to humans. When I got home I had a quick look in my text book and only saw mention of carbs providing food for plaque-causing bacteria in the mouth. Damn, I've should've got that!

I now just need to finish assessments for psychology and microbiology which are due in on the 10th and 11th. It's a lovely day to day and would like to do some sunbathing but my computer is in the room furthest away from the sun so I'll think I'll do some note-taking the old fashioned way with pen and paper

### caroline — 2014-06-06T14:28:38+0300

can you sunbathe topless?

I'm studying for a degree in health sciences but the modules I'm doing cover a wide variety of topics including microbiology and psychology. It's a distance learning degree and very general but I'm looking to using it as a stepping stone to something more specific such as biomedical sciences which I think will ultimately mean analysing people's blood for parasites etc.

That reminds me, I have a funny story to tell you but I'll go over to the 'girls in dresses' thread...

### HoneyChild — 2014-06-10T21:23:51+0300

I bought some cooked prawns with shells still on today and unfortunately I really don't like their "fishy" taste. I've soaked them in the some of my home made fermented lemon juice to try and mask the taste which has helped a little. Been eating them a few at a time over the last few hours and now I'm totally stuffed. Was going to have lamb chops tonight but now can't even look at them so just had a salad instead.

### HoneyChild — 2014-06-30T21:50:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-132754>

Been away for a week on a small island, west coast of Scotland and had the most amazing weather, got quite sunburnt on areas I forgot to apply SPF30 so I guess I need to up the DHA. At least I got to spend a fair bit of time paddling in the water, there are a few beaches and nobody really bothers with them. Oh, and there are some basalt rock pools too, if you're lucky some passing shrimp might give you a pedicure, lol. People who go to the island are more into walking and watching out for wildlife so we had the beaches all to ourselves  . There's no mobile phone reception at all but modern technology is creeping in such as free wifi at the restaurant. There's satellite broadband in the cottage but I switched it off at the plug.

### nonchalant — 2014-07-01T02:27:06+0300

### HoneyChild — 2014-07-01T20:43:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-132906>

Got my order of frozen seafood including oysters today. It says on the package that the oysters were produced May last year, so they're getting kinda old, I hope they're better than not eating any oysters at all. I've got a few in the fridge defrosting for tomorrow.

### HoneyChild — 2014-07-04T15:51:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-133155>

On Wed I had 3 of my defrosted oysters, next day I had 4 and today I had 5. It's still a struggle to overcome the gagging reflex but I think I'm getting better at it. I've also been cutting up frozen whitebait into smaller pieces and swallowing them whole with some water.

Had the first wild raspberries of the year, there were only a few ready for eating but they were delicious - certainly compared to oysters and whitebait!

### Inger — 2014-07-08T08:47:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-133483>

> [HoneyChild said:](/goto/post?id=132882)
>
> How long ago did your ancestors come from here? On the island there's evidence of Viking settlements from hundreds of years ago. There are stones laid out in the shape of boats and they are said to be Viking graves that point home to Norway.
>
> Haha! Yeah at the time I thought "if Inger was here she'd be barefoot" but what to do about all the sheep shit?  I didn't want to carry those big boots around with me and some of the tracks are really stony and my feet just wouldn't be able to cope with them. I guess I'm still too much of a "towny" I will try to do better next time

I remember it being my fathers grandpa....... The Vikings were some cool folks...lol

HoneyChild, I walk in sheep and chicken shit every single day!!! It is so not dangerous! And I do not use shoes at all, only when I go shopping or "in civilisation"
I am kind of worried about having to use shoes much anymore - I do not like it at all. I am addicted to the ground, to touch it. Seriously! I crave it
Does it hurt too? Sure! But some pain is good in life, I have very little pain anyways lol So when I step n a stone i just have to laugh a bit... and I do get wounds too, but I do not care! They heal. I do not even put anything on them. They might hurt a bit for a while but it is no big deal. If it annoys me I take some wild herb leaves and tie around the wound and they heal into being painless in one night.

I have gotten serious addictions.... but I let me... because I know those addictions do not hurt me! I am addicted to wild foods too... i get euphoric when picking and eating berries and wild greens.... ouch! Nice drugs
and when my bare feet touch the ground...... and my bare skin touch the hard rocks... sometimes there are hard moos on them that scratches me.. and some ants that walk on me and bite at times... but it all is part of the beauty!

> One way of thinking of the cloud is as the physical expression of the electron acting as a wave; an alternative interpretation is that the cloud shows the probability of finding one or more electrons at a given point, with the electron(s) spending most time where the cloud has the highest density as shown by darker shading. ### HoneyChild — 2014-10-10T18:52:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-143422>

Well, I have both good and bad news. The good news is that we moved to our new house 3 weeks ago and have been making the most of the good weather whilst it lasted plus getting some bits and pieces for the garden. Unfortunately a few days after moving in my Gran got diagnosed with oral cancer. It's spread into her jaw bone so is having surgery for it on Monday. She'd been having problems with her dentures hurting her gums so kept going to the dentist but was told there wasn't anything that could be done. So my Mum suggested she go to her own private biological dentist who promptly referred her to an oral surgeon. By this time she already had the cancer for a few months. If she doesn't have surgery she might only have 6 months to live. I'm really not sure why she's developed oral cancer because she doesn't smoke, rarely drinks and never uses wireless technology. My guess is that her dentures caused irritation/inflammation which eventually resulted in cancer. I really think her dentist should be sued for negligence because if she'd been referred in the early stages it might not have spread to the bone. Now she's having to have major surgery involving taking bone from her arm to reconstruct her jaw.

### HoneyChild — 2014-10-25T14:53:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-145068>

Just got the results back from my psychology module and I got a B which I'm quite happy about considering I didn't even want to study it in the first place! (It's a mandatory part of my health sciences degree.)

### HoneyChild — 2014-10-25T17:56:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-145103>

I'm really not doing well with the change in seasons. My raynaud's-type swelling in my toes has been back for a few weeks, on and off. However for the last few days they've been really hot, swollen and painful. I've been having to put them in a bucket of cold water (with iceblock) for about half an hour at a time and that seems to help somewhat. The pain has been making it very difficult to get to sleep and I have to walk about the house with my toes curled upwards.

We were at the local GP seeing a nurse in order to register with the practice on Thursday and I spoke a little to her about my swollen toes. She said I would need to make an appointment with the GP about that but commented on my pale cold hands and said they're like her friend's who has Raynaud's. I never really considered my hands to be a problem, I know they're pale and cold to touch but they don't _feel_ cold to me. We got our blood pressure taken and mine is 114/70 which is considered normal but I'm concerned it might actually be a bit on the low side. Years ago it was tested and was 110/70 so my BP hasn't really changed much over the years.

I'm currently looking through Amazon with the intention of buying a UV and IR lamp. We also have a coal/wood-burning stove so have asked my BF to fire it up tonight some I can get the IR. I've also placed an order to restock the freezer with seafood so that should arrive on Wednesday.

### Linz — 2014-10-26T17:22:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-145216>

Honeychild,
I don't have any answers but I have had hot swollen toes as the seasons changed in spring and autumn for several years but they are completely normal this year (so far). My Reynauds fingers disappeared a few years ago and I think that was improved fat consumption. Did you see Gretchen said her toes were also painful again at on another thread.? Do yours improve when you walk outside in the wet grass? Mine always flared up immediately when I had to go shopping in town and I couldn't wait to get home and take my shoes off. I'm sure we are all going to kick ourselves when we stumble on the reason but it is frustrating ( and painful) not knowing.

The only changes I can think of that I have made are that we eat a lot more local and home grown food along with the fish and make a real effort to get out in the morning sun. Although my toes have improved I have been struggling with a flare up of gut symptoms which I haven't seen for several years and which started a good month before the season change (usually autumn is the highest risk time). My BP is usually low too and has been for years.

Love your pigs

### HoneyChild — 2014-10-28T14:28:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-145411>

Linz, yes I saw Gretchen's post where she's having problems again with her toes. It certainly sounds like a vit A/D imbalance. I really don't know what's set it off or what helps it. Aspirin does absolutely nothing. I went to the GP yesterday and she said my Raynaud's is the worst she's ever seen and ordered a lot of blood tests which I had to get drawn today by the nurse. It was the same nurse I saw on Thursday and she was shocked to see me limping so much! I've to phone in a couple of days to see if my results are back.

Still doing research into UV lamps, they're not exactly cheap! I found a tanning salon near my where my parents live which has a bed with red and UV light combined. Apparently the frequencies that NASA have been studying. Although at the moment I'm not really fit to go anywhere let alone a tanning salon!

Re: my gran, her surgery was postponed because of issues with her heart (hardening of the arteries) but it went ahead yesterday and she's doing very well so far. They think she'll be able to transfer to the more normal ward sooner than expected.

### HoneyChild — 2014-10-31T15:42:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-145786>

Well, my feet are considerably better now, almost 100%. I was very pessimistic for a while, convinced I'd be crippled for life. My left food looked like it had been stung by a hive of bees! My BF was concerned that my problems stemmed from sitting in the cold with just lightweight clothing on and he thought I should wrap up warm. Cold adaptation may be good for most people but not for me. I couldn't come up with a logical counter-argument so did what he suggested so maybe he was right. I'm now wearing pink fleece socks to keep my feet warm plus a jumper to keep my core warm.

I can't really work out what happened to me but I have a couple of ideas.

1) The drop in temperature made my body try to change the fatty acids in my cell membranes but maybe I don't yet have enough omega 3 so ended up making pro-inflammatory prostaglandins.

2) Insulin isn't working at low temperatures and I'm still not a fat burner despite eating high fat low carb for a couple of years.

### HoneyChild — 2014-11-05T21:02:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/honeychilds-musings.8878/post-146240>

I've got the test results back from when my left food was really swollen and could barely walk. I had low neutrophils/WBCs and high cholesterol but my CRP was technically normal at 5 (of course we all know what normal really means!!).

Compared to the [blood test I did last year](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/labs-in-is-it-my-field-or-what.8790/), my LDL cholesterol and triglycerides have improved enormously. We don't actually do BUN/creatinine ratios here but I think mine has gone from 14 down to 11.69.

I have to go back next week to get my full blood count and lipids tested again.

>
> They are still operating. I just ordered mine today.

They still run ancestry stuff, not health stuff...hopefully one day they can again.....

Welcome to 23andMe.

At this time, we have suspended our health-related genetic tests to comply with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration’s directive to discontinue new consumer access during our regulatory review process.

We are continuing to provide you with both ancestry-related genetic tests and raw genetic data, without 23andMe’s interpretation.

If you are an existing customer please click the button below and then go to the health page for additional information, including information about refunds.

We remain firmly committed to fulfilling our long-term mission to help people everywhere have access to their own genetic data and have the ability to use that information to improve their lives.

Upon entering the site, please confirm you understand the new changes in our services.

### Gracelind — 2013-12-13T02:31:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fda-orders-23andme-to-stop-selling-dna-tests.8880/post-103274>

For now 23 and Me raw data files can still be run through Genetic Genie, Sterling Hill's App, and Promethease for interpretation of genes affecting methylation, detox, and other issues. To complicate matters, 23 and Me has changed over to the V4 chip which has a smaller number of some different "hand-picked" SNPs that are analyzed. There is concern that this chip may be incompatible with some of the other ancestral DNA databases such as GEDMatch.com and FTDNA. Not sure whether this will affect how they run through the health databases.

They say cut-off date for providing the health results at 23 and Me was purchase by Nov 22. However, there are lots of complaints on the Community Forum of specimen kits "sitting in the mailbags" and not yet moving to the lab for processing.

While I do genealogy and appreciate that data, the reason I ordered the kit from 23 and Me was the opportunity to easily get results for APOE.

As the intrusions by govt agencies into our daily lives in ways that majorly reduce quality of life add up, I hope that people are taking note of where this is coming from.

### nicld — 2013-12-13T04:08:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fda-orders-23andme-to-stop-selling-dna-tests.8880/post-103280>

Grrrr, just logged in to see if they got my sample and it said that I needed to register my kit, WTFudge, I already did that. I KNOW that I did. So I have an email into them to see what happened. NOT going to be happy if they say that they never got it and I ordered it just before the FDA crack down.

### Gracelind — 2013-12-13T05:32:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fda-orders-23andme-to-stop-selling-dna-tests.8880/post-103288>

My BIL's kit was processed mid-Nov. When he logs on he sometimes still gets the register-your-kit message. He goes to "change profile" on the top right, clicks, and all the data appears. For some reason he appears to have two profiles.

You may have a glitch--try the profile click.

### spalmi — 2013-11-25T19:04:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light.8881/post-101613>

The idea of no longer using blue light is overwhelming. Here in Massachusetts it is Dark at 4:30, which is also the time my children get home from school. They go to bed at 9:00. Without blue light what do you do from 4:30-9:00 without light? How do you do homework? Make and eat dinner? Play? Another way to ask this is how do you know the differences in potency? What is worse being in a well lit room? Or waching TV in a dark room? Is all light blue light?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-25T19:48:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light.8881/post-101616>

It maybe overwhelming but it is why you get ill. Try using red amber bulbs at night or go back to lanterns or candles. That is what I do. I sue old wine bottles filled with lantern fuel for light at night or my fire. I also use red bulbs to read.

### nicld — 2013-11-26T03:50:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light.8881/post-101653>

Blue blocker glasses will really help.

### Dan234561234 — 2013-11-27T17:06:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light.8881/post-101732>

F.lux for computer display auto change to less blue light per sun position.

### spalmi — 2013-11-28T22:43:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light.8881/post-101834>

Red Light Bulbs. I know that LED lights appear white, even though they are have a large amount of blue waves. Can you get white bulbs that give off red light instead of blue, that still appears white like LED?

### spalmi — 2013-11-29T14:37:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light.8881/post-101855>

### kjgraffin@yahoo.com — 2013-11-26T23:44:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advanced-oxidant-stress-profile.8894/post-101692>

My doctor ordered an oxidative stress test to see what my glutathione levels look like. We are working on so many things (Lyme, leaky gut, hypothyroidism), she thought it would be helpful. I'm wondering if anyone on here is familiar with it and what you might think. She seemed to think things looked great. What I would really like to know, is why this is good information to have and what exactly is it telling me (if anything).

Antioxidant (Intracellular)
Percent Reduced Glutathione 95% (93.3-100.0%)
total Glutathione 821.1 (658.3-988.5)

Lipid Peroxidation
F2-isoprostane .1 (.4-6.9)

DNA Damage
8-OHdG .4 (1.0-17.4)

Enzyme Assays
Glutathione Peroxidase 25.6 (18.1-191.2)
SOD I 26.9 (4.9-23.0)
SOD II 25.7 (9.4-47.0)

### kjgraffin@yahoo.com — 2013-12-04T19:03:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advanced-oxidant-stress-profile.8894/post-102295>

I hope everyone has a truly "thankful" Thanksgiving .... surrounded by their loved ones. Have a huge pile of fun too!!

I went to a concert last night at the Opera House .....Mandy Patinkin and Nathan Gunn. What amazing, amazing voices they both have ..... and Mandy is a hoot ... a very, very funny guy. I had just seen Homeland the night before.... he is quite the allrounder!

It was Nathan's birthday yesterday so Mandy surprised him on stage with a birthday pumpkin pie \[with candles\] and a bag piper who played happy birthday to him ...and we all sang.
Mandy did check under the kilt ...

Anyway ..... Mandy decided that Australians should have thanksgiving too ....we all have a lot to be thankful for - so he declared Thursday is Thanskgiving here too.....nice

He loved that we have a National "Sorry day"

Since we live far far away from the rest of our family (1000+ miles) our Thanksgiving will be spent with a "surrogate" family. We were invited out to a friend's place. About 40 people attending and it's a fun afternoon/evening. They live out in the countryside and have a vineyard on the property. Bonfire at dusk...

I've been asked to bring my famous (_infamous_?) carrot cake. I swear that the completed 3-layer cake weighs in at about 9.5 lbs. I only bake it twice per year... DH's birthday and Thanksgiving. The rule is that it can't stay in the house after I bake it. My husband has to take his cake into work and share it

Have a Happy Thanksgiving everyone!

# Thread: Dehydration.....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dehydration.8912/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2013-11-28T00:52:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dehydration.8912/post-101780>

I feel like some us are struggling with dehydration .... I know I am. How do we know when we are hydrated properly?

Maybe there is something I am not understanding properly....

I think we all now know that non native EMf is huge ... and the photoelectric effect... and grounding and fake light....and water.

Dr. Kruse has talked a bit lately about our life being a see saw - that makes perfect sense. We need balance. I think that he has a few more bomb shells for us - probably quite a few!

So - if we are out of balance in one area ...... we have to be an over achiever in another.... I get that.

What about our redox potential? that is coming .....is that the piece we are missing? - well - one of them?

Just because we drink a ton of spring water - doesn't seem to mean that we are properly hydrated and we are able to unfold our proteins etc. There is so much more going on at the quantum cell level.

I have a feeling that even if I think I am doing everything right - to the best of my ability - I am still missing a piece or two \[or more\] of the puzzle....

Soooooo - How do you know that you are properly hydrated????

I am thinking that when it gets right down to it - this is one of the most important things for us because if we are properly hydrated that tells us that everything else is in balance?

What am I missing? ..... and I do know that the answers to that question could be endless!

### cinnamon — 2013-11-28T03:37:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dehydration.8912/post-101788>

Hi Caroline,

In the comments to EE6 - Quantum Cell Theory, [I asked](http://www.jackkruse.com/ee-6-quantum-cell-theory-life-collective-phenomena/#IDComment731584698):

"If a person is dehydrated because non-native emf has interfered with the ability to lay down additional layers of structured water to build superconducting cables, does it actually help to drink more water? Or will the water just pass straight through and not improve the state of dehydration until the person reduces the emf exposure?"​

Jack's reply:

"Cinnamon if you have inflammation........yes if it is cold......if your inflamed you must lower the CRP first......and that is a job for CT. But I also use cold water prior to CT."​

I really want to build the superconducting cables described in EE6!

After Jack's response, I started drinking greater quantities of ice cold water surrounding CT. As you may remember, I'm mostly a shower person (though I know that most prefer the tub). I started having about 750 ml of ice cold (literally has ice crystals floating in it - like a slushie) water just before starting to turn the shower water colder and then drinking another 250 ml immediately after I'm done. I was previously just drinking 500 ml before.

Is it helping? I'm not sure. But I'm hopeful. I have a window in my shower and it feels pretty amazing to stand there, cold, pink and naked with the window open with the outside temps below freezing while I drink my final ice water slushie!  I could not have imagined myself doing this a couple of years ago. LOL

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-28T06:01:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dehydration.8912/post-101791>

well I went in the gulf tonight......temperture out side 39 degrees, water 54 degrees........22 mph winds. Getting out of the water was pretty bad. Getting in was easy.

### caroline — 2013-11-28T06:19:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dehydration.8912/post-101795>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=101791)
>
> well I went in the gulf tonight......temperture out side 39 degrees, water 54 degrees........22 mph winds. Getting out of the water was pretty bad. Getting in was easy.

Is that normal temps for this time of the year? I thought it would be quite a bit warmer.

So - actually you have the best of both worlds .... Very hot weather in the summer and you can drive for C/T now and eat a boatload of seafood....

How do you warm up after C/T?

### caroline — 2013-11-28T06:32:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dehydration.8912/post-101797>

Cinnamon .... You are a rock star girl! Showering is so much harder.... That is a gorgeous visual ...you standing pink and naked and looking out your window.

Cool Cinnamon! I love your CT rituals! I am going to up my amount of ice water before dipping in the river too (have had just 0,5 liter until now), and drink some right after too! Ice water is so energizing....

### georgeMdays — 2013-11-30T18:53:28+0200

My health is not good, and I'm only 33.

I keep an eye on the things I actually eat and make sure to excercise three sessions every week. I normally do strength training, but throw in some cardio also. I drink just on rare occasions, do not do drugs.

But i am still struggling with these issues:

Acute fatigue, continually worn-out. I hardly ever have good enough stamina. Even though I rest LOTS.

My head doesn't often function correctly, just like I'm within a daze. I've got a difficult time focusing on the work I need to do. My ability to chat has suffered (can not find the suitable words).

What basically disturbs me is not getting the enthusiasm to complete everything I aim to. I Actually have felt classical major depression as long as I can think back.

I've found it hard to talk to many people most of the time. It is absolutely awful not to have the ability to feel comfy in friendly occasions.

Sex seriously isn't a idea in my mind. When I see striking women my desire for them is just not here. I've additionally had performance anxiety, really shameful. It just seems like a continual fog.

What should I try out?

When I got my T levels checked, they were really low for my age (only 365 ng/dl), but my doctor will not likely give me test.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-too-easy-to-give-up-but-i-refuse.8930/post-101910>

Hi there, just uncovered this forum. All this time I've spent searching yet still have some questions about T levels. I'll try to get started with some background.

My state of being is not right, and I'm only 33.

I keep an eye on everything I actually eat and make sure to workout 4 times a week. I make sure to do both dumbbells and cardio. I usually do not go out to party, nor have I tried illegal drugs.

But overall I'm still struggling with the following obstacles:

Ongoing weariness, consistently tired. I barely have quite enough stamina. No matter if I rest PLENTY.

I regularly have a good deal of "brain fog". I can't completely focus in work, or on goals. My ability to connect has suffered (can not find the correct words).

This is hard considering I realize I've got great capabilities, that the dedication is simply lacking. I usually am pretty depressed/blue.

It's infrequent that I feel comfortable in public, mostly just background anxiety. It's discouraging in a way I can't explain.

My sex drive is very low. There are pretty women all around me, still I really fail to care. I've additionally dealt with ED, really shameful. It feels so incredibly hopeless.

Exactly what can I attempt?

My testosterone levels are low (365 ng/dl says the lab test), but my physician refused to prescribe me testosterone.

Do you have labs? You can post them in the lab section and someone will help you with interpretations. Getting Testosterone from your Doc may not be the way to go just yet .... you need to research and learn more.

You should not be exercising at all right now - just get outside in the sunshine and get as much grounding as possible. Where are you located?

Drink only spring water

What is your non native EMF situation? at work? at home? You have to reduce this as much as possible as soon as possible.

There is a lot to take in here. Just jump in with Leptin reset and then keep reading and asking questions - we are here to help!

# Thread: Leg of Lamb
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leg-of-lamb.8931/>
**Subforum:** Autumn Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2013-12-01T04:02:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leg-of-lamb.8931/post-101926>

Leg of Lamb with a rosemary red wine cranberry sauce.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/lamb-jpg.184/)

I do not have exact amount but just toss stuff together.

Fresh whole cranberries
About a cup of red wine
Rosemary (I only had dried but fresh would be better)
2 poundish leg of lamb
beef broth

Heat up the cranberries in some water and the wine till the cranberries pop. Mash up a bit with a hand beater.
Pout the lamb in a roasting pan, fat side up.
Pour the cranberry mixture over the top and add some of the beef broth to the bottom of the pan.

Cook at 400 F for 30 minutes per pound or until internal temp is either 160-165F for a rarer of 170-175F for well done. Take from oven, remove from pan and cover with foil for about 20 minutes to let it rest.

Take the sauce and reduce to the thickness desired.

Cut and enjoy.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-12-01T05:37:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leg-of-lamb.8931/post-101928>

I had lamb tonight too.......

### Shijin13 — 2013-12-01T16:27:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leg-of-lamb.8931/post-101957>

We had grilled leg of lamb on Friday night. IT. WAS. AMAZING.

# Thread: Quantum Physics and Life
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-physics-and-life.8940/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### QuantumRadical — 2013-12-02T04:34:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-physics-and-life.8940/post-102015>

I just finished a really good book by Mae-Wan Ho called "The Rainbow and the Worm: The Physics of Organisms." And I just started "Living Rainbow H2O." Excellent research and documentation of research on the physics of living organisms. Mae-Wan Ho's works have been referenced in one or two of Dr. Kruse's Blogs. I think these two books are quite readable and may help to tighten up some lose ends with respect to the science. Lots of talk about water and its special properties with respect to life, quantum coherence, electron tunneling, and a whole lot more. Amazing stuff. Until biologists embrace the fact that life is all about quantum physics, they will never "get it." Just like JK says. To understand is to embrace paradox and contradiction, but it all makes sense if you fathom how remarkable life truly is.

Caroline, especially for you!!

Q'Rad Randy

### caroline — 2013-12-02T04:50:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-physics-and-life.8940/post-102018>

Thanks Randy! ...... I am crushing on you

I think what confuses me is..... "we need to embrace paradox and contradiction". I always thought that science was much more absolute than that.

Randy - Did you see on Jack's FB page, the other day, the huge discussion he was having with some guys about all this?

I think I need to get a leg up with redox potential .... what say you?

What a great Q&A ... so much to take in.

I am still a little confused about our photoelectric affect ......it is kinda rambling around in my head and not fusing together.

So - because our photons are so much stronger we have to mitigate the damage by doing C/T all year and low carb all year and a magnetico would really help. But wouldn't it help to stay out of direct sunlight then? or only spend limited amounts of time? like sit under a conifer at the beach?
Dr. K. said we use those other things as our sunblock.

What am I not capturing Randy????

BTW .... you once called me A/C ........I had forgotten that is what my DH called me all the time - I don't know how I could have forgotten that - that really shook me up for a minute or two...... thanx

### QuantumRadical — 2013-12-02T05:57:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-physics-and-life.8940/post-102020>

A/C--alternating current. )

Paradox and contradiction--nothing can travel faster than light, except quantum coupling--action at a distance, instantaneously. Goes against what our classical science says.

Redox potential--think of it as rechargeable batteries. At some point, they are hard or even impossible to recharge. Redox potential is an individual's potential to reduce oxidation.If the body gets sick--that is, in an abnormal emf environment--then it is harder for it to recharge so to speak (reducing oxidation is basically recharging your cellular batteries--I hate that analogy but it works).
Photoelectric affect--all electrons are equal, but they are either free or they are in an electron orbit around a nucleus. They get freed or captured into an orbit by the action of a photon(s)--that is, if the electron goes from a high energy level to a lower one, it emits a photon (reduces energy), and if an electron goes from a low energy orbit to a higher one, it must "absorb" a photon of precisely the correct energy (frequency) to make that happen. It is much more complex than this simple explanation, but that is the gist of it. All things in the universe, including us humans and our biochemistry, deal only with quantum fields that are manifested in energy or particles, so everything is about the photoelectric affect, isn't it? Our bodies are "body electric"--that is, the interaction of electrons, protons, and electromagnetic forces. That is all there is--again a bit oversimplified, but that is the gist of it again. Think of photons as units of energy--and when they are too energetic for their own circumstances, they act more like particles than waves (energy). Thus, the issue with the 'down under". The photons are stronger, so their interaction with anything -- take for example, water -- will have a different outcome than less energetic photons (northern hemisphere). I think Jack is saying that the affect on water is the main issue with the photons down south, so that the water in you is not "normal" for a healthy human. It makes proteins bend differently, sometimes benignly, but sometimes the abnormal bending leads to diseases--different from the ones in the northern hemisphere because the water is different by virtue of its interactions with higher (different) energy photons that are coming from the sun, but are not being screened out because of the lack of an ozone layer.

A lot is going on at the cellular level, but it does all boil down to quantum physics and how energy and matter are interrelated. The quantum coherence part is the really fascinating piece in my mind. Read Ho's books.

Q'Rad Randy

### Jack Kruse — 2013-12-02T06:09:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-physics-and-life.8940/post-102021>

Science is never absolute Caroline. Your understanding is your perception of it. Here is one of the fathers of the math of QED who did most of the heavy lifting on quantum vortices and how they work. Here is a quote from Feynman:

“I think it's much more interesting to live not knowing than to have answers which might be wrong. I have approximate answers and possible beliefs and different degrees of uncertainty about different things, but I am not absolutely sure of anything and there are many things I don't know anything about, such as whether it means anything to ask why we're here. I don't have to know an answer. I don't feel frightened not knowing things, by being lost in a mysterious universe without any purpose, which is the way it really is as far as I can tell.”
― Richard P. Feynman

From this statement you can see this Nobel Laureate knew nothing is for sure............and in the link below you will see he believes it is the missing ingredient in modern science.........

I'd strongly suggest you read about Feynman and his life........there was never a better man, and a scientist who cared deeply about the truth and about his species. He had a true heart of a teacher. He cared deeply about mankind because he knew we were and will continue to be our own worse enemy.
[http://bigthink.com/experts-corner/the-three-values-of-science](http://bigthink.com/experts-corner/the-three-values-of-science)

### QuantumRadical — 2013-12-02T06:17:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-physics-and-life.8940/post-102023>

A/C.
On science being more absolute. Actually, the more we discover, the more we discover that we really don't know very much. Every major breakthrough in science only reveals another universe of stuff that we can't explain. This all goes back to the fractal nature of the universe. Every layer of the onion we peel off merely reveals another layer, and then another, and then another, and so forth. Is quantum physics the end? I doubt it. It is exquisitely accurate experimentally, but yet it can't explain the moment of creation (Quantum Physics has no answer for the singularity), nor can it explain black holes (it predicted them, but it can't actually explain them, like what is on the other side; does it even have an "other side"?; --lots of conjecture, but another theory may come along that makes Quantum Physics look like a sloppy approximation--sort of like Newtonian Physics being a reasonable (but not exactly--so, sloppy) approximation of quantum physics at the level of our everyday existence. Some physicists think String Theory is the next layer of the onion, but it also may not be, but merely reveal something entirely new. We think we are smart, but that is mere hubris on humanities part. If nature is fractal, then there are probably self-aware entities on the scale of galaxies or even galaxy clusters. How would we know? Just because we can't imagine it, doesn't make it impossible. Even elementary particles may not be so elementary after all, but may have their own structure that we cannot even begin to investigate with our measurement techniques today.

Quantum Physics isn't the answer. It is, however, a statement of possibilities far beyond our imagination.

Q'Rad Randy

### caroline — 2013-12-02T06:44:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-physics-and-life.8940/post-102027>
